And again, I say unto you, let my servants Joseph Wakefield and Solomon Humphrey take their journey into the eastern lands;

Let them labor with their families, Mosiah 18:19 (18–20); D&C 5:10; 31:4; 42:12; 52:9.declaring none other things than the prophets and apostles, that which they have John 3:11 (11, 32).seen and heard and most assuredly TG Believe.believe, that the prophecies may be fulfilled.
In consequence of transgression, let that which was bestowed upon Heman Basset be Matt. 13:12 (10–13); 25:29 (25–30).taken from him, and placed upon the head of Simonds Ryder.
And again, verily I say unto you, let Jared Carter be D&C 79:1.ordained a priest, and also George James be ordained a TG Priest, Aaronic Priesthood.priest.

Let the residue of the elders Alma 6:1.watch over the churches, and declare the word in the regions round about them; and let them Neh. 4:6; 1 Cor. 4:12; D&C 75:3; 115:10.labor with their own hands that there be no TG Idolatry.idolatry nor wickedness practiced.

And remember in all things the Prov. 14:21; Isa. 3:15; D&C 104:18. TG Generosity; Poor.poor and the TG Compassion; Welfare.needy, the TG Sickness.sick and the afflicted, for he that doeth not these things, the same is not my disciple.

And again, let my servants Joseph Smith, Jun., and Sidney Rigdon and Edward Partridge take with them a Acts 14:26; 15:40; D&C 20:64, 84; 72:17 (17, 19); 112:21.recommend from the church. And let there be one obtained for my servant Oliver Cowdery also.

And thus, even as I have said, if ye are faithful ye shall assemble yourselves together to rejoice upon the land of D&C 28:9. TG Zion.Missouri, which is the land of your Num. 32:18; D&C 25:2; 57:2 (1–3); 58:51 (17, 28, 51).inheritance, which is now the land of your enemies.
But, behold, I, the Lord, will hasten the city in its time, and will crown the faithful with TG Joy.joy and with rejoicing.
Behold, I am Jesus Christ, the Son of God, and I will D&C 5:35.lift them up at the last day. Even so. Amen.

div.p: Revelation given through Joseph Smith the Prophet to Algernon Sidney Gilbert, at Kirtland, Ohio, June 8, 1831. At Sidney Gilbert’s request, the Prophet inquired of the Lord as to Brother Gilbert’s work and appointment in the Church.
1–3, Sidney Gilbert’s calling and election in the Church is to be ordained an elder; 4–7, He is also to serve as a bishop’s agent.

chapter.verse: Behold, I say unto you, my servant Sidney Gilbert, that I have heard your prayers; and you have called upon me that it should be made known unto you, of the Lord your God, concerning your Compare with sections 12; 14; 15; 16.calling and TG Election.election in the church, which I, the Lord, have raised up in these last days.
Behold, I, the Lord, who was TG Jesus Christ, Crucifixion of.crucified for the sins of the world, give unto you a commandment that you shall 2 Cor. 6:17. TG World; Worldliness.forsake the world.

Take upon you mine ordination, even that of an elder, to preach faith and repentance and TG Remission of Sins.remission of sins, according to my word, and the reception of the Holy Spirit by the laying on of TG Hands, Laying on of.hands;
And also to be an D&C 57:6 (6–15); 84:113.agent unto this church in the place which shall be appointed by the bishop, according to commandments which shall be given hereafter.
And again, verily I say unto you, you shall take your journey with my servants Joseph Smith, Jun., and Sidney Rigdon.
Behold, these are the first ordinances which you shall receive; and the residue shall be made known in a time to come, according to your labor in my vineyard.
And again, I would that ye should learn that he only is saved who Matt. 10:22.endureth unto the end. Even so. Amen.

div.p: Revelation given through Joseph Smith the Prophet to Newel Knight, at Kirtland, Ohio, June 10, 1831. Members of the Church living in Thompson, Ohio, were divided on questions having to do with the consecration of properties. Selfishness and greed were manifest. Following his mission to the Shakers (see the heading to section 49), Leman Copley had broken his covenant to consecrate his large farm as a place of inheritance for the Saints arriving from Colesville, New York. As a consequence, Newel Knight (leader of the members living in Thompson) and other elders had come to the Prophet asking how to proceed. The Prophet inquired of the Lord and received this revelation, which commands the members in Thompson to leave Leman Copley’s farm and journey to Missouri.
1–6, The Saints must keep the gospel covenant to gain mercy; 7–10, They must be patient in tribulation.

chapter.verse: Behold, thus saith the Lord, even Rev. 1:8; D&C 19:1; 75:1.Alpha and Omega, the beginning and the end, even he who was 1 Cor. 15:3. TG Jesus Christ, Crucifixion of.crucified for the sins of the world—
Behold, verily, verily, I say unto you, my servant Newel Knight, you shall stand fast in the office whereunto I have appointed you.
And if your brethren desire to escape their enemies, let them repent of all their sins, and become truly Jer. 44:10.humble before me and contrite.
And as the covenant which they made unto me has been Josh. 23:16 (15–16).broken, even so it has become D&C 58:32 (32–33).void and of none effect.
And wo to him by whom this Matt. 18:6 (6–7); Luke 17:2 (1–2). TG Offense.offense cometh, for it had been better for him that he had been drowned in the depth of the sea.
But blessed are they who have kept the 1 Kgs. 8:23. TG Covenants.covenant and observed the 1 Kgs. 3:14 (12–14).commandment, for they shall obtain TG Mercy.mercy.
Wherefore, go to now and flee the land, lest your enemies come upon you; and take your journey, and appoint whom you will to be your leader, and to pay moneys for you.
And thus you shall take your journey into the regions westward, unto the land of D&C 52:3 (3, 42).Missouri, unto the borders of the Lamanites.
And after you have done journeying, behold, I say unto you, seek ye a 1 Thes. 4:11.living like unto men, until I prepare a place for you.

And again, be TG Patience; Test; Tribulation.patient in tribulation until I Rev. 22:12. TG Jesus Christ, Second Coming.come; and, behold, I come quickly, and my TG Reward.reward is with me, and they who have Prov. 8:17. TG Prayer.sought me early shall find Ps. 72:7; Matt. 11:29; D&C 45:66. TG Rest.rest to their souls. Even so. Amen.

div.p: Revelation given through Joseph Smith the Prophet to William W. Phelps, at Kirtland, Ohio, June 14, 1831. William W. Phelps, a printer, and his family had just arrived at Kirtland, and the Prophet sought the Lord for information concerning him.

1–3, William W. Phelps is called and chosen to be baptized, to be ordained an elder, and to preach the gospel; 4, He is also to write books for children in Church schools; 5–6, He is to travel to Missouri, which will be the area of his labors.

chapter.verse: Behold, thus saith the Lord unto you, my servant William, yea, even the Lord of the whole Deut. 10:14; 1 Ne. 11:6; 2 Ne. 29:7.earth, thou art called and chosen; and after thou hast been TG Baptism, Essential.baptized by water, which if you do with an eye single to my glory, you shall have a remission of your sins and a reception of the Holy Spirit by the laying on of TG Hands, Laying on of.hands;

And then thou shalt be ordained by the hand of my servant Joseph Smith, Jun., to be an elder unto this church, to preach repentance and TG Remission of Sins.remission of sins by way of baptism in the name of Jesus Christ, the Son of the living God.
And on whomsoever you shall D&C 20:41.lay your hands, if they are contrite before me, you shall have power to give the Holy Spirit.

And again, you shall be ordained to assist my servant Oliver Cowdery to do the work of printing, and of selecting and writing D&C 88:118; 97:3 (3–6); 109:7 (7, 14).books for TG Education.schools in this church, that little TG Children.children also may receive TG Family, Children, Responsibilities toward.instruction before me as is pleasing unto me.

And again, verily I say unto you, for this cause you shall take your journey with my servants Joseph Smith, Jun., and Sidney Rigdon, that you may be Amos 9:15.planted in the land of your inheritance to do this work.
And again, let my servant D&C 102:3 (3, 34).Joseph Coe also take his journey with them. The residue shall be made known hereafter, even as I will. Amen.

div.p: Revelation given through Joseph Smith the Prophet, at Kirtland, Ohio, June 15, 1831. This revelation chastises Ezra Thayre for not obeying a former revelation (the “commandment” referred to in verse 8), which Joseph Smith had received for him, instructing Thayre concerning his duties on Frederick G. Williams’ farm, where he lived. The following revelation also revokes Thayre’s call to travel to Missouri with Thomas B. Marsh (see section 52:22).

1–2, The Saints must take up their cross and follow the Lord to gain salvation; 3–13, The Lord commands and revokes, and the disobedient are cast off; 14–17, Wo unto the rich who will not help the poor, and wo unto the poor whose hearts are not broken; 18–20, Blessed are the poor who are pure in heart, for they will inherit the earth.

chapter.verse: Hearken, O ye people who D&C 41:1; 50:4; 112:26.profess my name, saith the Lord your God; for behold, mine anger is TG Provoking.kindled against the rebellious, and they shall know mine arm and mine indignation, in the day of Jer. 10:15; Hosea 9:7; D&C 1:14 (13–14).visitation and of wrath upon the nations.
And he that will not take up his Luke 14:27.cross and Matt. 8:19; 2 Ne. 31:10 (10–13); Moro. 7:11. TG Jesus Christ, Exemplar.follow me, and keep my commandments, the same shall not be saved.

Behold, I, the Lord, command; and he that will not TG Disobedience; Obedience.obey shall be D&C 1:14; 50:8; 64:35 (35–36).cut off in mine own due time, after I have commanded and the commandment is broken.

Wherefore I, the Lord, command and Num. 14:34; Jer. 18:10 (6–10); D&C 19:5; 58:32 (31–33); 75:6.revoke, as it seemeth me good; and all this to be answered upon the heads of the TG Rebellion.rebellious, saith the Lord.

Wherefore, I revoke the commandment which was given unto my servants D&C 31:1; 52:22; 75:31.Thomas B. Marsh and Ezra Thayre, and give a new commandment unto my servant Thomas, that he shall take up his journey speedily to the land of Missouri, and my servant Selah J. Griffin shall also go with him.

For behold, I revoke the commandment which was given unto my servants D&C 52:32.Selah J. Griffin and Newel Knight, in consequence of the TG Stiffnecked.stiffneckedness of my people which are in Thompson, and their rebellions.
Wherefore, let my servant Newel Knight remain with them; and as many as will go may go, that are contrite before me, and be led by him to the land which I have appointed.

And again, verily I say unto you, that my servant Ezra Thayre must repent of his TG Pride.pride, and of his TG Selfishness.selfishness, and obey the former commandment which I have given him concerning the place upon which he lives.
And if he will do this, as there shall be no divisions made upon the land, he shall be appointed still to go to the land of Missouri;
Otherwise he shall receive the D&C 51:5.money which he has paid, and shall leave the place, and shall be TG Apostasy of Individuals.cut off out of my church, saith the Lord God of hosts;
And though the heaven and the earth pass away, these words shall not 2 Ne. 9:16.pass away, but shall be fulfilled.

And if my servant Joseph Smith, Jun., must needs pay the money, behold, I, the Lord, will pay it unto him again in the land of Missouri, that those of whom he shall receive may be rewarded again according to that which they do;
For according to that which they do they shall receive, even in lands for their inheritance.

Behold, thus saith the Lord unto my people—you have many things to do and to repent of; for behold, your Jonah 1:2.sins have come up unto me, and are not Ex. 34:7. TG Forgive.pardoned, because you seek to Moses 6:43.counsel in your own ways.
And your hearts are not satisfied. And ye obey not the truth, but have Luke 21:34.pleasure in unrighteousness.

Wo unto you Jer. 17:11; Luke 21:1 (1–4); 2 Ne. 9:30; Mosiah 4:23; D&C 84:112. TG Treasure.rich men, that will not TG Almsgiving; Generosity.give your substance to the Prov. 14:20 (20, 31); Alma 5:55 (54–56); Hel. 6:39 (39–40). TG Poor.poor, for your Ps. 62:10; James 5:3; Alma 5:53 (53–56).riches will canker your souls; and this shall be your lamentation in the day of visitation, and of judgment, and of indignation: The Jer. 8:20; D&C 45:2.harvest is past, the summer is ended, and my soul is not saved!

Wo unto you Ex. 23:3 (1–3); Mosiah 4:25 (24–27); D&C 42:42; 68:30 (30–32).poor men, whose hearts are not broken, whose spirits are not contrite, and whose bellies are not satisfied, and whose hands are not stayed from laying hold upon other men’s goods, whose eyes are full of TG Covet.greediness, and who will not TG Labor; Laziness.labor with your own hands!

But blessed are the Ps. 35:10; 86:1; 109:31; Isa. 25:4; Matt. 5:3 (3, 8); Luke 6:20; 3 Ne. 12:3. TG Poor.poor who are pure in heart, whose hearts are broken, and whose spirits are TG Contrite Heart; Humility; Meek.contrite, for they shall see the TG Kingdom of God, on Earth.kingdom of God coming in power and great glory unto their deliverance; for the fatness of the TG Earth, Destiny of.earth shall be theirs.
For behold, the Lord shall come, and his Prov. 11:31; Rev. 22:12; D&C 1:10.recompense shall be with him, and he shall TG Reward.reward every man, and the poor shall rejoice;
And their generations shall Isa. 29:19; Matt. 5:5; D&C 45:58; 57:5.inherit the earth from generation to generation, forever and ever. And now I make an end of speaking unto you. Even so. Amen.

div.p: Revelation given through Joseph Smith the Prophet, in Zion, Jackson County, Missouri, July 20, 1831. In compliance with the Lord’s command to travel to Missouri, where He would reveal “the land of your inheritance” (section 52), the elders had journeyed from Ohio to Missouri’s western border. Joseph Smith contemplated the state of the Lamanites and wondered: “When will the wilderness blossom as the rose? When will Zion be built up in her glory, and where will Thy temple stand, unto which all nations shall come in the last days?” Subsequently he received this revelation.

1–3, Independence, Missouri, is the place for the City of Zion and the temple; 4–7, The Saints are to purchase lands and receive inheritances in that area; 8–16, Sidney Gilbert is to establish a store, William W. Phelps is to be a printer, and Oliver Cowdery is to edit material for publication.

chapter.verse: Hearken, O ye elders of my church, saith the Lord your God, who have assembled yourselves together, according to my commandments, in this land, which is the land of D&C 52:3 (3, 42).Missouri, which is the D&C 29:8 (7–8); 45:66 (64–66); 52:2 (2, 42); 58:1; 103:24.land which I have appointed and D&C 61:17.consecrated for the TG Mission of Latter-day Saints.gathering of the saints.
Wherefore, this is the D&C 48:5; 52:5 (5, 42).land of promise, and the D&C 28:9; 42:9 (9, 62); 103:24 (22–24).place for the city of D&C 62:4; 78:3. TG Zion.Zion.

And thus saith the Lord your God, if you will receive wisdom here is wisdom. Behold, the place which is now called Independence is the D&C 69:6.center place; and a spot for the D&C 58:57; 84:3 (3–5, 31); 97:10 (10–20); 124:51 (49–51).temple is lying westward, upon a lot which is not far from the courthouse.

Wherefore, it is wisdom that the land should be D&C 48:4. TG Jerusalem, New; Zion.purchased by the saints, and also every tract lying westward, even unto the line running directly IE by metonymy “Jew” here refers to the Lamanites, and “Gentile” to the white settlers.between Jew and Gentile;

And also every tract bordering by the prairies, inasmuch as my disciples are enabled to D&C 42:35; 58:49 (49–51); 101:70 (68–74); 103:23 (22–24).buy lands. Behold, this is wisdom, that they may Matt. 5:5; D&C 56:20; 59:2. TG Lands of Inheritance.obtain it for an everlasting inheritance.

And let my servant Sidney Gilbert stand in the office to which I have appointed him, to receive moneys, to be an D&C 53:4; 64:18; 70:11.agent unto the church, to buy land in all the regions round about, inasmuch as can be done in righteousness, and as wisdom shall direct.

And let my servant D&C 52:24; 58:14 (14, 19, 24).Edward Partridge stand in the D&C 58:40 (40–41); 61:7 (7–12).office to which I have appointed him, and D&C 41:9 (9–11); 51:1 (1–20); 58:17 (14–18).divide unto the saints their inheritance, even as I have commanded; and also those whom he has appointed to assist him.

And again, verily I say unto you, let my servant Sidney Gilbert plant himself in this place, and establish a D&C 63:42; 64:26.store, that he may sell goods TG Honesty.without fraud, that he may obtain money to buy D&C 48:4.lands for the good of the saints, and that he may obtain whatsoever things the disciples may need to plant them in their inheritance.

And also let my servant Sidney Gilbert obtain a license—behold here is Rev. 13:18.wisdom, and whoso readeth let him Matt. 24:15.understand—that he may send goods also unto the people, even by whom he will as clerks employed in his service;
And thus provide for my saints, that my gospel may be preached unto those who sit in Matt. 4:16. TG Darkness, Spiritual.darkness and in the region and Job 3:5; Ps. 23:4.shadow of death.
And again, verily I say unto you, let my servant D&C 58:40; 61:7 (7–9); 70:1.William W. Phelps be planted in this place, and be established as a D&C 58:37 (37, 40–41).printer unto the church.
And lo, if the world receive his writings—behold here is wisdom—let him obtain whatsoever he can obtain in righteousness, for the good of the saints.

And let my servant See Index for many references to Oliver Cowdery.Oliver Cowdery assist him, even as I have commanded, in whatsoever place I shall appoint unto him, to copy, and to correct, and select, that all things may be right before me, as it shall be proved by the Spirit through him.
And thus let those of whom I have spoken be planted in the land of Zion, as speedily as can be, with their TG Family.families, to do those things even as I have spoken.

And now concerning the gathering—Let the bishop and the agent make preparations for those families which have been commanded to come to this land, as soon as possible, and plant them in their inheritance.
And unto the residue of both elders and members further directions shall be given hereafter. Even so. Amen.

div.p: Revelation given through Joseph Smith the Prophet, in Zion, Jackson County, Missouri, August 1, 1831. Earlier, on the first Sabbath after the arrival of the Prophet and his party in Jackson County, Missouri, a religious service had been held, and two members had been received by baptism. During that week, some of the Colesville Saints from the Thompson Branch and others arrived (see section 54). Many were eager to learn the will of the Lord concerning them in the new place of gathering.

1–5, Those who endure tribulation will be crowned with glory; 6–12, The Saints are to prepare for the marriage of the Lamb and the supper of the Lord; 13–18, Bishops are judges in Israel; 19–23, The Saints are to obey the laws of the land; 24–29, Men should use their agency to do good; 30–33, The Lord commands and revokes; 34–43, To repent, men must confess and forsake their sins; 44–58, The Saints are to purchase their inheritance and gather in Missouri; 59–65, The gospel must be preached unto every creature.

chapter.verse: Hearken, O ye elders of my church, and give Isa. 50:5 (5–7).ear to my word, and learn of me what I will concerning you, and also concerning D&C 57:1 (1–8).this land unto which I have sent you.

For verily I say unto you, blessed is he that Ps. 19:11 (9–11); Mosiah 2:22.keepeth my commandments, whether in life or in 1 Pet. 4:6.death; and he that is 2 Thes. 1:4. TG Steadfastness.faithful in TG Adversity.tribulation, the TG Reward.reward of the same is greater in the kingdom of heaven.

Ye cannot behold with your natural TG God, Privilege of Seeing.eyes, for the present time, the design of your God concerning those things which shall come hereafter, and the 1 Pet. 1:11. TG Glory.glory which shall follow after much tribulation.

For after much Ps. 30:5; D&C 101:2 (2–7); 103:12 (11–14); 109:76. TG Tribulation.tribulation come the TG Blessing.blessings. Wherefore the day cometh that ye shall be TG Exaltation.crowned with much Rom. 8:18; D&C 63:66; 136:31.glory; the hour is not yet, but is nigh at hand.
Remember this, which I tell you before, that you may Deut. 11:18.lay it to heart, and receive that which is to follow.

Behold, verily I say unto you, for this cause I have sent you—that you might be obedient, and that your hearts might be 3 Ne. 17:3; D&C 29:8; 132:3.prepared to Isa. 43:10 (10–12); 44:8.bear TG Testimony.testimony of the things which are to come;
And also that you might be honored in laying the foundation, and in bearing record of the land upon which the TG Zion.Zion of God shall stand;

And also that a feast of fat things might be prepared for the Ps. 132:15 (13–16). TG Poor.poor; yea, a feast of fat things, of wine on the Isa. 25:6.lees well refined, that the earth may know that the mouths of the prophets shall not fail;
Yea, a supper of the house of the Lord, well prepared, unto which all TG Nations.nations shall be invited.
First, the rich and the learned, the wise and the noble;

And after that cometh the day of my power; then shall the TG Poor.poor, the lame, and the blind, and the deaf, come in unto the Matt. 22:2 (1–14); Rev. 19:9; D&C 65:3.marriage of the Lamb, and partake of the Luke 14:16 (16–24).supper of the Lord, prepared for the great day to come.
Behold, I, the Lord, have spoken it.
And that the Micah 4:2. TG Testimony.testimony might go forth from Zion, yea, from the mouth of the city of the heritage of God—
Yea, for this cause I have sent you hither, and have selected my servant D&C 57:7; 60:10.Edward Partridge, and have appointed unto him his mission in this land.
But if he repent not of his sins, which are TG Doubt.unbelief and blindness of TG Hardheartedness.heart, let him take heed lest he 1 Cor. 10:12.fall.
Behold his mission is given unto him, and it shall not be given again.

And whoso standeth in this mission is appointed to be a Deut. 16:18; D&C 64:40; 107:72 (72–74). TG Bishop.judge in Israel, like as it was in ancient days, to D&C 41:9 (9–11); 51:1 (1–20); 57:7.divide the lands of the heritage of God unto his TG Sons and Daughters of God.children;

And to judge his people by the testimony of the just, and by the assistance of his TG Counselor.counselors, according to the laws of the kingdom which are given by the TG Prophets, Mission of.prophets of God.
For verily I say unto you, my law shall be kept on this land.

Let no man think he is TG Unrighteous Dominion.ruler; but let God rule him that judgeth, according to the counsel of his own TG God, Will of.will, or, in other words, him that counseleth or sitteth upon the judgment seat.

Let no man break the Matt. 17:24 (24–27); Luke 20:22 (22–26); D&C 20:1; 51:6; 98:9 (4–10); A of F 1:12. TG Citizenship; Governments.laws of the land, for he that keepeth the laws of God hath no need to break the laws of the land.

Wherefore, be Rom. 13:1 (1–7). TG Governments; Submissiveness.subject to the powers that be, Gen. 49:10; Ezek. 21:27; Zech. 9:10; D&C 76:63. TG Jesus Christ, Messiah; Jesus Christ, Millennial Reign.until he reigns whose right it is to reign, and subdues all enemies under his feet.
Behold, the D&C 42:2 (2–28).laws which ye have received from my hand are the laws of the church, and in this light ye shall hold them forth. Behold, here is wisdom.

And now, as I spake concerning my servant Edward Partridge, this land is the land of his D&C 41:9; 72:6.residence, and those whom he has appointed for his counselors; and also the land of the residence of him whom I have appointed to keep my D&C 51:13; 70:7 (7–11).storehouse;
Wherefore, let them bring their families to this land, as they shall TG Guidance, Divine.counsel between themselves and me.

For behold, it is not meet that I should command in all things; for he that is TG Initiative.compelled in all things, the same is a Matt. 24:45 (45–51); D&C 107:100 (99–100). TG Apathy; Laziness.slothful and not a wise servant; wherefore he receiveth no reward.
Verily I say, men should be TG Dedication; Diligence; Good Works; Industry; Zeal.anxiously engaged in a good cause, and do many things of their own free will, and bring to pass much righteousness;
For the power is in them, wherein they are Amos 5:14. TG Agency.agents unto themselves. And inasmuch as men do good they shall in nowise lose their TG Reward.reward.
But he that Moro. 7:6 (6–9).doeth not anything until he is commanded, and receiveth a commandment with TG Doubt.doubtful heart, and keepeth it with slothfulness, the same is TG Damnation.damned.
Who am I that Isa. 45:9 (9–10); Hel. 12:6 (6–22).made man, saith the Lord, that will hold him Ex. 20:7; Mosiah 13:15; Morm. 7:7.guiltless that obeys not my commandments?
Who am I, saith the Lord, that have D&C 1:37 (37–38); 82:10.promised and have not fulfilled?
I command and men 1 Sam. 28:18. TG Disobedience.obey not; I Jer. 18:10 (6–10); D&C 19:5; 54:4; 56:4 (3–4).revoke and they receive not the blessing.

Then they 1 Ne. 15:9 (7–11).say in their hearts: This is not the work of the Lord, for his promises are not fulfilled. But wo unto such, for their TG Reward.reward lurketh D&C 29:45.beneath, and not from above.
And now I give unto you further directions concerning this land.
It is wisdom in me that my servant Martin Harris should be an example unto the church, in D&C 42:31 (30–32).laying his moneys before the bishop of the church.
And also, this is a law unto every man that cometh unto this land to receive an inheritance; and he shall do with his moneys according as the law directs.
And it is wisdom also that there should be lands purchased in Independence, for the place of the storehouse, and also for the house of the D&C 57:11 (11–12).printing.
And other directions concerning my servant Martin Harris shall be given him of the Spirit, that he may receive his inheritance as seemeth him good;
And let him repent of his sins, for he seeketh the 2 Ne. 26:29; D&C 121:35 (34–37).praise of the world.
And also let my servant D&C 57:11; 61:7 (7–9); 70:1.William W. Phelps stand in the office to which I have appointed him, and receive his inheritance in the land;
And also he hath need to repent, for I, the Lord, am not well pleased with him, for he seeketh to excel, and he is not sufficiently meek before me.
Behold, he who has TG Repent.repented of his Ps. 25:7.sins, the same is Isa. 1:18; Jer. 31:34. TG Forgive.forgiven, and I, the Lord, remember them no more.
By this ye may know if a man repenteth of his sins—behold, he will Num. 5:7 (6–10); D&C 19:20; 64:7. TG Confession.confess them and D&C 82:7.forsake them.

And now, verily, I say concerning the residue of the elders of my TG Jesus Christ, Head of the Church.church, the time has not yet come, for many years, for them to receive their TG Lands of Inheritance.inheritance in this land, except they desire it through the prayer of faith, only as it shall be appointed unto them of the Lord.
For, behold, they shall Deut. 33:17.push the people together from the TG Israel, Gathering of.ends of the earth.
Wherefore, assemble yourselves together; and they who are not appointed to stay in this land, let them preach the gospel in the regions round about; and after that let them return to their homes.
Let them preach by the way, and D&C 19:37; 63:37; 68:8; 71:7.bear testimony of the truth in all places, and call upon the rich, the high and the low, and the poor to repent.
And let them build up IE branches of the Church.churches, inasmuch as the inhabitants of the earth will repent.
And let there be an agent appointed by the voice of the church, unto the church in Ohio, to receive moneys to D&C 42:35; 57:5 (5–7); 101:70 (68–74); 103:23 (22–24).purchase lands in TG Zion.Zion.

And I give unto my servant Sidney Rigdon a commandment, that he shall D&C 63:56 (55–56); 100:9.write a description of the land of Zion, and a statement of the will of God, as it shall be made known by the Spirit unto him;

And an epistle and subscription, to be presented unto all the churches to obtain moneys, to be put into the hands of the bishop, of himself or the agent, as seemeth him good or as he shall direct, to D&C 51:8 (8–13); 84:104.purchase lands for an D&C 25:2; 52:42 (2, 5, 42).inheritance for the children of God.
For, behold, verily I say unto you, the Lord willeth that the disciples and the children of men should open their hearts, even to purchase this whole region of country, as soon as time will permit.
Behold, here is wisdom. Let them do this lest they D&C 63:29 (27–31); 101:70 (70–75).receive none inheritance, save it be by the shedding of blood.
And again, inasmuch as there is land obtained, let there be workmen sent forth of all kinds unto this land, to labor for the saints of God.
Let all these things be done in D&C 101:68. TG Order.order; and let the privileges of the lands be made known from time to time, by the bishop or the agent of the church.

And let the work of the D&C 63:24; 101:68.gathering be not in TG Haste; Rashness.haste, nor by flight; but let it be done as it shall be D&C 72:24.counseled by the elders of the church at the conferences, according to the knowledge which they receive from time to time.

And let my servant Sidney Rigdon D&C 52:2; 103:35; 105:15.consecrate and TG Dedication.dedicate this land, and the spot for the D&C 57:3; 84:3 (3–5, 31); 97:10 (10–20); 124:51 (49–51).temple, unto the Lord.

And let a conference meeting be called; and after that let my servants Sidney Rigdon and Joseph Smith, Jun., return, and also Oliver Cowdery with them, to accomplish the residue of the work which I have appointed unto them in their own land, and the residue as shall be TG Church Organization; Common Consent.ruled by the conferences.
And let no man return from this land except he bear TG Mission of Latter-day Saints; Testimony.record by the way, of that which he knows and most assuredly believes.

Let that which has been bestowed upon D&C 32:3.Ziba Peterson be taken from him; and let him stand as a member in the church, and labor with his own hands, with the brethren, until he is sufficiently TG Chastening; Forgive; Repent.chastened for all his sins; for he confesseth them not, and he thinketh to hide them.
Let the residue of the elders of this church, who are coming to this land, some of whom are exceedingly blessed even above measure, also hold a TG Meetings.conference upon this land.
And let my servant Edward Partridge direct the conference which shall be held by them.
And let them also return, preaching the gospel by the way, bearing record of the things which are revealed unto them.

For, verily, the sound must go forth from this place into all the world, and unto the uttermost parts of the earth—the gospel must be TG Mission of Latter-day Saints.preached unto every creature, with TG Holy Ghost, Gifts of; Miracle; Signs.signs following them that believe.
And behold the Son of Man D&C 1:12.cometh. Amen.

div.p: Revelation given through Joseph Smith the Prophet, in Zion, Jackson County, Missouri, August 7, 1831. Preceding this revelation, the land was consecrated, as the Lord had directed, and the site for the future temple was dedicated. On the day this revelation was received, Polly Knight, the wife of Joseph Knight Sr., died, the first Church member to die in Zion. Early members characterized this revelation as “instructing the Saints how to keep the sabbath and how to fast and pray.”

1–4, The faithful Saints in Zion will be blessed; 5–8, They are to love and serve the Lord and keep His commandments; 9–19, By keeping the Lord’s day holy, the Saints are blessed temporally and spiritually; 20–24, The righteous are promised peace in this world and eternal life in the world to come.

chapter.verse: Behold, blessed, saith the Lord, are they who have come up unto this land with an Matt. 6:22 (22–24).eye single to my glory, according to my commandments.

For those that live shall Matt. 5:5; D&C 57:5; 63:20 (20, 48–49).inherit the earth, and those that Isa. 57:1 (1–2); Rev. 14:13. TG Paradise.die shall rest from all their labors, and their works shall follow them; and they shall receive a TG Celestial Glory; Exaltation.crown in the Ps. 65:4; John 14:2; D&C 72:4; 76:111; 81:6; 98:18; 106:8.mansions of my Father, which I have prepared for them.

Yea, blessed are they whose feet stand upon the land of Zion, who have obeyed my gospel; for they shall receive for their reward the good things of the earth, and it shall bring forth in its Gen. 4:12; Moses 4:23 (23–24); 5:37. TG Strength.strength.

And they shall also be crowned with blessings from above, yea, and with Alma 29:9.commandments not a few, and with D&C 42:61; 76:7; 98:12; 101:32; 121:28 (26–33). TG Revelation.revelations in their time—they that are TG Steadfastness; Worthiness.faithful and TG Diligence.diligent before me.

Wherefore, I give unto them a commandment, saying thus: Thou shalt Deut. 11:1; Matt. 22:37; Mosiah 2:4; Moro. 10:32; D&C 20:19. TG God, Love of; Love.love the Lord thy God with all thy TG Heart.heart, with all thy might, mind, and strength; and in the name of Jesus Christ thou shalt TG Service; Worship.serve him.

Thou shalt TG Love.love thy Prov. 11:12 (12–17). TG Fellowshipping; Neighbor.neighbor as thyself. Thou shalt not TG Stealing.steal; neither commit TG Adulterer; Chastity.adultery, nor TG Murder.kill, nor do anything Ex. 22:19; Lev. 18:22; 1 Cor. 6:9; 1 Tim. 1:10.like unto it.
Thou shalt Ezra 3:11; Ps. 34:1 (1–3); 92:1; Alma 37:37; Ether 6:9; D&C 46:32. TG Communication; Thanksgiving.thank the Lord thy God in all things.

Thou shalt offer a TG Jesus Christ, Types of, in Memory; Self-Sacrifice.sacrifice unto the Lord thy God in TG Righteousness.righteousness, even that of a broken heart and a TG Contrite Heart; Poor in Spirit.contrite spirit.

And that thou mayest more fully keep thyself James 1:27. TG Abstain; Self-Mastery.unspotted from the world, thou shalt go to the house of TG Assembly for Worship; Prayer.prayer and offer up thy D&C 62:4. TG Sacrament.sacraments upon my Lev. 19:3; 23:3; Alma 1:26 (26–27); D&C 68:29. TG Sabbath.holy day;
For verily this is a Ex. 35:2; Lev. 23:25. TG Rest; Worship.day appointed unto you to rest from your labors, and to pay thy devotions unto the Most High;
Nevertheless thy TG Commitment; Vow.vows shall be offered up in righteousness on all days and at all times;

But remember that on this, the Neh. 8:10; Rev. 1:10.Lord’s day, thou shalt offer thine IE offerings, whether of time, talents, or means, in service of God and fellowman. TG Sacrifice.oblations and thy sacraments unto the Most High, TG Confession.confessing thy sins unto thy brethren, and before the Lord.

And on this day thou shalt do none other thing, only let thy food be prepared with singleness of heart that thy IE hungering and thirsting after righteousness; see Matt. 5:6; 3 Ne. 12:6. TG Fast, Fasting.fasting may be perfect, or, in other words, that thy TG Joy.joy may be full.
Verily, this is fasting and prayer, or in other words, rejoicing and prayer.

And inasmuch as ye do these things with TG Thanksgiving.thanksgiving, with Ex. 25:2 (1–7); 35:5; D&C 64:22 (22, 34); 97:8. TG Cheerful.cheerful Prov. 17:22.hearts and countenances, not with 1 Pet. 4:3; D&C 88:69.much TG Laughter.laughter, for this is sin, but with a glad heart and a cheerful countenance—

Verily I say, that inasmuch as ye do this, the TG Abundant Life.fulness of the earth is yours, the beasts of the field and the fowls of the air, and that which climbeth upon the trees and walketh upon the earth;

Yea, and the herb, and the Gen. 1:31; Moro. 7:12; Moses 2:31.good things which come of the earth, whether for food or for D&C 70:16 (15–16).raiment, or for houses, or for barns, or for orchards, or for gardens, or for vineyards;

Yea, all things which come of the earth, in the season thereof, are made for the TG Earth, Purpose of; Meat.benefit and the Gen. 1:29; 9:3 (3–4).use of man, both to please the eye and to TG Happiness.gladden the heart;
Yea, for TG Food.food and for raiment, for taste and for smell, to TG Health.strengthen the body and to enliven the soul.
And it pleaseth God that he hath given all these things unto man; for unto this end were they made to be used, with judgment, not to TG Temperance.excess, neither by extortion.

And in nothing doth man TG Offense.offend God, or against none is his TG God, Indignation of.wrath TG Provoking.kindled, save those who Neh. 12:24; Job 1:21; Ps. 97:12; Mosiah 2:20 (20–22); D&C 62:7. TG Ingratitude.confess not his hand in all things, and TG Disobedience.obey not his commandments.
Behold, this is according to the law and the prophets; wherefore, trouble me no more concerning this matter.

But learn that he who doeth the works of TG Righteousness.righteousness shall receive his TG Blessing; Reward.reward, even Matt. 11:29 (28–30). TG Happiness; Objectives; Peace; Peace of God.peace in this world, and D&C 14:7.eternal life in the world to come.
I, the Lord, have spoken it, and the Spirit beareth record. Amen.

div.p: Revelation given through Joseph Smith the Prophet, in Independence, Jackson County, Missouri, August 8, 1831. On this occasion the elders who had traveled to Jackson County and participated in the dedication of the land and the temple site desired to know what they were to do.

1–9, The elders are to preach the gospel in the congregations of the wicked; 10–14, They should not idle away their time, nor bury their talents; 15–17, They may wash their feet as a testimony against those who reject the gospel.

chapter.verse: Behold, thus saith the Lord unto the elders of his church, who are to return speedily to the land from whence they came: Behold, it pleaseth me, that you have come up hither;

But with some I am not well pleased, for they will not open their Ex. 4:10 (10–12); Jer. 1:6 (6–9); Luke 8:16 (16–18); Eph. 6:20 (19–20).mouths, but they hide the Matt. 25:25 (24–30). TG Talents.talent which I have given unto them, because of the TG Courage; Fearful; Peer Influence.fear of man. Wo unto such, for mine TG Anger; God, Indignation of.anger is TG Provoking.kindled against them.
And it shall come to pass, if they are not more faithful unto me, it shall be Matt. 25:29 (29–30); Mark 4:25; D&C 1:33.taken away, even that which they have.

For I, the Lord, Judg. 8:23 (22–23); Hel. 12:6.rule in the heavens above, and among the 2 Chr. 25:8. TG War.armies of the earth; and in the day when I shall make up my Isa. 62:3; Zech. 9:16; Mal. 3:17; D&C 101:3.jewels, all men shall know what it is that bespeaketh the power of God.

But, verily, I will speak unto you concerning your journey unto the land from whence you came. Let there be a craft made, or bought, as TG Agency; Initiative.seemeth you good, it mattereth not unto me, and take your journey speedily for the place which is called St. Louis.
And from thence let my servants, Sidney Rigdon, Joseph Smith, Jun., and Oliver Cowdery, take their journey for Cincinnati;

And in this place let them lift up their voice and declare my word with loud voices, without wrath or TG Doubt.doubting, lifting up holy hands upon them. For I am able to make you TG Holiness.holy, and your sins are TG Forgive.forgiven you.

And let the residue take their journey from St. Louis, Mark 6:7.two by two, and preach the word, not in haste, among the congregations of the wicked, until they return to the churches from whence they came.
And all this for the good of the D&C 51:10.churches; for this intent have I sent them.
And let my servant D&C 58:14 (14, 19, 24); 64:17.Edward Partridge impart of the D&C 51:8 (8–13).money which I have given him, a portion unto mine elders who are commanded to return;
And he that is able, let him return it by the way of the agent; and he that is not, of him it is not required.
And now I speak of the residue who are to come unto this land.

Behold, they have been sent to preach my gospel among the congregations of the wicked; wherefore, I give unto them a commandment, thus: Thou shalt not D&C 42:42. TG Apathy; Idleness; Laziness; Priesthood, Magnifying Callings within; Procrastination; Waste; Zeal.idle away thy time, neither shalt thou bury thy Matt. 25:25 (14–30); D&C 82:18.talent that it may not be known.

And after thou hast come up unto the land of Zion, and hast proclaimed my word, thou shalt speedily return, proclaiming my word among the congregations of the wicked, not in TG Rashness.haste, neither in Prov. 14:29.wrath nor with TG Strife.strife.

And shake off the Matt. 10:14; Luke 9:5; Acts 13:51; 18:6 (5–6); D&C 24:15; 75:20; 84:92 (92–95).dust of thy feet against those who receive thee not, not in their presence, lest thou TG Provoking.provoke them, but in secret; and TG Wash.wash thy feet, as a testimony against them in the day of judgment.
Behold, this is sufficient for you, and the will of him who hath sent you.
And by the mouth of my servant Joseph Smith, Jun., it shall be made known concerning Sidney Rigdon and Oliver Cowdery. The residue hereafter. Even so. Amen.

div.p: Revelation given through Joseph Smith the Prophet, on the bank of the Missouri River, McIlwaine’s Bend, August 12, 1831. On their return trip to Kirtland, the Prophet and ten elders had traveled down the Missouri River in canoes. On the third day of the journey, many dangers were experienced. Elder William W. Phelps, in a daylight vision, saw the destroyer riding in power upon the face of the waters.

1–12, The Lord has decreed many destructions upon the waters; 13–22, The waters were cursed by John, and the destroyer rides upon their face; 23–29, Some have power to command the waters; 30–35, Elders are to journey two by two and preach the gospel; 36–39, They are to prepare for the coming of the Son of Man.

chapter.verse: Behold, and hearken unto the voice of him who has all TG God, Power of.power, who is from everlasting to everlasting, even D&C 19:1.Alpha and Omega, the beginning and the end.

Behold, verily thus saith the Lord unto you, O ye elders of my church, who are assembled upon this spot, whose sins are now forgiven you, for I, the Lord, Mosiah 4:10 (10–11). TG Forgive.forgive sins, and am TG God, Mercy of.merciful unto those who TG Confession.confess their sins with humble hearts;
But verily I say unto you, that it is not needful for this whole company of mine elders to be moving swiftly upon the waters, whilst the inhabitants on either side are perishing in unbelief.
Nevertheless, I suffered it that ye might bear record; behold, there are many dangers upon the waters, and more especially hereafter;
For I, the Lord, have decreed in mine anger many destructions upon the waters; yea, and especially upon these waters.
Nevertheless, all flesh is in mine hand, and he that is faithful among you shall not TG Protection, Divine.perish by the waters.
Wherefore, it is expedient that my servant Sidney Gilbert and my servant D&C 57:11 (6–12); 58:40 (40–41); 70:1.William W. Phelps be in haste upon their errand and mission.
Nevertheless, I would not suffer that ye should part until you were TG Chastening.chastened for all your sins, that you might be one, that you might not perish in TG Wickedness.wickedness;

But now, verily I say, it behooveth me that ye should part. Wherefore let my servants Sidney Gilbert and William W. Phelps take their former company, and let them take their journey in haste that they may fill their mission, and through faith they shall overcome;
And inasmuch as they are Ps. 31:23.faithful they shall be preserved, and I, the Lord, will be Matt. 28:20.with them.
And let the residue take that which is needful for clothing.
Let my servant Sidney Gilbert take that which is not needful with him, as you shall agree.
And now, behold, for your Deut. 10:13; D&C 21:6.good I gave unto you a TG Commandments of God.commandment concerning these things; and I, the Lord, will reason with you as with men in days of old.
Behold, I, the Lord, in the beginning blessed the Gen. 1:20.waters; but in the last days, by the mouth of my servant John, I Rev. 8:10 (8–11). TG Curse; Last Days.cursed the waters.
Wherefore, the days will come that no flesh shall be safe upon the waters.
And it shall be said in days to come that none is able to go up to the land of Zion upon the waters, but he that is upright in heart.

And, as I, the Lord, in the beginning Moses 4:23.cursed the land, even so in the last days have I D&C 57:1 (1–5).blessed it, in its time, for the use of my saints, that they may partake the fatness thereof.

And now I give unto you a commandment that what I say unto one I say unto all, that you shall forewarn your brethren concerning these waters, that they come not in journeying upon them, lest their faith fail and they are caught in snares;
I, the Lord, have decreed, and the destroyer rideth upon the face thereof, and I revoke not the decree.
I, the Lord, was TG God, Indignation of; God, Love of.angry with you yesterday, but today mine anger is turned away.
Wherefore, let those concerning whom I have spoken, that should take their journey in haste—again I say unto you, let them take their journey in haste.

And it mattereth not unto me, after a little, if it so be that they fill their mission, whether they go by water or by land; let this be as it is made known unto them TG Agency; Initiative.according to their judgments hereafter.

And now, concerning my servants, Sidney Rigdon, Joseph Smith, Jun., and Oliver Cowdery, let them come not again upon the waters, save it be upon the canal, while journeying unto their homes; or in other words they shall not come upon the waters to journey, save upon the canal.

Behold, I, the Lord, have appointed a way for the journeying of my saints; and behold, this is the way—that after they leave the canal they shall journey by land, inasmuch as they are commanded to journey and go up unto the land of Zion;
And they shall do Num. 9:18.like unto the children of Israel, Num. 2:34 (32–34).pitching their tents by the way.
And, behold, this commandment you shall give unto all your brethren.
Nevertheless, unto whom is given TG Holy Ghost, Gifts of; Priesthood, Power of.power to command the waters, unto him it is given by the Spirit to know all his ways;
Wherefore, let him do as the Spirit of the living God commandeth him, whether upon the land or upon the waters, as it remaineth with me to do hereafter.
And unto you is given the course for the saints, or the way for the saints of the camp of the Lord, to journey.

And again, verily I say unto you, my servants, Sidney Rigdon, Joseph Smith, Jun., and Oliver Cowdery, shall not open their mouths in the Micah 6:10 (10–15); D&C 62:5; 68:1.congregations of the wicked until they arrive at Cincinnati;

And in that place they shall lift up their voices unto God against that people, yea, unto him whose anger is TG Provoking.kindled against their wickedness, a people who are well-nigh Alma 37:31; Hel. 13:14; D&C 101:11.ripened for destruction.
And from thence let them journey for the congregations of their brethren, for their labors even now are wanted more abundantly among them than among the congregations of the wicked.
And now, concerning the residue, let them journey and TG Preaching.declare the word among the congregations of the wicked, inasmuch as it is given;
And inasmuch as they do this they shall 2 Ne. 9:44; Jacob 2:2 (2, 16); Mosiah 2:28.rid their garments, and they shall be spotless before me.

And let them journey together, or Mark 6:7; Luke 10:1; D&C 52:10; 62:5. TG Missionary Work.two by two, as seemeth them good, only let my servant Reynolds Cahoon, and my servant Samuel H. Smith, with whom I am well pleased, be not separated until they return to their homes, and this for a wise purpose in me.

And now, verily I say unto you, and what I say unto one I say unto all, be of good TG Cheerful.cheer, John 13:33.little children; for I am in your Matt. 18:20; 28:20 (19–20).midst, and I have not Isa. 41:17 (15–17); 1 Ne. 21:15 (14–15).forsaken you;
And inasmuch as you have humbled yourselves before me, the blessings of the D&C 50:35; 62:9.kingdom are yours.
Gird up your loins and be TG Watch.watchful and be sober, looking forth for the coming of the Son of Man, for he cometh in an hour you think not.
Pray always that you enter not into TG Temptation.temptation, that you may abide the day of his coming, whether in life or in death. Even so. Amen.

div.p: Revelation given through Joseph Smith the Prophet, on the bank of the Missouri River at Chariton, Missouri, August 13, 1831. On this day the Prophet and his group, who were on their way from Independence to Kirtland, met several elders who were on their way to the land of Zion, and, after joyful salutations, received this revelation.
1–3, Testimonies are recorded in heaven; 4–9, The elders are to travel and preach according to judgment and as directed by the Spirit.

chapter.verse: Behold, and hearken, O ye elders of my church, saith the Lord your God, even Jesus Christ, your D&C 45:3. TG Jesus Christ, Advocate; Jesus Christ, Relationships with the Father.advocate, who knoweth the weakness of man and how to Heb. 2:18; Alma 7:12.succor them who are Luke 4:2.tempted.
And verily mine eyes are upon those who have not as yet gone up unto the land of Zion; wherefore your mission is not yet full.

Nevertheless, ye are TG Blessing.blessed, for the Luke 12:8 (8–9). TG Testimony.testimony which ye have borne is TG Book of Life.recorded in heaven for the angels to look upon; and they rejoice over you, and your Matt. 9:2; D&C 84:61.sins are forgiven you.

And now continue your journey. Assemble yourselves upon the land of D&C 57:2 (1–2).Zion; and hold a meeting and rejoice together, and offer a TG Assembly for Worship; Sacrament.sacrament unto the Most High.

And then you may return to bear record, yea, even altogether, or Mark 6:7; Luke 10:1; D&C 52:10; 61:35.two by two, as seemeth you good, it mattereth not unto me; only be faithful, and TG Missionary Work.declare glad tidings unto the inhabitants of the earth, or among the D&C 61:30.congregations of the wicked.

Behold, I, the Lord, have brought you together that the promise might be fulfilled, that the faithful among you should be preserved and rejoice together in the land of Missouri. I, the Lord, Ezek. 36:36.promise the faithful and cannot TG God, Perfection of; Honesty.lie.

I, the Lord, am willing, if any among you TG Agency.desire to ride upon horses, or upon mules, or in chariots, he shall receive this blessing, if he receive it from the hand of the Lord, with a Job 1:21; Mosiah 2:20 (20–22); D&C 59:21. TG Thanksgiving.thankful heart in all things.
These things remain with you to do according to judgment and the directions of the Spirit.
Behold, the D&C 61:37; 64:4.kingdom is yours. And behold, and lo, I am Matt. 28:20.with the faithful always. Even so. Amen.

div.p: Revelation given through Joseph Smith the Prophet, at Kirtland, Ohio, August 30, 1831. The Prophet, Sidney Rigdon, and Oliver Cowdery had arrived in Kirtland on August 27 from their visit to Missouri. Joseph Smith’s history describes this revelation: “In these infant days of the Church, there was a great anxiety to obtain the word of the Lord upon every subject that in any way concerned our salvation; and as the land of Zion was now the most important temporal object in view, I enquired of the Lord for further information upon the gathering of the Saints, and the purchase of the land, and other matters.”

1–6, A day of wrath will come upon the wicked; 7–12, Signs come by faith; 13–19, The adulterous in heart will deny the faith and be cast into the lake of fire; 20, The faithful will receive an inheritance upon the transfigured earth; 21, A full account of the events on the Mount of Transfiguration has not yet been revealed; 22–23, The obedient receive the mysteries of the kingdom; 24–31, Inheritances in Zion are to be purchased; 32–35, The Lord decrees wars, and the wicked slay the wicked; 36–48, The Saints are to gather to Zion and provide moneys to build it up; 49–54, Blessings are assured the faithful at the Second Coming, in the Resurrection, and during the Millennium; 55–58, This is a day of warning; 59–66, The Lord’s name is taken in vain by those who use it without authority.

chapter.verse: Hearken, O ye people, and open your hearts and give ear from afar; and listen, you that call yourselves the Lev. 26:12; 3 Ne. 20:19.people of the Lord, and hear the word of the Lord and his will concerning you.
Yea, verily, I say, hear the word of him whose anger is TG Provoking.kindled against the wicked and TG Rebellion.rebellious;
Who willeth to take even them whom he will Job 1:21; Isa. 57:1. TG Death.take, and Mosiah 2:20 (20–21).preserveth in life them whom he will preserve;
Who buildeth up at his own will and TG God, Justice of; Judgment.pleasure; and destroyeth when he pleases, and is able to Hel. 12:21 (20–22).cast the soul down to hell.
Behold, I, the Lord, utter my voice, and it shall be TG Obedience.obeyed.

Wherefore, verily I say, let the wicked take heed, and let the TG Rebellion.rebellious TG Day of the Lord.fear and tremble; and let the unbelieving hold their lips, for the D&C 1:13 (13–14).day of wrath shall come upon them as a Jer. 30:23; Dan. 11:40.whirlwind, and all flesh shall Isa. 49:26.know that I am God.
And he that seeketh TG Sign Seekers.signs shall see Mark 8:11 (11–21); D&C 46:9.signs, but not unto salvation.
Verily, I say unto you, there are those among you who seek signs, and there have been such even from the beginning;
But, behold, faith cometh not by signs, but Mark 16:17. TG Holy Ghost, Gifts of; Miracle; Signs.signs follow those that believe.
Yea, John 12:18 (9, 17–18); Rom. 15:19 (18–19); Morm. 9:19 (19–21).signs come by TG Faith.faith, not by the will of men, nor as they please, but by the will of God.

Yea, signs come by faith, unto mighty works, for without Heb. 11:6.faith no man pleaseth God; and with whom God is TG God, Indignation of.angry he is not well pleased; wherefore, unto such he showeth no signs, only in D&C 35:11.wrath unto their D&C 88:65.condemnation.
Wherefore, I, the Lord, am not pleased with those among you who have sought after signs and wonders for faith, and not for the good of men unto my glory.
Nevertheless, I give commandments, and many have turned away from my commandments and have TG Disobedience.not kept them.
There were among you D&C 42:24 (24–25).adulterers and adulteresses; some of whom have turned away from you, and others remain with you that hereafter shall be revealed.

Let such beware and repent speedily, lest judgment shall come upon them as a Luke 21:35.snare, and their 2 Tim. 3:9. TG Foolishness.folly shall be made manifest, and their works shall follow them in the eyes of the people.

And verily I say unto you, as I have said before, he that Matt. 5:28 (27–28).looketh on a woman to 2 Sam. 13:4 (2–4). TG Lust; Sensuality; Sexual Immorality.lust after her, or if any shall commit TG Adulterer; Chastity.adultery in their hearts, they shall not have the TG Holy Ghost, Loss of.Spirit, but shall deny the faith and shall fear.

Wherefore, I, the Lord, have said that the Rev. 21:8. TG Fearful.fearful, and the TG Unbelief.unbelieving, and all TG Honesty; Lying.liars, and whosoever loveth and maketh a lie, and the Rev. 22:15 (14–15); D&C 76:103. TG Whore.whoremonger, and the TG Sorcery.sorcerer, shall have their part in that Rev. 19:20; 2 Ne. 9:16 (8–19, 26); 28:23; Jacob 6:10; Alma 12:17 (16–18); D&C 76:36. TG Hell.lake which burneth with fire and brimstone, which is the TG Death, Spiritual, Second.second death.
Verily I say, that they shall not have part in the Rev. 20:6.first resurrection.
And now behold, I, the Lord, say unto you that ye are not TG Justification.justified, because these things are among you.

Nevertheless, he that 2 Cor. 1:6; D&C 101:35. TG Steadfastness.endureth in faith and doeth my TG God, Will of.will, the same shall overcome, and shall receive an Isa. 29:19; Matt. 5:5; D&C 59:2; 88:26 (25–26). TG Inheritance.inheritance upon the earth when the day of transfiguration shall come;

When the TG Earth, Destiny of.earth shall be TG Transfiguration; World, End of.transfigured, even according to the pattern which was shown unto mine apostles upon the Matt. 17:3 (1–3).mount; of which account the fulness ye have not yet received.

And now, verily I say unto you, that as I said that I would make known my will unto you, behold I will make it known unto you, 2 Cor. 8:8; D&C 28:5.not by the way of commandment, for there are many who observe not to keep my commandments.

But unto him that keepeth my commandments I will give the Alma 12:9 (9–11); D&C 42:61; 84:19; 107:19 (18–19).mysteries of my kingdom, and the same shall be in him a well of living TG Living Water.water, John 4:14.springing up unto everlasting life.

And now, behold, this is the will of the Lord your God concerning his saints, that they should D&C 58:56; 101:68.assemble themselves together unto the land of Zion, not in haste, lest there should be confusion, which bringeth pestilence.
Behold, the land of TG Zion.Zion—I, the Lord, hold it in mine own hands;
Nevertheless, I, the Lord, render unto Luke 20:25; 23:2; D&C 58:22 (21–23). TG Governments.Cæsar the things which are Cæsar’s.

Wherefore, I the Lord will that you should D&C 45:65.purchase the lands, that you may have advantage of the world, that you may have claim on the world, that they may not be TG Provoking.stirred up unto anger.
For TG Devil.Satan John 13:2; 2 Ne. 28:20.putteth it into their hearts to anger against you, and to the shedding of blood.
Wherefore, the land of Zion shall not be obtained but by D&C 58:53; 101:70 (70–75).purchase or by blood, otherwise there is none inheritance for you.
And if by purchase, behold you are blessed;

And if by TG Blood, Shedding of.blood, as you are forbidden to shed blood, lo, your enemies are upon you, and ye shall be scourged from city to city, and from synagogue to synagogue, and but D&C 95:5 (5–6); 121:34 (34–40).few shall stand to receive an inheritance.
I, the Lord, am Deut. 32:21; 2 Ne. 15:25; Moses 6:27. TG God, Indignation of.angry with the Isa. 57:17.wicked; I am holding my TG God, Spirit of.Spirit from the inhabitants of the earth.

I have sworn in my wrath, and D&C 45:26 (26, 33); 87:2; 88:91. TG War.decreed wars upon the face of the earth, and the wicked shall Ps. 139:19 (17–24); Isa. 11:4 (3–4).slay the wicked, and fear shall come upon every man;

And the TG Saints.saints also shall Matt. 24:22; Luke 21:36.hardly TG Protection, Divine.escape; nevertheless, I, the Lord, am with them, and will TG Jesus Christ, Second Coming.come down in heaven from the presence of my Father and Jer. 8:13; Matt. 3:12; 2 Ne. 26:6; D&C 29:9 (9, 21, 23); 45:57; 64:24; 88:94; 101:24 (23–25, 66). TG Earth, Cleansing of.consume the wicked with unquenchable fire.
And behold, this is not yet, but JS—H 1:41.by and by.
Wherefore, seeing that I, the Lord, have decreed all these things upon the face of the earth, I will that my saints should be assembled upon the land of Zion;

D&C 19:37; 58:47; 68:8; 71:7.And that every man should take TG Righteousness.righteousness in his hands and Isa. 11:5.faithfulness upon his loins, and lift a warning D&C 1:4.voice unto the inhabitants of the earth; and declare both by word and by flight that Isa. 47:11. TG Judgment.desolation shall come upon the wicked.
Wherefore, let my disciples in Kirtland arrange their temporal concerns, who dwell upon this farm.

Let my servant Titus Billings, who has the care thereof, dispose of the land, that he may be prepared in the coming spring to take his journey up unto the land of Zion, with those that dwell upon the face thereof, excepting those whom I shall reserve unto myself, that shall D&C 66:6.not go until I shall command them.
And let all the moneys which can be spared, it mattereth not unto me whether it be little or much, be sent up unto the land of Zion, unto them whom I have appointed to D&C 51:8 (3–9).receive.

Behold, I, the Lord, will give unto my servant Joseph Smith, Jun., power that he shall be enabled to TG Discernment, Spiritual.discern by the Spirit those who shall go up unto the land of Zion, and those of my disciples who shall tarry.
Let my servant D&C 64:26.Newel K. Whitney retain his D&C 57:8.store, or in other words, the store, yet for a little season.
Nevertheless, let him impart all the money which he can impart, to be sent up unto the land of Zion.
Behold, these things are in his own hands, let him do TG Agency; Initiative.according to wisdom.
Verily I say, let him be ordained as an agent unto the disciples that shall tarry, and let him be TG Delegation of Responsibility; Stewardship.ordained unto this power;
And now speedily visit the churches, expounding these things unto them, with my servant Oliver Cowdery. Behold, this is my will, obtaining moneys even as I have directed.
He that is Ps. 31:23; Mosiah 2:41; Ether 4:19; D&C 6:13.faithful and TG Perseverance.endureth shall overcome the world.

He that sendeth up treasures unto the land of Zion shall receive an D&C 25:2; 64:30; 85:7 (1–3, 7, 9); 99:7; 101:18 (1, 6, 18); 103:14 (11, 14).inheritance in this world, and his works shall follow him, and also a TG Reward.reward in the world to come.

Yea, and blessed are the dead that Rom. 14:8 (5–9); Rev. 14:13; D&C 42:44 (44–47).die in the Lord, from henceforth, when the Lord shall come, and old things shall 2 Cor. 5:17.pass away, and all things become new, they shall TG Resurrection.rise from the dead and shall not John 5:24; 8:51; 11:26; Rev. 21:4; Alma 11:45; 12:18 (18, 20); D&C 88:116. TG Immortality.die after, and shall receive an inheritance before the Lord, in the Jer. 31:40 (38–40).holy city.
And he that liveth when the Lord shall come, and hath kept the faith, TG Blessing.blessed is he; nevertheless, it is appointed to him to TG Death.die at the age of man.

Wherefore, TG Children; Millennium.children shall Isa. 65:20 (20–22); D&C 45:58; 101:30 (29–31). TG Mortality; Old Age.grow up until they become old; old men shall die; but they shall not sleep in the dust, but they shall be 1 Cor. 15:52 (51–52); D&C 43:32; 88:28 (20, 28).changed in the twinkling of an eye.
Wherefore, for this cause preached the apostles unto the world the resurrection of the dead.

These things are the things that ye must look for; and, speaking after the manner of the Lord, they are now D&C 35:15.nigh at hand, and in a time to come, even in the day of the coming of the Son of Man.

And until that hour there will be foolish Matt. 25:1; D&C 45:56 (56–59).virgins among the wise; and at that hour cometh an entire TG Separation.separation of the righteous and the wicked; and in that day will I send mine angels to Ps. 52:5; Matt. 13:40 (30, 39–41); Mosiah 16:2.pluck out the wicked and cast them into unquenchable fire.

And now behold, verily I say unto you, I, the Lord, am not pleased with my servant Lev. 4:22.Sidney Rigdon; he TG Haughtiness; Pride.exalted himself in his heart, and received not counsel, but TG Holy Ghost, Loss of.grieved the Spirit;

Wherefore his D&C 58:50 (50–51).writing is not acceptable unto the Lord, and he shall make another; and if the Lord receive it not, behold he standeth no longer in the office to which I have appointed him.
And again, verily I say unto you, D&C 4:3 (3–6); 36:5.those who desire in their hearts, in meekness, to D&C 18:14. TG Missionary Work.warn sinners to repentance, let them be ordained unto this power.
For this is a day of TG Warn.warning, and not a day of many words. For I, the Lord, am not to be TG Mocking.mocked in the last days.

Behold, I am from above, and my power lieth beneath. I am over all, and in all, and through all, and 1 Cor. 2:10.search all things, and the day cometh that all things shall be D&C 50:27.subject unto me.
Behold, I am D&C 35:1.Alpha and Omega, even Jesus Christ.
Wherefore, let all men TG Self-Mastery.beware how they take my Ezek. 43:8 (8–11). TG Jesus Christ, Taking the Name of; Profanity.name in their lips—
For behold, verily I say, that many there be who are under this condemnation, who use the name of the Lord, and use it in vain, having not TG Authority.authority.
Wherefore, let the church repent of their sins, and I, the Lord, will Rev. 3:5.own them; otherwise they shall be cut off.

Remember that that which cometh from above is TG Sacred.sacred, and must be TG Reverence.spoken with care, and by constraint of the Spirit; and in this there is no condemnation, and ye receive the Spirit D&C 42:14.through prayer; wherefore, without this there remaineth condemnation.
Let my servants, Joseph Smith, Jun., and Sidney Rigdon, seek them a home, as they are TG God, Spirit of; Prayer; Teaching.taught through prayer by the Spirit.
These things remain to overcome through patience, that such may receive a more exceeding and eternal 2 Cor. 4:17.weight of Rom. 8:18; D&C 58:4; 136:31.glory, otherwise, a greater condemnation. Amen.

div.p: Revelation given through Joseph Smith the Prophet to the elders of the Church, at Kirtland, Ohio, September 11, 1831. The Prophet was preparing to move to Hiram, Ohio, to renew his work on the translation of the Bible, which had been laid aside while he had been in Missouri. A company of brethren who had been commanded to journey to Zion (Missouri) was earnestly engaged in making preparations to leave in October. At this busy time, the revelation was received.

1–11, The Saints are commanded to forgive one another, lest there remain in them the greater sin; 12–22, The unrepentant are to be brought before the Church; 23–25, He that is tithed will not be burned at the Lord’s coming; 26–32, The Saints are warned against debt; 33–36, The rebellious will be cut off out of Zion; 37–40, The Church will judge the nations; 41–43, Zion will flourish.

chapter.verse: Behold, thus saith the Lord your God unto you, O ye elders of my TG Jesus Christ, Head of the Church.church, hearken ye and hear, and receive my will concerning you.
For verily I say unto you, I will that ye should 1 Jn. 5:4.overcome the world; wherefore I will have Micah 7:19. TG Compassion.compassion upon you.
There are those among you who have sinned; but verily I say, for this once, for mine own Moses 1:39.glory, and for the salvation of souls, I have Isa. 43:25; Luke 5:21.forgiven you your sins.
I will be merciful unto you, for I have given unto you the D&C 62:9; 82:24.kingdom.

And the D&C 28:7; 84:19. TG Priesthood, Keys of.keys of the mysteries of the kingdom shall not be taken from my servant Joseph Smith, Jun., through the means I have appointed, while he liveth, inasmuch as he obeyeth mine TG Ordinance.ordinances.
There are those who have sought occasion against him without cause;

Nevertheless, he has sinned; but verily I say unto you, I, the Lord, Dan. 9:9. TG Remission of Sins.forgive sins unto those who Num. 5:7 (6–10); D&C 19:20; 58:43. TG Confession.confess their sins before me and ask forgiveness, who have not 1 Jn. 3:15; 5:17; Alma 5:42 (41–42).sinned unto D&C 76:33 (31–37).death.

My disciples, in days of old, sought Dan. 6:4. TG Contention; Disputations.occasion against one another and forgave not one another in their hearts; and for this TG Evil.evil they were TG Affliction.afflicted and sorely TG Chastening.chastened.

Wherefore, I say unto you, that ye ought to Prov. 17:9; Mark 11:26 (25–26); D&C 82:1. TG Reconciliation.forgive one another; for he that Matt. 6:15 (14–15); Eph. 4:32.forgiveth not his brother his trespasses standeth condemned before the Lord; for there remaineth in him the greater sin.
I, the Lord, will Ex. 33:19; 34:7; Rom. 9:18; Alma 39:6; D&C 56:14. TG Forgive.forgive whom I will forgive, but of you it is required to Mosiah 26:31 (29–31). TG Benevolence; Forbear.forgive all men.
And ye ought to say in your hearts—let God 1 Sam. 24:12; Ps. 75:7. TG Judgment.judge between me and thee, and TG Reward.reward thee according to thy 2 Tim. 4:14.deeds.

And him that TG Repent.repenteth not of his sins, and TG Confession.confesseth them not, ye shall bring before the 1 Cor. 6:1.church, and do with him as the D&C 42:81 (80–93).scripture saith unto you, either by commandment or by revelation.

And this ye shall do that God may be glorified—not because ye forgive not, having not compassion, but that ye may be justified in the eyes of the law, that ye may not TG Offense.offend him who is your lawgiver—
Verily I say, for this cause ye shall do these things.
Behold, I, the Lord, was angry with him who was my servant Ezra Booth, and also my servant Isaac Morley, for they D&C 42:32 (32–35).kept not the law, neither the commandment;

They sought Prov. 1:16; 2 Ne. 19:17. TG Evil.evil in their hearts, and I, the Lord, TG Holy Ghost, Loss of.withheld my Spirit. They 2 Ne. 15:20; D&C 121:16.condemned for evil that thing in which there was no evil; nevertheless I have forgiven my servant Isaac Morley.

And also my servant D&C 60:10; 115:2 (2–6).Edward Partridge, behold, he hath sinned, and TG Devil.Satan seeketh to destroy his soul; but when these things are made known unto them, and they repent of the evil, they shall be forgiven.
And now, verily I say that it is expedient in me that my servant Sidney Gilbert, after a few weeks, shall return upon his business, and to his D&C 57:6.agency in the land of Zion;
And that which he hath seen and heard may be made known unto my disciples, that they perish not. And for this cause have I spoken these things.

And again, I say unto you, that my servant Isaac Morley may not be TG Temptation.tempted above that which he is able to bear, and counsel wrongfully to your hurt, I gave commandment that his farm should be sold.

I will not that my servant Frederick G. Williams should sell his farm, for I, the Lord, will to retain a strong hold in the land of Kirtland, for the space of five years, in the which I will not overthrow the wicked, that thereby I may save some.

And after that day, I, the Lord, will not hold any TG Guilt.guilty that shall go with an open heart up to the land of Zion; for I, the Lord, require the Ex. 35:5; D&C 59:15; 64:34.hearts of the children of men.

Behold, now it is called John 9:4; D&C 45:6; 64:25 (24–25).today until the TG Jesus Christ, Second Coming.coming of the Son of Man, and verily it is a day of TG Sacrifice.sacrifice, and a day for the tithing of my people; for he that is Mal. 3:10 (10–11). See note on tithing in heading to section 119.tithed shall not be Isa. 9:5 (5, 18–19); Mal. 4:1; 3 Ne. 25:1; JS—H 1:37.burned at his coming.

For after today cometh the Isa. 66:15 (15–16); Joel 2:5; 2 Ne. 15:24; 26:6 (4, 6). TG Earth, Cleansing of; World, End of.burning—this is speaking after the manner of the Lord—for verily I say, tomorrow all the Job 40:11; Prov. 15:25; Mal. 3:15; 2 Ne. 12:12; 23:11. TG Pride.proud and they that do wickedly shall be as Rev. 18:8 (6–8).stubble; and I will burn them up, for I am the Lord of Hosts; and I will not Gen. 18:23; 1 Ne. 22:16; Hel. 13:13 (12–14).spare any that remain in D&C 1:16. TG Babylon.Babylon.
Wherefore, if ye believe me, ye will labor while it is called John 9:4; D&C 45:6; 64:23. TG Procrastination.today.

And it is not meet that my servants, D&C 63:42.Newel K. Whitney and Sidney Gilbert, should sell their D&C 57:8.store and their possessions here; for this is not wisdom until the residue of the church, which remaineth in this place, shall go up unto the land of Zion.
Behold, it is said in my laws, or forbidden, to get in TG Debt.debt to thine enemies;
But behold, it is not said at any time that the Lord should not take when he please, and pay as seemeth him good.
Wherefore, as ye are agents, ye are on the Lord’s errand; and whatever ye do according to the will of the Lord is the Lord’s business.
And he hath set you to provide for his saints in these last days, that they may obtain an D&C 63:48 (29, 31, 48); 85:7 (1–3, 7, 9).inheritance in the land of Zion.
And behold, I, the Lord, declare unto you, and my Ps. 19:4; Mark 13:31; 2 Ne. 31:15.words are sure and shall not 1 Ne. 20:14; D&C 76:3.fail, that they shall obtain it.
But all things must come to pass in their time.

Wherefore, be not Gal. 6:9. TG Dedication; Laziness.weary in TG Benevolence; Good Works; Self-Sacrifice.well-doing, for ye are laying the foundation of a great work. And out of D&C 123:16 (16–17).small things proceedeth that which is great.

Behold, the Lord Micah 6:8.requireth the Deut. 32:46; Josh. 22:5; 1 Kgs. 2:4; Morm. 9:27. TG Heart.heart and a Isa. 1:19; 2 Cor. 8:12; Moro. 7:8; D&C 64:22; 97:8.willing mind; and the willing and TG Loyalty; Obedience.obedient shall D&C 101:101. TG Abundant Life.eat the good of the land of Zion in these last days.

And the TG Rebellion.rebellious shall be Gen. 6:13; Obad. 1:9; D&C 41:5; 42:37; 50:8 (8–9); 56:3; Moses 8:30 (26, 30). TG Excommunication.cut off out of the land of Zion, and shall be sent away, and shall not inherit the land.
For, verily I say that the rebellious are not of the blood of Gen. 48:16; 49:26 (22–26); Deut. 33:17 (16–17); Hosea 7:1 (1–16); Zech. 10:7 (7–12).Ephraim, wherefore they shall be plucked out.
Behold, I, the Lord, have made my church in these last days like unto a Obad. 1:21.judge sitting on a hill, or in a high place, to TG Judgment.judge the nations.
For it shall come to pass that the inhabitants of Zion shall Isa. 2:3; Joel 3:16; D&C 133:21.judge all things pertaining to Zion.
And TG Honesty; Lying.liars and hypocrites shall be proved by them, and they who are TG False Prophets.not Rev. 2:2. TG Apostles.apostles and prophets shall be Deut. 18:21 (21–22).known.

And even the TG Bishop.bishop, who is a D&C 58:17; 107:72 (72–74).judge, and his counselors, if they are not faithful in their TG Stewardship.stewardships shall be condemned, and D&C 35:18 (17–18); 42:10; 104:77; 107:99 (99–100).others shall be planted in their D&C 93:50 (47–50).stead.
For, behold, I say unto you that TG Zion.Zion shall flourish, and the D&C 45:67; 84:5 (4–5, 31); 97:15 (15–20). TG Glory.glory of the Lord shall be upon her;
And she shall be an TG Ensign; Mission of Latter-day Saints.ensign unto the people, and there shall come unto her out of every 1 Kgs. 8:41; Isa. 60:9.nation under heaven.
And the day shall come when the nations of the earth shall Isa. 60:14; D&C 45:67; 97:19 (19–20).tremble because of her, and shall fear because of her terrible ones. The Lord hath spoken it. Amen.

div.p: Revelation on prayer given through Joseph Smith the Prophet, at Hiram, Ohio, October 30, 1831.
1–2, The keys of the kingdom of God are committed to man on earth, and the gospel cause will triumph; 3–6, The millennial kingdom of heaven will come and join the kingdom of God on earth.

chapter.verse: Hearken, and lo, a voice as of one sent down from on high, who is mighty and powerful, whose going forth is unto the ends of the earth, yea, whose voice is unto men—Isa. 40:3; Matt. 3:3; John 1:23. TG Millennium, Preparing a People for.Prepare ye the way of the Lord, make his paths straight.

The Matt. 16:19; D&C 42:69. TG Priesthood, Keys of; Priesthood, Melchizedek; Restoration of the Gospel.keys of the Micah 4:7; Luke 17:21 (20–21); D&C 90:3 (1–5).kingdom of God are committed unto man on the earth, and from thence shall the TG Mission of Latter-day Saints.gospel roll forth unto the ends of the earth, as the Dan. 2:45 (34–45); 8:25.stone which is cut out of the mountain without hands shall roll forth, until it has Num. 14:21; Ps. 72:19. TG Last Days.filled the whole earth.
Yea, a voice crying—Matt. 3:3; D&C 88:66.Prepare ye the way of the Lord, prepare ye the Matt. 22:2 (1–14); Rev. 19:9; D&C 58:11.supper of the Lamb, make ready for the Mark 2:19 (19–20).Bridegroom.
Pray unto the Lord, Gen. 4:26; 1 Chr. 16:8; Ps. 116:17.call upon his holy name, make known his wonderful 1 Chr. 16:24. TG God, Works of.works among the people.

Call upon the Lord, that his kingdom may go forth upon the earth, that the inhabitants thereof may receive it, and be prepared for the days to come, in the which the Son of Man shall Matt. 16:27; 24:30.come down in heaven, Ps. 93:1.clothed in the brightness of his TG Glory.glory, to meet the Dan. 2:44.kingdom of God which is set up on the earth.

Wherefore, may the TG Kingdom of God, in Heaven; Kingdom of God, on Earth.kingdom of God go forth, that the Rev. 11:15.kingdom of heaven may come, that thou, O God, mayest be John 17:4.glorified in heaven so on earth, that thine 1 Chr. 17:10; Ps. 89:10; Micah 4:10. TG Enemies.enemies may be subdued; for 1 Chr. 29:11; Matt. 6:13; Rev. 11:15.thine is the honor, power and glory, forever and ever. Amen.

div.p: Revelation given through Joseph Smith the Prophet, at Hiram, Ohio, October 29, 1831. William E. McLellin had petitioned the Lord in secret to make known through the Prophet the answer to five questions, which were unknown to Joseph Smith. At McLellin’s request, the Prophet inquired of the Lord and received this revelation.
1–4, The everlasting covenant is the fulness of the gospel; 5–8, Elders are to preach, testify, and reason with the people; 9–13, Faithful ministerial service ensures an inheritance of eternal life.

chapter.verse: Behold, thus saith the Lord unto my servant D&C 68:7; 75:6; 90:35.William E. McLellin—Blessed are you, inasmuch as you have Mal. 2:6.turned away from your iniquities, and have received my truths, saith the Lord your Redeemer, the John 1:12; 4:42; 1 Jn. 4:14.Savior of the world, even of as many as believe on my name.

TG Glory.glories which are to be revealed in the last days, as it was written by the prophets and apostles in days of old.

And now, verily, I, the Lord, will show unto you what I TG God, Will of; Guidance, Divine.will concerning you, or what is my will concerning you.

Behold, verily I say unto you, that it is my will that you should Mark 16:15.proclaim my gospel from land to land, and from Luke 8:1; Alma 23:4; D&C 75:18.city to city, yea, in those regions round about where it has not been proclaimed.
Tarry not many days in this place; go D&C 63:39 (24–39).not up unto the land of Zion as yet; but inasmuch as you can D&C 63:40 (40–46).send, send; otherwise, think not of thy property.
