Let my servant Samuel H. Smith go with you, and forsake him not, and give him thine instructions; and he that is Ps. 31:23.faithful shall be made D&C 52:17; 133:58.strong in every place; and I, the Lord, will go with you.

Lay your TG Administrations to the Sick; Hands, Laying on of.hands upon the TG Sickness.sick, and they shall Matt. 9:18.recover. Return not till I, the Lord, shall send you. Be patient in affliction. John 16:24.Ask, and ye shall receive; knock, and it shall be opened unto you.
Seek not to be Luke 10:40.cumbered. Forsake all Prov. 9:6.unrighteousness. Commit not Mark 10:19. TG Adulterer.adultery—a temptation with which thou hast been troubled.

D&C 35:24; 103:7.Keep these sayings, for they are true and 2 Ne. 31:15.faithful; and thou shalt Rom. 11:13.magnify thine office, and push many people to D&C 11:6.Zion with Isa. 35:10; D&C 45:71. TG Singing.songs of everlasting joy upon their heads.

2 Tim. 3:14.Continue in these things even unto the end, and you shall have a Isa. 62:3; Matt. 25:21; 1 Pet. 5:4; Rev. 2:10.crown of eternal life at the right hand of my Father, who is full of John 1:14.grace and truth.
Verily, thus saith the Lord your Isa. 43:12 (11–14); 44:6.God, your Redeemer, even Jesus Christ. Amen.

div.p: Revelation given through Joseph Smith the Prophet, at Hiram, Ohio, early November 1831. The occasion was that of a special conference, and the publication of the revelations already received from the Lord through the Prophet was considered and acted upon (see the heading to section 1). William W. Phelps had recently established the Church printing press in Independence, Missouri. The conference decided to publish the revelations in the Book of Commandments and to print 10,000 copies (which because of unforeseen difficulties was later reduced to 3,000 copies). Many of the brethren bore solemn testimony that the revelations then compiled for publication were verily true, as was witnessed by the Holy Ghost shed forth upon them. Joseph Smith’s history records that after the revelation known as section 1 had been received, some conversation was had concerning the language used in the revelations. The present revelation followed.

1–3, The Lord hears the prayers of and watches over His elders; 4–9, He challenges the wisest person to duplicate the least of His revelations; 10–14, Faithful elders will be quickened by the Spirit and see the face of God.

chapter.verse: Behold and hearken, O ye TG Elder, Melchizedek Priesthood.elders of my church, who have assembled yourselves together, whose 1 Kgs. 9:3.prayers I have heard, and whose Acts 1:24. TG God, Omniscience of.hearts I know, and whose desires have come up before me.
Behold and lo, mine Ps. 34:15; Amos 9:8.eyes are upon you, and the heavens and the earth are in mine Ps. 112:8 (7–8); Heb. 1:10. TG God, Works of.hands, and the riches of eternity are mine to give.

Ye endeavored to TG Faith.believe that ye should receive the blessing which was offered unto you; but behold, verily I say unto you there were TG Fearful.fears in your hearts, and verily this is the reason that ye did not receive.
And now I, the Lord, give unto you a TG Testimony; Truth.testimony of the truth of these commandments which are lying before you.

Your eyes have been upon my servant Joseph Smith, Jun., and his D&C 1:24 (18–24).language you have known, and his imperfections you have known; and you have sought in your hearts knowledge that you might express beyond his language; this you also know.
Now, seek ye out of the Book of Commandments, even the least that is among them, and appoint him that is the most 2 Ne. 9:29 (28–29, 42).wise among you;
Or, if there be any among you that shall make one Prov. 30:5 (5–6).like unto it, then ye are justified in saying that ye do not know that they are true;
But if ye cannot make one like unto it, ye are under condemnation if ye do not TG Testimony; Witness.bear record that they are true.

For ye know that there is no unrighteousness in them, and that which is Ps. 119:138; Isa. 45:19; James 1:17; Moro. 7:16 (15–18).righteous cometh down from above, from the Father of D&C 50:24; 84:45; 88:49.lights.

And again, verily I say unto you that it is your privilege, and a TG Promise.promise I give unto you that have been ordained unto this ministry, that inasmuch as you TG Humility; Purity.strip yourselves from TG Jealous.jealousies and TG Courage; Fearful.fears, and Prov. 6:3. TG Teachable.humble yourselves before me, for ye are not sufficiently humble, the TG Veil.veil shall be rent and you shall Lev. 9:4; D&C 50:45; 88:68; 93:1; 97:16; Moses 1:11.see me and know that I am—not with the carnal neither natural mind, but with the spiritual.

For no Ex. 19:21; 33:20; JST Ex. 33:20 (Bible Appendix); John 1:18 (JST 1 Jn. 4:12 No man hath seen God at any time, except them who believe …); D&C 84:22; Moses 1:11 (11, 14). TG God, Privilege of Seeing.man has seen God at any time in the flesh, except quickened by the Spirit of God.
Neither can any JST Ex. 33:20 (Bible Appendix); Mosiah 3:19. TG Man, Natural, Not Spiritually Reborn.natural man abide the presence of God, neither after the carnal mind.
Ye are not able to abide the presence of God now, neither the ministering of angels; wherefore, Rom. 2:7. TG Patience.continue in patience until ye are Matt. 5:48.perfected.

Let not your minds D&C 133:15 (14–15).turn back; and when ye are TG Worthiness.worthy, in mine own due time, ye shall see and know that which was conferred upon you by the hands of my servant Joseph Smith, Jun. Amen.

div.p: Revelation given through Joseph Smith the Prophet, at Hiram, Ohio, November 1, 1831, in response to prayer that the mind of the Lord be made known concerning Orson Hyde, Luke S. Johnson, Lyman E. Johnson, and William E. McLellin. Although part of this revelation was directed toward these four men, much of the content pertains to the whole Church. This revelation was expanded under Joseph Smith’s direction when it was published in the 1835 edition of the Doctrine and Covenants.

1–5, The words of the elders when moved upon by the Holy Ghost are scripture; 6–12, Elders are to preach and baptize, and signs will follow true believers; 13–24, The firstborn among the sons of Aaron may serve as the Presiding Bishop (that is, hold the keys of presidency as a bishop) under the direction of the First Presidency; 25–28, Parents are commanded to teach the gospel to their children; 29–35, The Saints are to observe the Sabbath, labor diligently, and pray.

chapter.verse: My servant, Orson Hyde, was called by his ordination to proclaim the D&C 18:4.everlasting gospel, by the TG God, Spirit of.Spirit of the living God, from people to people, and from land to land, in the Ps. 26:5; D&C 61:30.congregations of the wicked, in their D&C 66:7.synagogues, reasoning with and Acts 28:23.expounding all scriptures unto them.
And, behold, and lo, this is an ensample unto all those who were ordained unto this priesthood, whose mission is appointed unto them to go forth—
And this is the TG Example.ensample unto them, that they shall Ex. 4:12 (12–16); 2 Pet. 1:21; D&C 18:32; 34:10; 42:16; 100:5.speak as they are moved upon by the Holy Ghost.

And whatsoever they shall speak when moved upon by the Acts 4:31. TG Revelation.Holy Ghost shall be scripture, shall be the will of the Lord, shall be the mind of the Lord, shall be the word of the Lord, shall be the voice of the Lord, and the Rom. 1:16.power of God unto salvation.
Behold, this is the promise of the Lord unto you, O ye my servants.

Wherefore, be of good Matt. 9:2.cheer, and do not Gen. 26:24; Isa. 41:10; Dan. 10:12; Philip. 1:14 (12–17); D&C 98:1; JS—H 1:32.fear, for I the Lord am with you, and will stand by you; and ye shall bear record of me, even Jesus Christ, that I am the Son of the living God, that I Rev. 1:4.was, that I am, and that I am to come.

This is the word of the Lord unto you, my servant Orson Hyde, and also unto my servant Luke Johnson, and unto my servant Lyman Johnson, and unto my servant D&C 66:1; 75:6; 90:35.William E. McLellin, and unto all the faithful elders of my church—

D&C 1:2; 19:37; 58:47; 63:37; 71:7.Go ye into all the world, TG Preaching.preach the gospel to every Mark 16:15.creature, acting in the TG Authority; Priesthood, Authority.authority which I have given you, TG Baptism.baptizing in the name of the Father, and of the Son, and of the Holy Ghost.
And Mark 16:16; D&C 20:25.he that believeth and is baptized shall be saved, and he that believeth not shall be TG Damnation.damned.
And he that believeth shall be blest with TG Signs.signs following, even as it is written.
And unto you it shall be given to know the signs of the Acts 1:7; D&C 121:12 (12, 27, 31).times, and the D&C 1:12.signs of the coming of the Son of Man;
And of as many as the Father shall bear record, to you shall be given power to D&C 1:8; 132:19, 49. TG Sealing.seal them up unto eternal life. Amen.
And now, concerning the items in addition to the D&C 1:6. See also D&C “Introduction.”covenants and commandments, they are these—
There remain hereafter, in the due time of the Lord, other D&C 41:9. TG Bishop.bishops to be set apart unto the TG Church Organization.church, to minister even according to the first;

Wherefore they shall be D&C 72:1.high priests who are worthy, and they shall be appointed by the D&C 81:2; 107:17 (9, 17, 22).First Presidency of the Melchizedek Priesthood, except they be literal descendants of Lev. 1:7; D&C 84:30; 132:59.Aaron.
And if they be literal descendants of D&C 107:16 (15–17, 68–69). TG Priesthood, Aaronic.Aaron they have a legal right to the bishopric, if they are the TG Firstborn.firstborn among the sons of Aaron;
For the firstborn holds the right of the presidency over this priesthood, and the TG Priesthood, Keys of.keys or authority of the same.

No man has a legal right to this office, to hold the keys of this priesthood, except he be a Ex. 40:15 (12–15); D&C 84:18 (18, 30); 107:16 (13–16, 70–76).literal descendant and the firstborn of Aaron.

But, as a TG High Priest, Melchizedek Priesthood.high priest of the Melchizedek Priesthood has authority to officiate in all the lesser offices he may officiate in the office of TG Bishop.bishop when no literal descendant of Aaron can be found, provided he is called and set apart and ordained unto this power, under the hands of the First Presidency of the Melchizedek Priesthood.

And a literal descendant of Aaron, also, must be designated by this Presidency, and found worthy, and D&C 109:35. TG Anointing; Priesthood, Ordination.anointed, and ordained under the hands of this Presidency, otherwise they are not legally authorized to officiate in their priesthood.

But, by virtue of the decree concerning their right of the priesthood descending from father to son, they may claim their Num. 18:8.anointing if at any time they can prove their lineage, or do ascertain it by revelation from the Lord under the TG Hands, Laying on of.hands of the above named Presidency.

And inasmuch as he is found TG Guilt.guilty before this Presidency, by testimony that cannot be impeached, he shall be condemned;
And if he repent he shall be TG Forgive.forgiven, according to the covenants and D&C 42:13 (13–29).commandments of the church.

TG Family, Children, Responsibilities toward.teach them not to understand the Heb. 6:2.doctrine of repentance, faith in Christ the Son of the living God, and of baptism and the gift of the Holy Ghost by the laying on of the hands, when D&C 18:42; 20:71.eight years old, the Ezek. 33:4 (2–8); Jacob 1:19; D&C 29:48.sin be upon the heads of the parents.

And they shall also Prov. 13:24. TG Learn; Prayer; Teaching.teach their children to pray, and to walk uprightly before the Lord.

TG Children.children are also growing up in TG Wickedness.wickedness; they also Matt. 6:33 (25–34); Alma 39:14 (12–14); D&C 6:7 (6–7). These things ought not to be, and must be done away from among them; wherefore, let my servant Oliver Cowdery D&C 69:1.carry these sayings unto the land of Zion.

div.p: Revelation given through Joseph Smith the Prophet, at Hiram, Ohio, November 11, 1831. The compilation of revelations intended for early publication had been passed upon at the special conference of November 1–2. On November 3, the revelation herein appearing as section 133, later called the Appendix, was added. Oliver Cowdery had previously been appointed to carry the manuscript of the compiled revelations and commandments to Independence, Missouri, for printing. He was also to take with him money that had been contributed for the building up of the Church in Missouri. This revelation instructs John Whitmer to accompany Oliver Cowdery and also directs Whitmer to travel and collect historical material in his calling as Church historian and recorder.
1–2, John Whitmer is to accompany Oliver Cowdery to Missouri; 3–8, He is also to preach and to collect, record, and write historical data.

chapter.verse: Hearken unto me, saith the Lord your God, for my servant Oliver Cowdery’s sake. It is not wisdom in me that he should be entrusted with the commandments and the moneys which he shall D&C 68:32.carry unto the land of Zion, except one go with him who will be TG Dependability; Trustworthiness.true and faithful.
Wherefore, I, the Lord, will that my servant, John Whitmer, should go with my servant Oliver Cowdery;
And also that he shall continue in TG Record Keeping.writing and making a D&C 21:1; 47:1 (1–3); 85:1.history of all the important things which he shall observe and know concerning my church;
And also that he receive Prov. 20:18. TG Counsel.counsel and assistance from my servant Oliver Cowdery and others.
And also, my servants who are abroad in the earth should send forth the accounts of their TG Stewardship.stewardships to the land of Zion;
For the land of Zion shall be a D&C 57:3.seat and a place to receive and do all these things.
Nevertheless, let my servant John Whitmer travel many times from place to place, and from church to church, that he may the more easily obtain knowledge—

Preaching and expounding, writing, copying, selecting, and obtaining all things which shall be for the good of the church, and for the rising generations that shall grow up on the land of Zion, to TG Zion.possess it from generation to generation, forever and ever. Amen.

div.p: Revelation given through Joseph Smith the Prophet, at Hiram, Ohio, November 12, 1831. The Prophet’s history states that four special conferences were held from the 1st to the 12th of November, inclusive. In the last of these assemblies, the great importance of the revelations that would later be published as the Book of Commandments and then the Doctrine and Covenants was considered. This revelation was given after the conference voted that the revelations were “worth to the Church the riches of the whole Earth.” Joseph Smith’s history refers to the revelations as “the foundation of the Church in these last days, and a benefit to the world, showing that the keys of the mysteries of the kingdom of our Savior are again entrusted to man.”
1–5, Stewards are appointed to publish the revelations; 6–13, Those who labor in spiritual things are worthy of their hire; 14–18, The Saints should be equal in temporal things.

chapter.verse: Behold, and hearken, O ye inhabitants of Zion, and all ye people of my church who are afar off, and Isa. 39:5.hear the word of the Lord which I give unto my servant Joseph Smith, Jun., and also unto my servant Martin Harris, and also unto my servant Oliver Cowdery, and also unto my servant John Whitmer, and also unto my servant Sidney Rigdon, and also unto my servant D&C 57:11; 58:40; 61:7 (7–9).William W. Phelps, by the way of commandment unto them.
For I give unto them a commandment; wherefore hearken and hear, for thus saith the Lord unto them—

I, the Lord, have appointed them, and ordained them to be 1 Cor. 4:1; D&C 72:20.stewards over the revelations and commandments which I have given unto them, and which I shall hereafter give unto them;
And an account of this TG Scriptures, Preservation of; Stewardship.stewardship will I require of them in the day of judgment.

Wherefore, I have appointed unto them, and this is their business in the church of God, to TG Accountability; Delegation of Responsibility.manage them and the concerns thereof, yea, the benefits thereof.
Wherefore, a commandment I give unto them, that they shall not give these things unto the church, neither unto the Moses 1:42; 4:32.world;
Nevertheless, inasmuch as they D&C 42:55.receive more than is needful for their necessities and their wants, it shall be given into my D&C 58:24 (24, 37); 72:10.storehouse;
And the D&C 72:21. TG Wages.benefits shall be consecrated unto the inhabitants of Zion, and unto their generations, inasmuch as they become D&C 38:20.heirs according to the laws of the kingdom.
Behold, this is what the Lord requires of every man in his TG Stewardship.stewardship, even as I, the Lord, have appointed or shall hereafter appoint unto any man.
And behold, none are exempt from this D&C 85:3.law who belong to the church of the living God;
Yea, neither the bishop, neither the D&C 57:6.agent who keepeth the Lord’s storehouse, neither he who is appointed in a stewardship over D&C 107:68 (68–71).temporal things.
He who is appointed to administer spiritual things, the same is Luke 10:7.worthy of his hire, even as those who are appointed to a stewardship to administer in temporal things;
Yea, even more abundantly, which abundance is multiplied unto them through the D&C 5:16.manifestations of the Spirit.

Nevertheless, in your temporal things you shall be D&C 49:20. TG Consecration; Selfishness.equal, and this not grudgingly, otherwise the abundance of the manifestations of the Spirit shall be TG Holy Ghost, Loss of.withheld.

Now, this commandment I give unto my servants for their Deut. 10:13.benefit while they remain, for a manifestation of my blessings upon their heads, and for a TG Reward.reward of their TG Dependability; Diligence.diligence and for their security;
For food and for D&C 59:17 (16–20).raiment; for an inheritance; for houses and for lands, in whatsoever circumstances I, the Lord, shall place them, and whithersoever I, the Lord, shall send them.
For they have been faithful over Matt. 25:21 (21–23).many things, and have done well inasmuch as they have not sinned.
Behold, I, the Lord, am Ps. 34:8; Lam. 3:25; Nahum 1:7.merciful and will bless them, and they shall enter into the joy of these things. Even so. Amen.

div.p: Revelation given to Joseph Smith the Prophet and Sidney Rigdon, at Hiram, Ohio, December 1, 1831. The Prophet had continued to translate the Bible with Sidney Rigdon as his scribe until this revelation was received, at which time it was temporarily laid aside so as to enable them to fulfill the instruction given herein. The brethren were to go forth to preach in order to allay the unfriendly feelings that had developed against the Church as a result of the publication of letters written by Ezra Booth, who had apostatized.
1–4, Joseph Smith and Sidney Rigdon are sent forth to proclaim the gospel; 5–11, Enemies of the Saints will be confounded.

chapter.verse: Behold, thus saith the Lord unto you my servants Joseph Smith, Jun., and Sidney Rigdon, that the time has verily come that it is necessary and expedient in me that you should open your mouths in Matt. 4:23. TG Missionary Work.proclaiming my gospel, the things of the kingdom, expounding the D&C 42:61 (61–65).mysteries thereof out of the scriptures, according to that portion of Spirit and power which shall be given unto you, even as I will.
Verily I say unto you, proclaim unto the world in the regions round about, and in the church also, for the space of a season, even until it shall be D&C 73:3.made known unto you.
Verily this is a mission for a season, which I give unto you.
Wherefore, D&C 24:19.labor ye in my vineyard. Call upon the inhabitants of the earth, and bear record, and prepare the way for the commandments and revelations which are to come.
Now, behold this is wisdom; whoso readeth, let him TG Understanding.understand and Alma 12:10 (9–11).receive also;
For unto him that receiveth it shall be given more Matt. 13:12.abundantly, even power.

Wherefore, Ps. 83:17 (2–17); 2 Ne. 25:14; Moses 7:15 (14–16).confound your TG Enemies.enemies; call upon them to D&C 19:37; 58:47; 63:37; 68:8.meet you both in public and in private; and inasmuch as ye are faithful their TG Shame.shame shall be made manifest.
Wherefore, let them bring forth their Isa. 41:21.strong reasons against the Lord.
Verily, thus saith the Lord unto you—there is no Isa. 54:17.weapon that is formed against you shall prosper;
And if any man lift his voice against you he shall be Jer. 17:18 (15–18).confounded in mine own due time.
Wherefore, Ex. 16:28.keep my commandments; they are true and faithful. Even so. Amen.

div.p: Revelation given through Joseph Smith the Prophet, at Kirtland, Ohio, December 4, 1831. Several elders and members had assembled to learn their duty and to be further edified in the teachings of the Church. This section is a compilation of three revelations received on the same day. Verses 1 through 8 make known the calling of Newel K. Whitney as a bishop. He was then called and ordained, after which verses 9 through 23 were received, giving additional information as to a bishop’s duties. Thereafter, verses 24 through 26 were given, providing instructions concerning the gathering to Zion.

1–8, Elders are to render an account of their stewardship unto the bishop; 9–15, The bishop keeps the storehouse and cares for the poor and needy; 16–26, Bishops are to certify the worthiness of elders.

chapter.verse: Hearken, and listen to the voice of the Lord, O ye who have assembled yourselves together, who are the D&C 68:15 (15–19).high priests of my church, to whom the D&C 35:27. TG Kingdom of God, on Earth.kingdom and power have been given.
For verily thus saith the Lord, it is expedient in me for a TG Bishop.bishop to be appointed unto you, or of you, unto the church in this part of the Lord’s vineyard.

And verily in this thing ye have done wisely, for it is required of the Lord, at the hand of every Ezek. 34:10; Matt. 25:19; D&C 51:19; 70:9 (9–11); 104:13 (11–13).steward, to render an D&C 42:32 (30–42).account of his Luke 19:15 (11–27). TG Stewardship.stewardship, both in time and in eternity.
For he who is faithful and Matt. 24:45.wise in time is accounted worthy to inherit the D&C 59:2.mansions prepared for him of my Father.

Verily I say unto you, the elders of the church in this part of my vineyard shall render an account of their stewardship unto the TG Bishop.bishop, who shall be appointed of me in this part of my vineyard.
These things shall be had on TG Record Keeping.record, to be handed over unto the D&C 41:9; 58:14 (14–24).bishop in Zion.
And the duty of the D&C 41:9; 42:31 (30–31); 46:27; 58:17 (17–18); 68:16; 107:88 (87–88).bishop shall be made known by the commandments which have been given, and the voice of the conference.
And now, verily I say unto you, my servant Newel K. Whitney is the man who shall be appointed and ordained unto this power. This is the will of the Lord your God, your Redeemer. Even so. Amen.

The word of the Lord, in addition to the TG Bishop.law which has been given, making known the TG Duty.duty of the D&C 58:14–24.bishop who has been ordained unto the church in this part of the vineyard, which is verily this—
To keep the Lord’s D&C 70:7 (7–11); 78:3. TG Welfare.storehouse; to receive the funds of the church in this part of the vineyard;
To take an account of the elders as before has been commanded; and to D&C 75:24 (24–26).administer to their wants, who shall pay for that which they receive, inasmuch as they have wherewith to pay;
That this also may be consecrated to the good of the church, to the poor and needy.
And he who D&C 58:14. TG Poor.hath not wherewith to pay, an account shall be taken and handed over to the bishop of Zion, who shall pay the debt out of that which the Lord shall put into his hands.

And the labors of the faithful who labor in spiritual things, in administering the gospel and the TG Almsgiving; Generosity.things of the kingdom unto the church, and unto the world, shall answer the debt unto the bishop in Zion;
Thus it cometh out of the church, for according to the D&C 42:30.law every man that cometh up to Zion must lay all things before the bishop in Zion.
And now, verily I say unto you, that as every elder in this part of the vineyard must give an account of his stewardship unto the bishop in this part of the vineyard—

A D&C 20:64 (64, 84); 52:41; 72:19 (18–26); 112:21.certificate from the judge or bishop in this part of the vineyard, unto the bishop in Zion, rendereth every man acceptable, and answereth all things, for an inheritance, and to be received as a wise D&C 42:32.steward and as a faithful TG Work, Value of.laborer;
Otherwise he shall not be D&C 72:26.accepted of the bishop of Zion.

And now, verily I say unto you, let every elder who shall give an account unto the bishop of the church in this part of the vineyard be D&C 20:64.recommended by the church or churches, in which he labors, that he may render himself and his accounts approved in all things.
And again, let my servants who are appointed as stewards over the D&C 70:3 (3–5).literary concerns of my church have claim for assistance upon the bishop or bishops in all things—
That the revelations may be D&C 67:6 (6–9).published, and go forth unto the ends of the earth; that they also may obtain D&C 70:8 (5–8).funds which shall benefit the church in all things;
That they also may render themselves approved in all things, and be accounted as D&C 42:32.wise stewards.
And now, behold, this shall be an D&C 51:18. TG Example.ensample for all the extensive branches of my church, in whatsoever land they shall be established. And now I make an end of my sayings. Amen.

A few words in addition to the laws of the kingdom, respecting the members of the church—they that are TG Holy Ghost, Mission of.appointed by the Holy Spirit to go up unto Zion, and they who are D&C 58:56.privileged to go up unto Zion—
Let them carry up unto the bishop a D&C 72:17 (17–18).certificate from three elders of the church, or a certificate from the bishop;
Otherwise he who shall go up unto the land of Zion shall not be accounted as a wise steward. This is also an ensample. Amen.

div.p: Revelation given to Joseph Smith the Prophet and Sidney Rigdon, at Hiram, Ohio, January 10, 1832. Since the early part of the preceding December, the Prophet and Sidney had been engaged in preaching, and by this means much was accomplished in diminishing the unfavorable feelings that had arisen against the Church (see the heading to section 71).
1–2, Elders are to continue to preach; 3–6, Joseph Smith and Sidney Rigdon are to continue to translate the Bible until it is finished.

chapter.verse: For verily, thus saith the Lord, it is expedient in me that IE the others who were on missions; see D&C 57–68.they should continue preaching the gospel, and in exhortation to the churches in the regions round about, until conference;
And then, behold, it shall be made known unto them, by the D&C 20:63.voice of the conference, their several missions.
Now, verily I say unto you my servants, Joseph Smith, Jun., and Sidney Rigdon, saith the Lord, it is D&C 71:2.expedient to IE the translation of the Bible. D&C 45:60 (60–61); 76:15.translate again;
And, inasmuch as it is practicable, to preach in the regions round about until conference; and after that it is expedient to continue the work of D&C 90:13.translation until it be finished.
And let this be a pattern unto the elders until further knowledge, even as it is written.
Now I give no more unto you at this time. 1 Pet. 1:13.Gird up your loins and be sober. Even so. Amen.

div.p: Revelation given to Joseph Smith the Prophet, at Wayne County, New York, in 1830. Even before the organization of the Church, questions had arisen about the proper mode of baptism, leading the Prophet to seek answers on the subject. Joseph Smith’s history states that this revelation is an explanation of 1 Corinthians 7:14, a scripture that had often been used to justify infant baptism.
1–5, Paul counsels the Church of his day not to keep the law of Moses; 6–7, Little children are holy and are sanctified through the Atonement.

chapter.verse: For the 1 Cor. 7:14 (14–19).unbelieving TG Marriage, Marry.husband is TG Family, Love within; Sanctification.sanctified by the wife, and the unbelieving wife is sanctified by the husband; else were your children unclean, but now are they holy.
Now, in the days of the apostles the law of circumcision was had among all the Jews who believed not the gospel of Jesus Christ.

And it came to pass that there arose a great Acts 15:1 (1–35); Gal. 2:3 (1–5); 5:6 (1–14).contention among the people concerning the law of TG Circumcision.circumcision, for the unbelieving husband was desirous that his children should be circumcised and become subject to the TG Law of Moses.law of Moses, which law was fulfilled.

And it came to pass that the children, being brought up in subjection to the law of Moses, gave heed to the TG Traditions of Men.traditions of their fathers and believed not the gospel of Christ, wherein they became unholy.

Wherefore, for this cause the apostle wrote unto the church, giving unto them a commandment, not of the Lord, but of himself, that a believer should not be TG Marriage, Interfaith.united to an TG Unbelief.unbeliever; except the Rom. 7:4 (4–6); 2 Ne. 25:25 (24–27).law of Moses should be done away among them,
That their children might remain without circumcision; and that the TG Traditions of Men.tradition might be done away, which saith that little children are unholy; for it was had among the Jews;

But little TG Children; Family.children are Moro. 8:8 (8–15); D&C 29:46. TG Holiness.holy, being TG Salvation of Little Children.sanctified through the TG Jesus Christ, Atonement through.atonement of Jesus Christ; and this is what the scriptures mean.

div.p: Revelation given through Joseph Smith the Prophet, at Amherst, Ohio, January 25, 1832. This section comprises two separate revelations (the first in verses 1 through 22 and the second in verses 23 through 36) given on the same day. The occasion was a conference at which Joseph Smith was sustained and ordained President of the High Priesthood. Certain elders desired to learn more about their immediate duties. These revelations followed.

1–5, Faithful elders who preach the gospel will gain eternal life; 6–12, Pray to receive the Comforter, who teaches all things; 13–22, Elders will sit in judgment on those who reject their message; 23–36, Families of missionaries are to receive help from the Church.

chapter.verse: Verily, verily, I say unto you, I who speak even by the TG Revelation.voice of my Spirit, even Rev. 1:8; D&C 68:35.Alpha and Omega, your Lord and your God—
Hearken, O ye who have D&C 75:23. TG Initiative; Zeal.given your names to go forth to proclaim my gospel, and to Jacob 5:62 (62–69).prune my vineyard.
Behold, I say unto you that it is my will that you should go forth and not tarry, neither be TG Idleness.idle but Neh. 4:6. TG Diligence; Industry.labor with your might—
Lifting up your voices as with the sound of a trump, TG Missionary Work.proclaiming the 2 Cor. 4:2; D&C 19:37.truth according to the revelations and commandments which I have given you.

And thus, if ye are faithful ye shall be laden with many Ps. 126:6; Alma 26:5; D&C 4:4; 33:9.sheaves, and TG Eternal Life; Exaltation; Glory; Honor.crowned with honor, and glory, and immortality, and eternal life.
Therefore, verily I say unto my servant D&C 68:7; 90:35.William E. McLellin, I D&C 66:7 (1–13).revoke the commission which I gave unto him to go unto the eastern countries;
And I give unto him a new commission and a new commandment, in the which I, the Lord, TG Chastening.chasten him for the Num. 11:1 (1–2); 1 Ne. 17:2.murmurings of his heart;
And he sinned; nevertheless, I forgive him and say unto him again, Go ye into the south countries.
And let my servant Luke Johnson go with him, and proclaim the things which I have commanded them—
Calling on the name of the Lord for the TG Holy Ghost, Comforter.Comforter, which shall teach them all things that are expedient for them—
2 Ne. 32:9.Praying always that they Luke 18:1.faint not; and inasmuch as they do this, I will be with them even unto the end.
Behold, this is the will of the Lord your God concerning you. Even so. Amen.

And again, verily thus saith the Lord, let my servant Orson Hyde and my servant Samuel H. Smith take their journey into the eastern countries, and proclaim the things which I have commanded them; and inasmuch as they are faithful, lo, I will be Matt. 28:20.with them even unto the end.

And again, verily I say unto my servant Lyman Johnson, and unto my servant Orson Pratt, they shall also take their journey into the eastern countries; and behold, and lo, I am with them also, even unto the end.

And again, I say unto my servant Asa Dodds, and unto my servant Calves Wilson, that they also shall take their journey unto the western countries, and proclaim my gospel, even as I have commanded them.
And he who is faithful shall overcome all things, and shall be D&C 5:35.lifted up at the last day.
And again, I say unto my servant Major N. Ashley, and my servant Burr Riggs, let them take their journey also into the south country.
Yea, let all those take their journey, as I have commanded them, going from Luke 8:1; Alma 23:4; D&C 66:5.house to house, and from village to village, and from city to city.
And in whatsoever house ye enter, and they receive you, leave your blessing upon that house.

And in whatsoever house ye enter, and they receive you not, ye shall depart speedily from that house, and Matt. 10:14 (12–14); Luke 10:11 (11–12); D&C 24:15; 60:15; 99:4 (4–5).shake off the dust of your feet as a testimony against them.
And you shall be filled with Matt. 5:12 (11–12).joy and gladness; and know this, that in the day of judgment you shall be TG Priesthood, Authority.judges of that house, and condemn them;

And it shall be more Matt. 11:22; D&C 45:54.tolerable for the TG Heathen.heathen in the day of judgment, than for that house; therefore, D&C 27:15 (15–18).gird up your loins and be faithful, and ye shall overcome all things, and be D&C 5:35.lifted up at the last day. Even so. Amen.
And again, thus saith the Lord unto you, O ye elders of my church, who have D&C 75:2.given your names that you might know his will concerning you—

Behold, I say unto you, that it is the D&C 72:11 (11–12).duty of the church to assist in D&C 31:5.supporting the families of those, and also to support the families of those who are called and must needs be sent unto the world to proclaim the gospel unto the world.
Wherefore, I, the Lord, give unto you this commandment, that ye obtain places for your D&C 83:2 (2–4).families, inasmuch as your brethren are willing to open their hearts.
And let all such as can obtain places for their families, and support of the church for them, not fail to go into the world, whether to the east or to the west, or to the north, or to the south.
Let them D&C 4:7.ask and they shall receive, knock and it shall be opened unto them, and be made known from on high, even by the TG Holy Ghost, Comforter.Comforter, whither they shall go.

And again, verily I say unto you, that every TG Marriage, Husbands.man who is obliged to 1 Tim. 5:8. TG Family, Managing Finances in; Marriage, Fatherhood.provide for his own TG Family; Family, Love within.family, let him provide, and he shall in nowise lose his crown; and let him labor in the church.
Let every man be TG Diligence; Zeal.diligent in all things. And the TG Idleness; Laziness.idler shall not have place in the church, except he repent and mend his ways.
Wherefore, let my servant Simeon Carter and my servant Emer Harris be united in the ministry;
And also my servant Ezra Thayre and my servant D&C 56:5 (5–6).Thomas B. Marsh;
Also my servant Hyrum Smith and my servant Reynolds Cahoon;
And also my servant Daniel Stanton and my servant Seymour Brunson;
And also my servant Sylvester Smith and my servant Gideon Carter;
And also my servant Ruggles Eames and my servant D&C 80:1.Stephen Burnett;
And also my servant Micah B. Welton and also my servant D&C 80:2.Eden Smith. Even so. Amen.

div.p: A vision given to Joseph Smith the Prophet and Sidney Rigdon, at Hiram, Ohio, February 16, 1832. Prefacing the record of this vision, Joseph Smith’s history states: “Upon my return from Amherst conference, I resumed the translation of the Scriptures. From sundry revelations which had been received, it was apparent that many important points touching the salvation of man had been taken from the Bible, or lost before it was compiled. It appeared self-evident from what truths were left, that if God rewarded every one according to the deeds done in the body the term ‘Heaven,’ as intended for the Saints’ eternal home, must include more kingdoms than one. Accordingly, … while translating St. John’s Gospel, myself and Elder Rigdon saw the following vision.” At the time this vision was given, the Prophet was translating John 5:29.

1–4, The Lord is God; 5–10, The mysteries of the kingdom will be revealed to all the faithful; 11–17, All will come forth in the resurrection of the just or the unjust; 18–24, The inhabitants of many worlds are begotten sons and daughters unto God through the Atonement of Jesus Christ; 25–29, An angel of God fell and became the devil; 30–49, Sons of perdition suffer eternal damnation; all others gain some degree of salvation; 50–70, The glory and reward of exalted beings in the celestial kingdom is described; 71–80, Those who will inherit the terrestrial kingdom are described; 81–113, The status of those in the telestial, terrestrial, and celestial glories is explained; 114–19, The faithful may see and understand the mysteries of God’s kingdom by the power of the Holy Spirit.

chapter.verse: Isa. 1:2.Hear, O ye heavens, and give ear, O earth, and rejoice ye inhabitants thereof, for the Lord is Josh. 22:34; Jer. 10:10.God, and beside him there is Ex. 8:10 (8–10); 1 Kgs. 8:60; Isa. 43:11; Hosea 13:4.no TG Jesus Christ, Savior.Savior.
Ex. 15:11. TG God, Intelligence of; God, Wisdom of.Great is his wisdom, Ps. 25:4 (1–5); 118:23; Rev. 15:3 (1–3).marvelous are his ways, and the extent of his doings none can find out.
His 1 Kgs. 8:56; 1 Ne. 20:14; D&C 64:31.purposes fail not, neither are there any who can stay his hand.
From eternity to eternity he is the Heb. 13:8; D&C 20:12; 35:1; 38:1 (1–4); 39:1 (1–3). TG God, Eternal Nature of; God, Perfection of.same, and his years never Ps. 102:27 (25–27); Heb. 1:12.fail.

For thus saith the Lord—I, the Lord, am Ex. 34:6; Ps. 103:8; Prov. 8:17. TG God, Mercy of.merciful and gracious unto those who Deut. 6:13; Josh. 4:24; 1 Kgs. 18:3. TG Reverence.fear me, and delight to honor those who 1 Sam. 7:3; Ps. 34:15; D&C 4:2.serve me in righteousness and in truth unto the end.
Great shall be their reward and eternal shall be their TG Celestial Glory.glory.

And to them will I D&C 42:61; 59:4; 98:12; 101:32; 121:28 (26–33).reveal all 2 Pet. 1:2. TG Mysteries of Godliness.mysteries, yea, all the hidden mysteries of my kingdom from days of old, and for ages to come, will I make known unto them the good pleasure of my will concerning all things pertaining to my kingdom.
Yea, even the wonders of TG Eternity.eternity shall they know, and things to come will I show them, even the things of many generations.

And their TG Wisdom.wisdom shall be great, and their TG Understanding.understanding reach to heaven; and before them the wisdom of the wise shall Isa. 29:14; 2 Ne. 9:28.perish, and the understanding of the TG Prudence.prudent shall come to naught.

For by my TG God, Spirit of.Spirit will I TG Testimony.enlighten them, and by my TG Jesus Christ, Power of.power will I make known unto them the Dan. 2:28.secrets of my TG God, Will of.will—yea, even those things which Isa. 64:4; 1 Cor. 2:9; 3 Ne. 17:16 (15–25); D&C 76:116 (114–19).eye has not seen, nor ear heard, nor yet entered into the heart of man.
We, Joseph Smith, Jun., and Sidney Rigdon, being Rev. 1:10; 4:2.in the Spirit on the sixteenth day of February, in the year of our Lord one thousand eight hundred and thirty-two—

By the power of the TG Transfiguration.Spirit our Eph. 1:18; D&C 11:13; 110:1; 137:1; 138:11 (11, 29). TG God, Privilege of Seeing.eyes were opened and our understandings were enlightened, so as to see and understand the things of God—

Even those things which were from the beginning before the world was, which were ordained of the Father, through his Only Begotten Son, who was in the bosom of the Father, even from the TG Jesus Christ, Foreordained.beginning;
Of whom we bear record; and the record which we bear is the fulness of the gospel of Jesus Christ, who is the Son, whom we saw and with whom we D&C 109:57.conversed in the heavenly TG Vision.vision.

For while we were doing the work of D&C 73:3 (3–4); 93:53.translation, which the Lord had appointed unto us, we came to the twenty-ninth verse of the fifth chapter of John, which was given unto us as follows—
Speaking of the resurrection of the dead, concerning those who shall John 5:28.hear the voice of the TG Jesus Christ, Son of Man.Son of Man:

And shall come forth; Joseph Smith Translation has the same wording as used here, which differs from the King James Version of John 5:29.they who have done TG Good Works.good, in the TG Resurrection.resurrection of the Dan. 12:2 (1–3); Acts 24:15; D&C 76:65 (50, 64–65).just; and they who have done evil, in the resurrection of the unjust.
Now this caused us to marvel, for it was given unto us of the Spirit.

And while we 1 Ne. 11:1; D&C 138:11 (1, 11); JS—H 1:44. TG Meditation.meditated upon these things, the Lord touched the eyes of our understandings and they were opened, and the TG God, Glory of.glory of the Lord shone round about.
And we beheld the TG Jesus Christ, Glory of.glory of the Son, on the Acts 7:56; D&C 49:6; 104:7.right hand of the TG God the Father, Elohim.Father, and received of his fulness;

And saw the holy Matt. 25:31; 2 Thes. 1:7; Heb. 12:22; D&C 130:7; 136:37.angels, and them who are TG Sanctification.sanctified before his throne, worshiping God, and the Lamb, who TG Worship.worship him forever and ever.

And now, after the many testimonies which have been given of him, this is the TG Testimony; Witness.testimony, last of all, which we give of him: That he Josh. 3:10; 2 Sam. 22:47; D&C 20:17. TG Jesus Christ, Resurrection.lives!

For we TG God, Privilege of Seeing; Jesus Christ, Appearances, Postmortal.saw him, even on the Heb. 1:3.right hand of TG Godhead.God; and we heard the voice bearing record that he is the John 1:14.Only Begotten of the Father—

That by Gen. 1:1; John 1:3 (1–3); Rom. 11:36 (34–36); Heb. 1:2 (1–3); Mosiah 4:2; Morm. 9:11; D&C 14:9; 93:10 (8–10).him, and through him, and of him, the Job 9:9 (7–9); Ps. 8:3 (3–4); Moses 1:33 (31–33); 7:30 (29–31). TG Astronomy; Creation; Jesus Christ, Creator; Jesus Christ, Power of.worlds are and were created, and the D&C 88:61.inhabitants thereof are begotten Mal. 2:10; 1 Cor. 15:45 (45–48); 2 Ne. 2:20 (19–20); D&C 27:11; Moses 1:34. TG Sons and Daughters of God.sons and daughters unto God.

And this we saw also, and bear record, that an D&C 29:36 (36–39); Moses 4:1 (1–3).angel of God who was in authority in the presence of God, who TG Council in Heaven; Rebellion.rebelled against the Only Begotten TG Jesus Christ, Divine Sonship.Son whom the Father TG Jesus Christ, Relationships with the Father.loved and who was in the bosom of the Father, was thrust down from the presence of God and the Son,
And was called D&C 76:32 (32–48); Moses 5:24. TG Sons of Perdition.Perdition, for the heavens Moses 7:29 (28–31).wept over him—he was Isa. 14:12.Lucifer, a son of the morning.
And we beheld, and lo, he is Luke 10:18.fallen! is fallen, even a son of the morning!

And while we were yet in the Spirit, the Lord commanded us that we should write the vision; for we beheld Satan, that old Rev. 12:9.serpent, even the TG Devil.devil, who rebelled against God, and sought to take the kingdom of our Isa. 14:14; D&C 29:36 (36–37); Moses 4:1 (1–4).God and his Christ—
Wherefore, he maketh Rev. 12:9 (7–9); 13:7; 2 Ne. 2:18; 28:20 (19–23); D&C 10:27. TG War.war with the saints of God, and encompasseth them round about.
And we saw a vision of the Jude 1:6 (6–8); D&C 76:44 (36, 44–49).sufferings of those with whom he made war and overcame, for thus came the voice of the Lord unto us:

Thus saith the Lord concerning all those who know my power, and have been made partakers thereof, and TG Apostasy of Individuals.suffered themselves through the power of the devil to be overcome, and to deny the truth and defy my power—
They are they who are the TG Sons of Perdition.sons of D&C 76:26; Moses 5:24.perdition, of whom I say that it had been better for them never to have been born;
For they are Rom. 9:22 (20–23); Rev. 2:27 (26–27).vessels of wrath, doomed to suffer the wrath of God, with the devil and his angels in eternity;
Concerning whom I have said there is D&C 42:18; 84:41; 132:27.no TG Forgive.forgiveness in this world nor in the world to come—

Having 2 Pet. 2:20 (20–22); Alma 39:6; D&C 76:83. TG Holy Ghost, Loss of; Holy Ghost, Unpardonable Sin against.denied the Holy Spirit after having received it, and having denied the Only Begotten Son of the Father, having Heb. 6:6 (4–6); 1 Ne. 19:7; D&C 132:27.crucified him unto themselves and put him to an open TG Shame.shame.

And the D&C 76:44 (44–49).only ones on whom the D&C 29:28 (28, 41).second D&C 64:7. TG Death, Spiritual, Second.death shall have any power;
Yea, verily, the only ones who shall TG Sons of Perdition.not be redeemed in the due time of the Lord, after the sufferings of his wrath.

For all the rest shall be IE redeemed; see v. 38. All will be resurrected. See Alma 11:41–45; D&C 88:32.brought forth by the resurrection of the dead, through the TG Jesus Christ, Resurrection.triumph and the glory of the Lamb, who was slain, who was in the bosom of the Father John 1:1 (1–3, 10); Rev. 13:8; D&C 93:7.before the worlds were made.
And this is the 3 Ne. 27:13 (13–22). TG Gospel; Salvation, Plan of.gospel, the glad Luke 8:1.tidings, which the voice out of the heavens bore record unto us—

That he TG Jesus Christ, Birth of; Jesus Christ, Mission of.came into the world, even Jesus, to be TG Jesus Christ, Crucifixion of.crucified for the world, and to Isa. 53:12 (4–12); Heb. 9:28.bear the sins of the 1 Jn. 2:2 (1–2); Alma 11:41 (40–41).world, and to TG Salvation, Plan of; Sanctification.sanctify the world, and to TG Purification; Redemption.cleanse it from all unrighteousness;
That through him all might be TG Salvation.saved whom the Father had put into his TG Jesus Christ, Authority of.power and made by him;

Who John 17:4. TG Jesus Christ, Relationships with the Father.glorifies the Father, and saves all the works of his hands, except those sons of TG Sons of Perdition.perdition who deny the Son after the Father has revealed him.

Wherefore, he saves all D&C 76:37.except them—they shall go away into D&C 19:6.everlasting D&C 76:30. TG Damnation; Punish.punishment, which is endless punishment, which is eternal punishment, to D&C 88:24.reign with the TG Devil.devil and his angels in eternity, where their Isa. 66:24; Mark 9:48 (43–48).worm dieth not, and the fire is not quenched, which is their torment—
And the D&C 29:28 (28–29).end thereof, neither the place thereof, nor their torment, no man knows;
Neither was it revealed, neither is, neither will be revealed unto man, except to them who are made partakers thereof;
Nevertheless, I, the Lord, show it by Moses 1:20. TG Vision.vision unto many, but straightway shut it up again;

Wherefore, the end, the width, the height, the Rev. 20:1.depth, and the misery thereof, they understand not, neither any man except those who are IE sentenced, consigned.ordained unto this Alma 42:22.condemnation.
And we heard the voice, saying: TG Scriptures, Writing of.Write the vision, for lo, this is the end of the vision of the sufferings of the ungodly.

And again we bear record—for we TG Vision.saw and heard, and this is the TG Testimony.testimony of the TG Gospel.gospel of Christ concerning them who shall come forth in the resurrection of the D&C 29:13; 76:17.just—

TG Baptism, Qualifications for.testimony of Jesus, and D&C 20:25.believed on his name and were TG Baptism, Essential.baptized after the D&C 124:29; 128:13.manner of his burial, being Rom. 6:4 (3–5). That by D&C 138:12.keeping the commandments they might be 2 Ne. 9:23; Moro. 8:25.washed and TG Purification.cleansed from all their sins, and receive the Holy Spirit by the laying on of the TG Hands, Laying on of; Holy Ghost, Gift of.hands of him who is TG Authority.ordained and sealed unto this power;

And who TG Self-Mastery.overcome by faith, and are TG Holy Ghost, Mission of; Sealing.sealed by the Holy Spirit of Eph. 1:13; D&C 88:3 (3–4); 124:124; 132:19 (18–26). TG Promise.promise, which the Father Acts 2:33.sheds forth upon all those who are just and true.

They are they who are Ex. 19:6; Rev. 1:6 (1–6); 5:10; 20:6; D&C 78:15 (15, 18); 132:19 (19–20).priests and D&C 104:7.kings, who have received of his fulness, and of his glory;

And are TG High Priest, Melchizedek Priesthood; Priesthood, Melchizedek.priests of the Most High, after the order of Melchizedek, which was after the order of Gen. 5:23; Moses 6:27 (27–68); 7:1 (1–69).Enoch, which was after the D&C 107:3 (2–4).order of the Only Begotten Son.

And they shall Rev. 3:5; 21:7.overcome all things.

These shall Ps. 15:1 (1–5); 24:3 (3–4); 27:4; 1 Ne. 10:21; 15:33 (33–36); Mosiah 15:23 (19–26); Morm. 7:7; Moses 6:57 (55–59).dwell in the D&C 76:94 (94, 119); 130:7. TG Eternal Life; God, Presence of.presence of God and his Christ forever and ever.
