These are they who shall come forth in the resurrection of the D&C 76:17.just.

These are they who are come unto Isa. 24:23; Joel 2:32; Obad. 1:21; Heb. 11:10; 12:22 (22, 24); Rev. 14:1; D&C 84:2 (2, 18, 32); 133:56 (18, 56).Mount TG Zion.Zion, and unto the city of the living God, the heavenly place, the holiest of all.

These are they who have come to an innumerable company of TG Angels.angels, to the general assembly and church of D&C 38:4; 45:11 (11–12); 84:100 (99–100); 133:54.Enoch, and of the D&C 76:54 (53–54).Firstborn.
These are they whose names are TG Book of Life.written in heaven, where God and Christ are the TG Jesus Christ, Judge; Judgment.judge of all.

These are they who are Ezek. 18:9 (5–9); Heb. 12:23; D&C 129:3 (3–6); 138:12.just men made TG Man, New, Spiritually Reborn; Perfection.perfect through Jesus the mediator of the TG New and Everlasting Covenant.new covenant, who wrought out this perfect TG Jesus Christ, Atonement through.atonement through the shedding of his own TG Blood, Symbolism of.blood.

These are they whose bodies are D&C 88:29; 131:1 (1–4); 137:7. TG Celestial Glory.celestial, whose Dan. 12:3; D&C 137:2 (2–4).glory is that of the Matt. 13:43; 1 Cor. 15:41 (40–42).sun, even the glory of God, the TG God, Perfection of.highest of all, whose glory the sun of the firmament is written of as being typical.

And again, we saw the D&C 88:30. TG Terrestrial Glory.terrestrial world, and behold and lo, these are they who are of the terrestrial, whose glory differs from that of the church of the D&C 76:54.Firstborn who have received the fulness of the Father, even as that of the 1 Cor. 15:41.moon differs from the sun in the firmament.
Behold, these are they who died Rom. 2:12; D&C 137:7 (7–10).without Acts 17:30. TG Accountability; Ignorance.law;

And also they who are the Job 14:10; Alma 11:45; 40:11 (11–14); Moses 7:57. TG Spirit Body; Spirits, Disembodied; Spirits in Prison.spirits of men kept in D&C 88:99; 138:8. TG Genealogy and Temple Work; Salvation for the Dead.prison, whom the Son visited, and 1 Pet. 3:19 (19–20); 4:6; D&C 138:19.preached the TG Gospel.gospel unto them, that they might be judged according to men in the flesh;
Who D&C 138:32.received not the TG Salvation for the Dead; Testimony.testimony of Jesus in the flesh, but afterwards received it.
These are they who are TG Honorable.honorable men of the earth, who were TG Spiritual Blindness.blinded by the craftiness of men.
These are they who receive of his glory, but not of his fulness.
These are they who receive of the TG God, Presence of.presence of the Son, but not of the fulness of the Father.
Wherefore, they are 1 Cor. 15:40 (40–42).bodies terrestrial, and not bodies celestial, and differ in glory as the moon differs from the sun.

These are they who are not Jer. 8:20; D&C 45:2; 56:16; 121:29. TG Apathy; Diligence; Trustworthiness.valiant in the TG Testimony.testimony of Jesus; wherefore, they obtain not the crown over the kingdom of our God.

And now this is the end of the TG Vision.vision which we saw of the terrestrial, that the Lord commanded us to Moses 2:1. TG Record Keeping; Scriptures, Writing of.write while we were yet in the Spirit.

And again, we TG Vision.saw the glory of the D&C 88:31. TG Telestial Glory.telestial, which glory is that of the lesser, even as the TG Glory.glory of the stars differs from that of the glory of the moon in the firmament.
These are they who received not the gospel of Christ, neither the D&C 138:21. TG Testimony.testimony of Jesus.
These are they who D&C 76:35.deny not the Holy Spirit.
These are they who are thrust down to Prov. 9:18; 2 Ne. 9:12 (11–12); Alma 12:11; D&C 29:38. TG Hell.hell.

These are they who shall not be redeemed from the TG Devil.devil until the Rev. 20:13 (12–13); Alma 11:41; D&C 43:18; 88:100 (100–101).last resurrection, until the Lord, even Christ the Gen. 22:8 (8–14); 1 Ne. 13:35.Lamb, shall have finished his work.
These are they who receive not of his fulness in the eternal world, but of the Holy Spirit through the ministration of the terrestrial;
And the terrestrial through the D&C 138:37.ministration of the celestial.

And also the telestial receive it of the administering of angels who are appointed to minister for them, or who are appointed to be TG Angels.ministering spirits for them; for they shall be D&C 7:6.heirs of salvation.
And thus we saw, in the heavenly vision, the glory of the TG Telestial Glory.telestial, which surpasses all understanding;
And no man knows it except him to whom God has revealed it.
And thus we saw the glory of the TG Terrestrial Glory.terrestrial which excels in all things the glory of the telestial, even in glory, and in power, and in might, and in dominion.

And thus we saw the TG Celestial Glory.glory of the celestial, which D&C 18:45; 84:38 (35–38).excels in all things—where God, even the Father, reigns upon his TG Kingdom of God, in Heaven.throne forever and ever;
Before whose throne all things bow in humble TG Reverence.reverence, and give him glory forever and ever.

They who dwell in his D&C 76:62; 130:7. TG God, Presence of.presence are the church of the D&C 76:54.Firstborn; and they see as they are seen, and 1 Cor. 13:12.know as they are known, having received of his fulness and of his TG Grace.grace;
And he makes them D&C 29:13 (12–13); 78:5 (5–7); 84:38 (35–39); 88:107; 132:20 (18–20).equal in power, and in might, and in dominion.
And the glory of the celestial is one, even as the glory of the 1 Cor. 15:41 (40–41).sun is one.
And the glory of the terrestrial is one, even as the glory of the moon is one.
And the glory of the telestial is one, even as the glory of the stars is one; for as one star differs from another star in glory, even so differs one from another in glory in the telestial world;
For these are they who are of 1 Cor. 3:22.Paul, and of Apollos, and of Cephas.

These are they who say they are some of one and some of another—some of Christ and some of John, and some of Moses, and some of Elias, and some of D&C 84:13 (11–13).Esaias, and some of Isaiah, and some of Enoch;
But TG Prophets, Rejection of.received not the gospel, neither the testimony of Jesus, neither the prophets, neither the TG New and Everlasting Covenant.everlasting covenant.

Last of all, these all are they who will not be TG Separation.gathered with the saints, to be 1 Thes. 4:16 (16–17); D&C 84:100; 88:96 (96–98); 101:31.caught up unto the D&C 78:21.church of the Firstborn, and received into the cloud.

These are Rev. 21:8; 22:15 (14–15); D&C 63:17 (17–18).they who are TG Lying.liars, and TG Sorcery.sorcerers, and TG Adulterer; Sexual Immorality.adulterers, and TG Whore.whoremongers, and whosoever loves and makes a lie.
These are they who suffer the TG Damnation.wrath of God on earth.
These are they who suffer the Jude 1:7.vengeance of eternal fire.

These are they who are cast down to TG Damnation; Hell.hell and D&C 19:6.suffer the wrath of D&C 87:6.Almighty God, until the Eph. 1:10 (9–10).fulness of times, when Christ shall have 1 Cor. 15:28; Philip. 3:21.subdued all enemies under his Heb. 2:8.feet, and shall have Heb. 10:14 (12–14).perfected his work;

When he shall 1 Cor. 15:24 (24–28).deliver up the TG Jesus Christ, Relationships with the Father; Kingdom of God, on Earth.kingdom, and present it unto the Father, spotless, saying: I have John 16:33.overcome and have Rev. 14:20 (15–20); 19:15; D&C 88:106; 133:50 (46–53).trodden the Gen. 49:11 (11–12); Isa. 63:2 (1–3); Joel 3:13; D&C 133:48.wine-press Mark 14:37 (37, 40–41); D&C 122:8 (7–8).alone, even the wine-press of the fierceness of the wrath of Almighty God.
Then shall he be Rev. 19:16.crowned with the crown of his glory, to sit on the D&C 137:3.throne of his power to reign forever and ever.
But behold, and lo, we saw the glory and the inhabitants of the telestial world, that they were as Matt. 7:13.innumerable as the stars in the firmament of heaven, or as the sand upon the seashore;
And heard the voice of the Lord saying: These all shall bow the knee, and every tongue shall Philip. 2:10 (9–11).confess to him who sits upon the throne forever and ever;

For they shall be judged according to their TG Good Works.works, and every man shall receive according to his own Rev. 20:12 (12–13).works, his own Dan. 7:27.dominion, in the John 14:2; D&C 59:2; 81:6.mansions which are prepared;
And they shall be TG Servant.servants of the Most High; but D&C 29:29.where God and Christ Rev. 21:27 (9–27).dwell they D&C 43:33 (18, 33).cannot come, Eph. 3:21.worlds without end.
This is the end of the vision which we saw, which we were commanded to write while we were yet in the Spirit.

But 1 Chr. 16:9; Ps. 9:1; 26:7; 40:5; 92:5; Rev. 15:3; Morm. 9:16 (16–20); D&C 88:47; Moses 1:4 (3–5).great and marvelous are the works of the Lord, and the Jacob 4:8; D&C 19:10.mysteries of his kingdom which he showed unto us, which surpass all understanding in glory, and in might, and in dominion;
Which he commanded us we should not write while we were yet in the Spirit, and are not 2 Cor. 12:4; 3 Ne. 28:14 (12–14).lawful for man to utter;

Neither is man 3 Ne. 5:18; 17:16 (15–25); 19:32 (30–36); D&C 76:10.capable to make them known, for they are only to be Ezek. 12:2.seen and 1 Cor. 2:11 (10–12). TG Holy Ghost, Mission of.understood by the power of the Holy Spirit, which God bestows on those who D&C 20:31.love him, and purify themselves before him;
To whom he grants this privilege of TG God, Privilege of Seeing.seeing and knowing for themselves;
That through the power and manifestation of the Spirit, while in the flesh, they may be able to D&C 88:22.bear his TG God, Presence of.presence in the world of glory.
And to God and the Lamb be Matt. 6:13.glory, and honor, and dominion forever and ever. Amen.

div.p: Revelation given to Joseph Smith the Prophet, at Hiram, Ohio, about March 1832. Joseph Smith’s history states, “In connection with the translation of the Scriptures, I received the following explanation of the Revelation of St. John.”

1–4, Beasts have spirits and will dwell in eternal felicity; 5–7, This earth has a temporal existence of 7,000 years; 8–10, Various angels restore the gospel and minister on earth; 11, The sealing of the 144,000; 12–14, Christ will come in the beginning of the seventh thousand years; 15, Two prophets will be raised up to the Jewish nation.

verse.divCT: Q. What is the Ezek. 1:22; Rev. 4:6.sea of glass spoken of by John, 4th chapter, and 6th verse of the Revelation?
A. It is the TG Earth, Destiny of.earth, in its D&C 130:9. TG Sanctification.sanctified, TG Immortality.immortal, and TG Celestial Glory.eternal state.
Q. What are we to understand by the four beasts, spoken of in the same verse?

A. They are TG Symbolism.figurative expressions, used by the Revelator, John, in describing TG Heaven.heaven, the TG Paradise.paradise of God, the TG Happiness.happiness of man, and of beasts, and of creeping things, and of the fowls of the air; that which is spiritual being in the likeness of that which is temporal; and that which is temporal in the likeness of that which is spiritual; the D&C 93:33; Abr. 5:7 (7–8). TG Man, a Spirit Child of Heavenly Father; Spirit Body.spirit of man in the likeness of his person, as also the spirit of the D&C 29:24 (24–25); Moses 3:19.beast, and every other creature which God has created.
Q. Are the four beasts limited to individual beasts, or do they represent classes or D&C 88:42 (37–42); Abr. 3:9.orders?

A. They are limited to four individual beasts, which were shown to John, to represent the glory of the classes of beings in their destined TG Order.order or D&C 93:30; Moses 3:9.sphere of creation, in the enjoyment of their D&C 29:24.eternal TG Joy.felicity.
Q. What are we to understand by the Zech. 3:9; Rev. 5:6.eyes and 2 Chr. 5:8; Isa. 6:2 (2–7); Ezek. 1:11.wings, which the beasts had?

A. Their eyes are a representation of light and knowledge, that is, they are full of TG God, Omniscience of.knowledge; and their wings are a TG Symbolism.representation of TG God, Power of.power, to move, to act, etc.
Q. What are we to understand by the four and twenty Rev. 4:4 (4, 10).elders, spoken of by John?

A. We are to understand that these elders whom John saw, were elders who had been Rev. 14:4 (2–5).faithful in the work of the ministry and were dead; who belonged to the Rev. 1:4.seven churches, and were then in the paradise of God.
Q. What are we to understand by the book which John saw, which was Rev. 5:1.sealed on the back with seven seals?

A. We are to understand that it contains the revealed will, TG Mysteries of Godliness.mysteries, and the works of God; the hidden things of his economy concerning this TG Earth, Destiny of.earth during the seven thousand years of its continuance, or its temporal existence.
Q. What are we to understand by the seven TG Seal.seals with which it was sealed?

A. We are to understand that the first seal contains the things of the D&C 88:108 (108–10).first thousand years, and the Rev. 6:3 (3–4); Moses 8:28 (22, 28–29).second also of the second thousand years, and so on until the seventh.
Q. What are we to understand by the four Rev. 7:1 (1–8).angels, spoken of in the 7th chapter and 1st verse of Revelation?

A. We are to understand that they are four angels sent forth from God, to whom is given power over the four parts of the earth, to save life and to destroy; these are they who have the Rev. 14:6.everlasting gospel to commit to every nation, kindred, tongue, and people; having power to 1 Kgs. 8:35; Ether 4:9.shut up the heavens, to seal up unto life, or to cast down to the Matt. 8:12 (11–12); 22:13 (1–14); D&C 133:72 (71–73).regions of darkness.
Q. What are we to understand by the angel Rev. 7:2.ascending from the east, Revelation 7th chapter and 2nd verse?

A. We are to understand that the angel ascending from the east is he to whom is given the seal of the living God over the twelve tribes of Rev. 7:4.Israel; wherefore, he crieth unto the four angels having the everlasting gospel, saying: Hurt not the earth, neither the sea, nor the trees, till we have sealed the servants of our God in their Ezek. 9:4.foreheads. And, if you will receive it, this is BD Elias.Elias which was to come to gather together the tribes of Israel and TG Restoration of the Gospel.restore all things.
Q. What time are the things spoken of in this chapter to be accomplished?
A. They are to be accomplished in the Rev. 6:12 (12–17).sixth thousand years, or the opening of the sixth seal.
Q. What are we to understand by sealing the Rev. 7:4 (1–8); 14:3.one hundred and forty-four thousand, out of all the tribes of Israel—twelve thousand out of every tribe?

A. We are to understand that those who are sealed are TG High Priest, Melchizedek Priesthood.high priests, ordained unto the holy order of God, to administer the everlasting gospel; for they are they who are ordained out of every nation, kindred, tongue, and people, by the angels to whom is given power over the nations of the earth, to bring as many as will come to the church of the D&C 76:54 (54, 67, 71, 102). TG Jesus Christ, Firstborn.Firstborn.
Q. What are we to understand by the sounding of the Rev. 8:2.trumpets, mentioned in the 8th chapter of Revelation?

A. We are to understand that as God TG Creation.made the world in six days, and on the seventh day he finished his work, and Gen. 2:3 (1–3); Ex. 20:11; Mosiah 13:19 (16–19); Moses 3:3 (1–3); Abr. 5:3 (1–3).sanctified it, and also formed man out of the Gen. 2:7; Morm. 9:17; D&C 93:35 (33–35).dust of the earth, even so, in the beginning of the seventh thousand years will the Lord God TG Sanctification.sanctify the earth, and complete the salvation of man, and TG Jesus Christ, Judge; Judgment, the Last.judge all things, and shall TG Jesus Christ, Redeemer.redeem all things, except that which he hath not put into his power, when he shall have sealed all things, unto the end of all things; and the sounding of the trumpets of the seven angels are the preparing and finishing of his work, in the beginning of the seventh thousand years—the TG Millennium, Preparing a People for.preparing of the way before the time of his coming.
Q. When are the things to be accomplished, which are written in the 9th chapter of Revelation?
A. They are to be accomplished after the Rev. 8:1.opening of the seventh seal, Mal. 4:5.before the coming of Christ.
Q. What are we to understand by the little book which was Ezek. 2:9 (9–10); 3:2 (1–3); Rev. 10:10.eaten by John, as mentioned in the 10th chapter of Revelation?

A. We are to understand that it was a mission, and an ordinance, for him to TG Israel, Gathering of.gather the tribes of Israel; behold, this is Elias, who, as it is written, must come and Matt. 17:11. TG Restoration of the Gospel.restore all things.
Q. What is to be understood by the two Zech. 4:14 (12–14); Rev. 11:3 (1–14).witnesses, in the eleventh chapter of Revelation?

A. They are two prophets that are to be raised up to the TG Israel, Judah, People of.Jewish nation in the last days, at the time of the TG Last Days; Restoration of the Gospel.restoration, and to prophesy to the Jews after they are gathered and have built the city of Jerusalem in the Amos 9:15 (14–15). TG Israel, Land of.land of their fathers.

div.p: Revelation given through Joseph Smith the Prophet, at Kirtland, Ohio, March 1, 1832. On that day, the Prophet and other leaders had assembled to discuss Church business. This revelation originally instructed the Prophet, Sidney Rigdon, and Newel K. Whitney to travel to Missouri and organize the Church’s mercantile and publishing endeavors by creating a “firm” that would oversee these efforts, generating funds for the establishment of Zion and for the benefit of the poor. This firm, known as the United Firm, was organized in April 1832 and disbanded in 1834 (see section 82). Sometime after its dissolution, under the direction of Joseph Smith, the phrase “the affairs of the storehouse for the poor” replaced “literary and mercantile establishments” in the revelation, and the word “order” replaced the word “firm.”

1–4, The Saints should organize and establish a storehouse; 5–12, Wise use of their properties will lead to salvation; 13–14, The Church should be independent of earthly powers; 15–16, Michael (Adam) serves under the direction of the Holy One (Christ); 17–22, Blessed are the faithful, for they will inherit all things.

chapter.verse: The Lord spake unto Joseph Smith, Jun., saying: Hearken unto me, saith the Lord your God, who are ordained unto the TG Priesthood, Melchizedek.high priesthood of my church, who have assembled yourselves together;

And listen to the TG Counsel.counsel of him who has TG Priesthood, Authority; Priesthood, Ordination.ordained you from on high, who shall speak in your ears the words of TG God, Wisdom of; Wisdom.wisdom, that salvation may be unto you in that thing which you have presented before me, saith the Lord God.

For verily I say unto you, the time has come, and is now at hand; and behold, and lo, it must needs be that there be an D&C 51:3; 82:11 (11, 15–21).organization of my people, in regulating and establishing the affairs of the D&C 72:10; 83:5.storehouse for the D&C 42:30 (30–42). TG Poor; Welfare.poor of my people, both in this place and in the land of D&C 57:2.Zion—
For a permanent and everlasting establishment and order unto my church, to advance the cause, which ye have espoused, to the salvation of man, and to the glory of your Father who is in heaven;
That you may be TG Consecration; Zion.equal in the bonds of heavenly things, yea, and earthly things also, for the obtaining of heavenly things.
For if ye are not equal in earthly things ye cannot be D&C 49:20.equal in obtaining heavenly things;

For if you will that I give unto you a place in the TG Celestial Glory; Objectives.celestial world, you must D&C 29:8; 58:6; 132:3.prepare yourselves by TG Commitment.doing the things which I have commanded you and required of you.
And now, verily thus saith the Lord, it is expedient that all things be done unto my TG Glory.glory, by you who are joined together in this D&C 92:1.order;
Or, in other words, let my servant Newel K. Whitney and my servant Joseph Smith, Jun., and my servant Sidney Rigdon sit in council with the saints which are in D&C 72:6 (1–26).Zion;

Otherwise TG Devil.Satan seeketh to turn their TG Hardheartedness.hearts away from the truth, that they become TG Spiritual Blindness.blinded and understand not the things which are prepared for them.
Wherefore, a commandment I give unto you, to prepare and organize yourselves by a D&C 82:11 (11, 15).bond or everlasting TG Covenants.covenant that cannot be broken.

And he who breaketh it shall lose his office and standing in the church, and shall be 1 Tim. 1:20.delivered over to the 1 Cor. 5:5 (1–7); D&C 82:21; 104:10 (8–10).buffetings of Satan until the day of redemption.

Behold, this is the preparation wherewith I prepare you, and the foundation, and the TG Millennium, Preparing a People for.ensample which I give unto you, whereby you may accomplish the commandments which are given you;

That through my providence, notwithstanding the D&C 58:4 (3–4). TG Tribulation.tribulation which shall descend upon you, that the church may stand independent above all other creatures beneath the celestial world;

That you may come up unto the TG Exaltation.crown prepared for you, and be made Ex. 19:6; Rev. 5:10; 20:6; D&C 76:56; 132:19 (19–20).rulers over many kingdoms, saith the Lord God, the Holy One of Zion, who hath established the foundations of D&C 107:53; 116; 117:8 (8, 11).Adam-ondi-Ahman;

Who hath appointed D&C 27:11. TG Adam.Michael your prince, and established his feet, and set him upon high, and given unto him the keys of salvation under the counsel and direction of the D&C 107:54 (54–55).Holy One, who is without beginning of days or end of life.

Verily, verily, I say unto you, ye are John 13:33; 1 Cor. 2:9; 1 Jn. 2:1 (1, 12–13).little children, and ye have not as yet understood how great blessings the Father hath in his own hands and prepared for you;

And ye cannot John 16:12; 3 Ne. 17:2 (2–4); D&C 50:40.bear all things now; nevertheless, be of good TG Cheerful.cheer, for I will TG Guidance, Divine.lead you along. The kingdom is yours and the blessings thereof are yours, and the TG Treasure.riches of TG Eternity.eternity are yours.

And he who receiveth all things with Ps. 34:1 (1–3); Mosiah 2:20 (20–21). TG Thanksgiving.thankfulness shall be made glorious; and the things of this earth shall be added unto him, even an Matt. 19:29 (27–29).hundred fold, yea, more.
Wherefore, do the things which I have commanded you, saith your Redeemer, even the Son D&C 95:17; 107:53; 116.Ahman, who prepareth all things before he 1 Thes. 4:17; Rev. 11:12.taketh you;
For ye are the D&C 76:102.church of the D&C 76:54 (53–54).Firstborn, and he will take you up in a D&C 88:97.cloud, and appoint every man his portion.
And he that is a faithful and D&C 101:61.wise TG Stewardship.steward shall inherit D&C 84:38.all things. Amen.

div.p: Revelation given through Joseph Smith the Prophet, at Hiram, Ohio, March 12, 1832.
1–4, Jared Carter is called to preach the gospel by the Comforter.

chapter.verse: Verily I say unto you, that it is my will that my servant Jared Carter should go again into the eastern countries, from place to place, and from city to city, in the power of the D&C 52:38 (38–39).ordination wherewith he has been ordained, proclaiming glad tidings of great joy, even the Rev. 14:6.everlasting gospel.
And I will send upon him the TG Holy Ghost, Comforter.Comforter, which shall teach him the truth and the Ps. 25:4 (1–5).way whither he shall go;
And inasmuch as he is faithful, I will crown him again with D&C 31:5.sheaves.
Wherefore, let your heart be glad, my servant Jared Carter, and TG Courage; Fearful; Joy.fear not, saith your Lord, even Jesus Christ. Amen.

div.p: Revelation given through Joseph Smith the Prophet to Stephen Burnett, at Hiram, Ohio, March 7, 1832.
1–5, Stephen Burnett and Eden Smith are called to preach in whatever place they choose.

chapter.verse: Verily, thus saith the Lord unto you my servant D&C 75:35.Stephen Burnett: Go ye, go ye into the world and preach the gospel to every Mark 16:15.creature that cometh under the sound of your voice.
And inasmuch as you desire a companion, I will give unto you my servant D&C 75:36.Eden Smith.
Wherefore, go ye and preach my gospel, whether to the north or to the south, to the east or to the west, it mattereth not, for ye cannot go amiss.
Therefore, declare the things which ye have heard, and verily believe, and D&C 20:17. TG Testimony.know to be true.
Behold, this is the will of him who hath TG Called of God.called you, your Redeemer, even Jesus Christ. Amen.

div.p: Revelation given through Joseph Smith the Prophet, at Hiram, Ohio, March 15, 1832. Frederick G. Williams is called to be a high priest and a counselor in the Presidency of the High Priesthood. The historical records show that when this revelation was received in March 1832, it called Jesse Gause to the office of counselor to Joseph Smith in the Presidency. However, when he failed to continue in a manner consistent with this appointment, the call was subsequently transferred to Frederick G. Williams. The revelation (dated March 1832) should be regarded as a step toward the formal organization of the First Presidency, specifically calling for the office of counselor in that body and explaining the dignity of the appointment. Brother Gause served for a time but was excommunicated from the Church in December 1832. Brother Williams was ordained to the specified office on March 18, 1833.
1–2, The keys of the kingdom are always held by the First Presidency; 3–7, If Frederick G. Williams is faithful in his ministry, he will have eternal life.

chapter.verse: Verily, verily, I say unto you my servant Frederick G. Williams: Listen to the voice of him who speaketh, to the word of the Lord your God, and hearken to the calling wherewith you are called, even to be a D&C 68:15. TG High Priest, Melchizedek Priesthood.high priest in my church, and a D&C 35:22 (3–23); 90:6; 107:24. TG Counselor.counselor unto my servant Joseph Smith, Jun.;
Unto whom I have given the TG Priesthood, Keys of.keys of the kingdom, which belong always unto the D&C 68:15; 107:17 (9, 17, 22).Presidency of the High Priesthood:

Therefore, verily I acknowledge him and will bless him, and also thee, inasmuch as thou art faithful in counsel, in the office which I have appointed unto you, in prayer always, vocally and in thy heart, in public and in private, also in thy TG Mission of Latter-day Saints.ministry in proclaiming the gospel in the Ps. 27:13.land of the living, and among thy brethren.
And in doing these things thou wilt do the greatest TG Good Works.good unto thy fellow beings, and wilt promote the D&C 124:18; Moses 1:39.glory of him who is your Lord.

Wherefore, be faithful; stand in the office which I have appointed unto you; TG Service.succor the Rom. 14:1 (1–3).weak, lift up the hands which hang down, and D&C 23:3; 31:8; 108:7.strengthen the Isa. 35:3.feeble knees.

And if thou art TG Steadfastness.faithful unto the end thou shalt have a TG Exaltation.crown of TG Immortality.immortality, and eternal life in the John 14:2 (2–3); D&C 59:2; 76:111; 98:18; 106:8.mansions which I have prepared in the house of my Father.
Behold, and lo, these are the words of Alpha and Omega, even Jesus Christ. Amen.

div.p: Revelation given to Joseph Smith the Prophet, in Independence, Jackson County, Missouri, April 26, 1832. The occasion was a council of high priests and elders of the Church. At the council, Joseph Smith was sustained as the President of the High Priesthood, to which office he had previously been ordained at a conference of high priests, elders, and members, at Amherst, Ohio, January 25, 1832 (see the heading to section 75). This revelation reiterates instructions given in an earlier revelation (section 78) to establish a firm—known as the United Firm (under Joseph Smith’s direction, the term “order” later replaced “firm”)—to govern the Church’s mercantile and publishing endeavors.

1–4, Where much is given, much is required; 5–7, Darkness reigns in the world; 8–13, The Lord is bound when we do what He says; 14–18, Zion must increase in beauty and holiness; 19–24, Every man should seek the interest of his neighbor.

chapter.verse: Verily, verily, I say unto you, my servants, that inasmuch as you have Matt. 6:14. TG Forgive.forgiven one another your trespasses, even so I, the Lord, forgive you.

Nevertheless, there are those among you who have sinned exceedingly; yea, even Rom. 3:23.all of you have sinned; but verily I say unto you, beware from henceforth, and TG Abstain.refrain from sin, lest sore judgments fall upon your heads.

For of him unto whom Luke 12:48; James 4:17. TG Accountability; Talents.much is Matt. 25:29. TG Stewardship.given much is TG Mission of Latter-day Saints.required; and he who TG Apostasy of Individuals; Sin.sins against the greater John 15:22 (22–24); Rom. 7:7 (7–8). TG Intelligence; Light [noun].light shall Luke 12:47 (47–48); James 3:1.receive the greater D&C 18:46. TG Punish.condemnation.

Ye call upon my name for TG Revelation.revelations, and I give them unto you; and inasmuch as ye keep not my sayings, which I give unto you, ye become transgressors; and TG God, Justice of; Justice.justice and judgment are the penalty which is affixed unto my law.
Therefore, what I say unto one I say unto all: TG Watch.Watch, for the TG Devil.adversary Isa. 60:2; D&C 38:11 (11–12).spreadeth his dominions, and TG Darkness, Spiritual.darkness reigneth;
And the anger of God kindleth against the inhabitants of the earth; and Eccl. 7:20; Matt. 19:17.none doeth good, for all have gone out of the Gen. 6:12; Rom. 3:12; D&C 1:16; Moses 8:29.way.

And now, verily I say unto you, I, the Lord, will not lay any John 8:11 (2–11). TG Sin.sin to your charge; go your ways and sin no more; but unto that soul who sinneth shall the Ps. 79:8; Matt. 12:45 (43–45); 18:32–34; D&C 1:33 (32–33); 58:43.former sins return, saith the Lord your God.
And again, I say unto you, I give unto you a John 13:34.new commandment, that you may understand my will concerning you;
Or, in other words, I give unto you directions how you may D&C 43:8; 103:1.act before me, that it may Philip. 1:19.turn to you for your salvation.

I, the Lord, am Josh. 23:14; 1 Kgs. 8:23; Ps. 97:10; 145:20 (1–21); Prov. 12:2; 1 Ne. 17:35 (33–35); D&C 1:37 (37–38); 58:31; 130:20 (20–21). TG Blessing; Obedience.bound when ye do what I say; but when ye do not what I say, ye have no TG Promise.promise.

Therefore, verily I say unto you, that it is expedient for my servants Edward Partridge and Newel K. Whitney, A. Sidney Gilbert and Sidney Rigdon, and my servant Joseph Smith, and John Whitmer and Oliver Cowdery, and W. W. Phelps and Martin Harris to be bound D&C 78:3 (3–7, 11–15); 92:1.together by a bond and covenant that cannot be D&C 78:11.broken by transgression, except judgment shall immediately follow, in your several TG Stewardship.stewardships—
To manage the affairs of the poor, and all things pertaining to the bishopric D&C 104:47.both in the land of Zion and in the land of Kirtland;
For I have consecrated the land of Kirtland in mine own due time for the benefit of the saints of the Most High, and for a Isa. 33:20; 54:2; D&C 68:26; 94:1.stake to Zion.

For TG Mission of Latter-day Saints.Zion must increase in TG Beauty.beauty, and in Lev. 19:2 (2–37).holiness; her borders must be enlarged; her TG Stake.stakes must be strengthened; yea, verily I say unto you, Zion must TG Israel, Restoration of.arise and put on her Isa. 52:1; D&C 113:8 (7–8).beautiful garments.
Therefore, I give unto you this commandment, that ye bind yourselves by this covenant, and it shall be done according to the laws of the Lord.
Behold, here is TG God, Wisdom of.wisdom also in me for your good.

And you are to be D&C 51:3.equal, or in other words, you are to have equal TG Consecration.claims on the TG Wages.properties, for the benefit of TG Family, Managing Finances in.managing the concerns of your stewardships, every man according to his wants and his needs, inasmuch as his wants are just—

And all this for the benefit of the church of the living God, that every man may TG Industry.improve upon his Matt. 25:25 (14–30); D&C 60:13.talent, that every man may TG Work, Value of.gain other TG Talents.talents, yea, even an hundred fold, to be cast into the Lord’s D&C 42:33 (33–34, 55); 51:13; 119:1 (1–3).storehouse, to become the common D&C 42:30.property of the whole church—
1 Cor. 10:24.Every man seeking the interest of his TG Neighbor.neighbor, and doing all things with an Prov. 4:21; D&C 88:67. TG Motivations.eye single to the glory of God.
This order I have appointed to be an D&C 104:47.everlasting D&C 78:3 (3–6).order unto you, and unto your successors, inasmuch as you sin not.

And the soul that sins against this covenant, and TG Apostasy of Individuals; Hardheartedness.hardeneth his heart against it, shall be dealt with according to the laws of my church, and shall be delivered over to the D&C 78:12; 104:9 (8–10); 132:26.buffetings of Satan until the day of redemption.
And now, verily I say unto you, and this is wisdom, make unto yourselves friends with the Luke 16:9.mammon of unrighteousness, and they will not destroy you.
Leave judgment alone with me, for it is mine and I will Rom. 12:19; Morm. 3:15.repay. Peace be with you; my blessings continue with you.
For even yet the Luke 12:32; D&C 64:4. TG Kingdom of God, on Earth.kingdom is yours, and shall be forever, if you fall not from your TG Dedication; Dependability.steadfastness. Even so. Amen.

div.p: Revelation given through Joseph Smith the Prophet, at Independence, Missouri, April 30, 1832. This revelation was received as the Prophet sat in council with his brethren.
1–4, Women and children have claim upon their husbands and fathers for their support; 5–6, Widows and orphans have claim upon the Church for their support.

chapter.verse: Verily, thus saith the Lord, in addition to the D&C 51:8 (7–14).laws of the church concerning women and children, those who belong to the church, who have Deut. 10:18; Isa. 1:17 (16–17); James 1:27.lost their husbands or fathers:

D&C 75:25.Women have 1 Tim. 5:8 (8–16).claim on their husbands for their maintenance, until their TG Family, Patriarchal; Marriage, Husbands.husbands are taken; and if they are not found transgressors they shall have fellowship in the church.
And if they are not faithful they shall not have fellowship in the church; yet they may remain upon their inheritances according to the laws of the land.

And the storehouse shall be kept by the consecrations of the church; and Deut. 14:29; Mal. 3:5; Acts 4:35. TG Widows.widows and orphans shall be provided for, as also the Zech. 7:10; Mosiah 4:26 (16–26); Hel. 4:12; D&C 42:30 (30–39, 71).poor. Amen.

div.p: Revelation given through Joseph Smith the Prophet, at Kirtland, Ohio, September 22 and 23, 1832. During the month of September, elders had begun to return from their missions in the eastern states and to make reports of their labors. It was while they were together in this season of joy that the following communication was received. The Prophet designated it a revelation on priesthood.

1–5, The New Jerusalem and the temple will be built in Missouri; 6–17, The line of priesthood from Moses to Adam is given; 18–25, The greater priesthood holds the key of the knowledge of God; 26–32, The lesser priesthood holds the key of the ministering of angels and of the preparatory gospel; 33–44, Men gain eternal life through the oath and covenant of the priesthood; 45–53, The Spirit of Christ enlightens men, and the world lies in sin; 54–61, The Saints must testify of those things they have received; 62–76, They are to preach the gospel, and signs will follow; 77–91, Elders are to go forth without purse or scrip, and the Lord will care for their needs; 92–97, Plagues and cursings await those who reject the gospel; 98–102, The new song of the redemption of Zion is given; 103–10, Let every man stand in his own office and labor in his own calling; 111–20, The Lord’s servants are to proclaim the abomination of desolation of the last days.

And they shall not go hungry, neither athirst.

For your TG Man, a Spirit Child of Heavenly Father.Father, who is in heaven, Matt. 6:8, 32.knoweth that you have need of all these things.
Therefore, let the morrow take Matt. 6:34.thought for the things of itself.

Neither take ye thought beforehand Matt. 10:19 (19–20); Luke 12:11 (11–12); 21:14; D&C 100:6.what ye shall say; but Alma 17:2 (2–3); Hel. 5:18 (18–19); D&C 6:20; 11:26 (21–26); 24:6; 100:5 (5–8). TG Meditation; Mind; Motivations; Study.treasure up in your minds continually the words of life, and it shall be TG Teaching with the Spirit.given you in the very hour that portion that shall be meted unto every man.

Therefore, let no man among you, for this commandment is unto all the Matt. 24:45; D&C 58:26 (26–29); 107:100 (99–100).faithful who are called of God in the church unto the ministry, from this hour take purse or scrip, that goeth forth to proclaim this gospel of the kingdom.
Behold, I send you out to TG Missionary Work.reprove the world of all their unrighteous deeds, and to teach them of a judgment which is to come.

And whoso Matt. 10:40 (40–42); John 13:20; 3 Jn. 1:5 (1–14).receiveth you, there I will be also, for I will go Ex. 23:23; Lev. 26:12 (3–13); Judg. 4:14; Ether 1:42 (42–43).before your face. I will be on your right hand and on your left, and my TG God, Spirit of.Spirit shall be in your hearts, and mine D&C 84:42. TG Angels.angels round about you, to bear you up.
Whoso receiveth you receiveth me; and the same will feed you, and clothe you, and give you money.
And he who feeds you, or clothes you, or gives you money, shall in nowise Matt. 10:42; Mark 9:41.lose his reward.
And he that doeth not these things is not my disciple; by this you may know John 13:35.my disciples.

He that receiveth you not, go away from him alone by yourselves, and Matt. 10:14; Luke 9:5; D&C 60:15. TG Wash.cleanse your feet even with water, pure water, whether in heat or in cold, and bear testimony of it unto your Father which is in heaven, and return not again unto that man.
And in whatsoever village or city ye enter, do likewise.
Nevertheless, search diligently and spare not; and wo unto that house, or that village or city that rejecteth you, or your words, or your testimony concerning me.
Wo, I say again, unto that house, or that village or city that rejecteth you, or your words, or your testimony of me;
For I, the TG God, Power of.Almighty, have laid my hands upon the nations, to D&C 1:14 (13–14).scourge them for their TG Wickedness.wickedness.
And TG Plague.plagues shall go forth, and they shall not be taken from the earth until I have completed my work, which shall be cut Matt. 24:22.short in righteousness—

Until all shall TG God, Knowledge about.know me, who remain, even from the least unto the greatest, and shall be filled with the knowledge of the Lord, and shall Isa. 52:8.see eye to eye, and shall lift up their voice, and with the voice together Ps. 96:1; Rev. 15:3; D&C 25:12; 133:56. TG Singing.sing this new song, saying:

lgCT.l: The Lord hath brought again Zion;
The Lord hath Deut. 21:8; Ps. 25:22; Rom. 11:26 (25–28); D&C 43:29; 100:13; 136:18.redeemed his people, TG Israel, Restoration of.Israel,
According to the TG Election.election of TG Grace.grace,
Which was brought to pass by the faith
And TG Abrahamic Covenant.covenant of their fathers.
The Lord hath redeemed his people;
And Satan is Rev. 20:2 (2–3); D&C 43:31; 45:55; 88:110; 101:28.bound and Alma 40:8. TG Time.time is no longer.
The Lord hath gathered all things in Eph. 1:10; D&C 27:13.one.
The Lord hath brought down D&C 45:12 (11–14); Moses 7:63 (62–64). TG Zion.Zion from above.
The Lord hath D&C 76:102; 88:96.brought up Zion from beneath.
The TG Earth, Destiny of.earth hath travailed and Moses 7:64 (54–67).brought forth her strength;
And truth is established in her bowels;
And the heavens have smiled upon her;
And she is clothed with the TG Glory.glory of her God;
For he TG God, Presence of.stands in the midst of his Jer. 30:22.people.
Glory, and honor, and power, and might,
Be ascribed to our God; for he is full of TG God, Mercy of.mercy,
Justice, grace and truth, and TG Peace of God.peace,

chapter.verse: And again, verily, verily, I say unto you, it is expedient that every man who goes forth to proclaim mine everlasting gospel, that inasmuch as they have TG Family.families, and receive TG Family, Managing Finances in.money by gift, that they should send it unto them or make use of it for their benefit, as the Lord shall direct them, for thus it seemeth me good.

And let all those who have not families, who receive D&C 51:8 (8–13); 58:51 (49–51).money, send it up unto the bishop in Zion, or unto the bishop in Ohio, that it may be consecrated for the bringing forth of the revelations and the printing thereof, and for establishing Zion.
And if any man shall give unto any of you a coat, or a suit, take the old and cast it unto the TG Poor.poor, and go on your way rejoicing.

And if any man among you be TG Fellowshipping.strong in the Spirit, let him take with him him that is Rom. 14:1 (1–4, 10, 13).weak, that he may be TG Edification.edified in all TG Meek.meekness, that he may become strong also.

Therefore, take with you those who are ordained unto the TG Priesthood, Aaronic.lesser priesthood, and send them Luke 10:1 (1–12).before you to make appointments, and to prepare the way, and to fill appointments that you yourselves are not able to fill.
Behold, this is the way that mine apostles, in ancient days, built up my church unto me.

Therefore, let every man stand in his own TG Church Organization.office, and TG Priesthood, Magnifying Callings within.labor in his own calling; and let not the 1 Cor. 12:21.head say unto the feet it hath no need of the feet; for without the feet how shall the body be able to stand?
Also the body hath need of every TG Church.member, that all may be TG Edification.edified together, that the system may be kept perfect.

And behold, the TG High Priest, Melchizedek Priesthood.high priests should travel, and also the elders, and also the lesser TG Priest, Aaronic Priesthood.priests; but the TG Deacon.deacons and TG Teacher.teachers should be appointed to TG Watch.watch over the church, to be standing ministers unto the church.

And the bishop, Newel K. Whitney, also should travel round about and among all the churches, searching after the poor to TG Poor; Welfare.administer to their wants by D&C 56:16.humbling the rich and the proud.
He should also employ an D&C 51:8 (8–12); 53:4; 90:22.agent to take charge and to do his secular business as he shall direct.

Nevertheless, let the bishop go unto the city of New York, also to the city of Albany, and also to the city of Boston, and warn the people of those Matt. 11:20; Alma 34:31 (31–36); 3 Ne. 9:3.cities with the sound of the gospel, with a loud voice, of the D&C 1:13 (13–14).desolation and utter abolishment which await them if they do reject these things.
For if they do reject these things the hour of their judgment is nigh, and their house shall be left unto them Luke 13:35.desolate.

Let him Prov. 28:25 (25–26); Isa. 50:10; D&C 11:12. TG Trust in God.trust in me and he shall not be Ps. 22:5; 1 Pet. 2:6; D&C 49:27.confounded; and a Matt. 10:30; Luke 21:18.hair of his head shall not fall to the ground unnoticed.

And verily I say unto you, the rest of my servants, go ye forth as your circumstances shall permit, in your several callings, unto the great and notable cities and villages, Titus 1:13.reproving the world in righteousness of all their unrighteous and ungodly deeds, setting forth clearly and understandingly the desolation of TG Abomination of Desolation; Warn.abomination in the last days.

For, with you saith the Lord TG God, Power of.Almighty, I will Dan. 2:44 (34–35, 44–45).rend their TG Kings, Earthly.kingdoms; I will not only Joel 2:10; D&C 43:18; 45:33 (33, 48); 88:87 (87, 90). TG Last Days.shake the earth, but the D&C 21:6.starry heavens shall tremble.

For I, the Lord, have put forth my hand to exert the Matt. 24:29.powers of heaven; ye cannot see it now, yet a Heb. 10:37.little while and ye shall see it, and know that I am, and that Ex. 15:18.I will D&C 1:36 (12, 35–36); 29:11 (9–11); 45:59; 104:59. TG Jesus Christ, Millennial Reign; Jesus Christ, Second Coming.come and reign with my people.
I am D&C 35:1.Alpha and Omega, the beginning and the end. Amen.

div.p: Revelation given through Joseph Smith the Prophet, at Kirtland, Ohio, November 27, 1832. This section is an extract from a letter of the Prophet to William W. Phelps, who was living in Independence, Missouri. It answers questions about those Saints who had moved to Zion but who had not followed the commandment to consecrate their properties and had thus not received their inheritances according to the established order in the Church.
1–5, Inheritances in Zion are to be received through consecration; 6–12, One mighty and strong will give the Saints their inheritance in Zion.

chapter.verse: It is the duty of the Lord’s clerk, whom he has appointed, to keep a D&C 21:1; 47:1; 69:3 (3–8).history, and a general church TG Record Keeping.record of all things that transpire in Zion, and of all those who D&C 42:30 (30–35).consecrate properties, and receive D&C 51:3 (1–3); 90:30 (30–31).inheritances legally from the bishop;
And also their manner of life, their faith, and works; and also of the TG Apostasy of Individuals.apostates who apostatize after receiving their inheritances.

It is contrary to the will and commandment of God that those who receive not their Num. 34:13.inheritance by D&C 42:30. TG Consecration.consecration, agreeable to his D&C 70:10.law, which he has given, that he may TG Tithing.tithe his people, to prepare them against the day of Mal. 3:11 (11, 17); D&C 1:13 (13–14); 97:26.vengeance and burning, should have their Mal. 3:16; D&C 20:82.names enrolled with the people of God.
Neither is their Ezra 2:62 (62–63).genealogy to be kept, or to be had where it may be found on any of the records or history of the church.
Their names shall not be found, neither the names of the fathers, nor the names of the children written in the TG Book of Life; Record Keeping.book of the law of God, saith the Lord of Hosts.

Yea, thus saith the 1 Kgs. 19:12 (11–13); Job 4:16 (12–21); Hel. 5:30 (30–31); 3 Ne. 11:3 (3–6).still small voice, which whispereth through and Heb. 4:12.pierceth all things, and often times it maketh my bones to quake while it maketh manifest, saying:

And it shall come to pass that I, the Lord God, will send one mighty and strong, holding the scepter of power in his hand, clothed with light for a covering, whose mouth shall utter words, eternal words; while his bowels shall be a fountain of truth, to set in TG Order.order the house of God, and to arrange by Num. 33:54.lot the D&C 64:30; 99:7.inheritances of the saints whose names are found, and the names of their fathers, and of their children, enrolled in the book of the law of God;

While that man, who was called of God and appointed, that putteth forth his hand to Num. 1:51; D&C 64:17.steady the 2 Sam. 6:6 (6–7); 1 Chr. 13:10 (9–12). TG Ark of the Covenant.ark of God, shall fall by the shaft of death, like as a tree that is smitten by the vivid shaft of lightning.

And all they who are not found written in the 3 Ne. 24:16; Moses 6:5. TG Book of Remembrance.book of remembrance shall find none inheritance in that day, but they shall be cut asunder, and their portion shall be appointed them among TG Unbelief.unbelievers, where are D&C 19:5.wailing and gnashing of teeth.
These things I say not of D&C 1:38.myself; therefore, as the Lord speaketh, he will also fulfil.

And they who are of the High Priesthood, whose names are not found written in the TG Book of Life.book of the law, or that are found to have TG Apostasy of Individuals.apostatized, or to have been TG Excommunication; Worthiness.cut off from the church, as well as the lesser priesthood, or the members, in that day shall D&C 51:5 (4–5).not find an inheritance among the saints of the Most High;

Therefore, it shall be done unto them as unto the Ezra 2:61 (61–62); Neh. 7:63 (63–64).children of the TG Priesthood, Qualifying for.priest, as will be found recorded in the second chapter and sixty-first and second verses of Ezra.

div.p: Revelation given through Joseph Smith the Prophet, at Kirtland, Ohio, December 6, 1832. This revelation was received while the Prophet was reviewing and editing the manuscript of the translation of the Bible.
1–7, The Lord gives the meaning of the parable of the wheat and tares; 8–11, He explains priesthood blessings to those who are lawful heirs according to the flesh.

chapter.verse: Verily, thus saith the Lord unto you my servants, concerning the D&C 101:65 (64–75).parable of the Matt. 13:36 (6–43).wheat and of the tares:
Behold, verily I say, the field was the world, and the apostles were the Matt. 13:37; Mark 4:3. TG Mission of Early Saints.sowers of the seed;

And after they have fallen asleep the great persecutor of the church, the apostate, the TG Whore.whore, even Rev. 17:5 (1–9). TG Babylon.Babylon, that maketh all nations to drink of her cup, in whose hearts the enemy, even Satan, sitteth to reign—behold he soweth the TG Apostasy of the Early Christian Church.tares; wherefore, the tares choke the wheat and drive the Rev. 12:6 (6, 14).church into the wilderness.
But behold, in the TG Last Days.last days, even now while the Lord is beginning to bring forth the word, and the blade is springing up and is yet tender—
Behold, verily I say unto you, the D&C 38:12.angels are crying unto the Lord day and night, who are ready and waiting to be sent forth to TG Harvest.reap down the fields;
But the Lord saith unto them, pluck not up the tares while the blade is yet tender (for verily your faith is weak), lest you destroy the wheat also.

Therefore, let the wheat and the D&C 101:66.tares grow together until the harvest is fully ripe; then ye shall first gather out the wheat from among the tares, and after the gathering of the wheat, behold and lo, the tares are bound in bundles, and the field remaineth to be Matt. 13:40; Rev. 18:8 (6–8).burned.
Therefore, thus saith the Lord unto you, with whom the D&C 113:8. TG Priesthood, History of.priesthood hath continued through the lineage of your fathers—
For ye are lawful Abr. 2:9 (9–11). TG Abrahamic Covenant; Birthright; Seed of Abraham.heirs, according to the flesh, and have been Col. 3:3 (3–4).hid from the world with Christ in God—

Therefore your life and the TG Priesthood, Melchizedek.priesthood have remained, and must needs remain through you and your lineage until the Acts 3:21; D&C 132:45. TG Restoration of the Gospel.restoration of all things spoken by the mouths of all the holy prophets since the world began.

Therefore, blessed are ye if ye continue in my Ex. 34:6 (5–7); 2 Ne. 4:17; 9:10.goodness, a Isa. 49:6. TG Mission of Latter-day Saints; Peculiar People.light unto the Gentiles, and through this priesthood, a Obad. 1:21.savior unto my people Neh. 1:10; D&C 109:59 (59–67).Israel. The Lord hath said it. Amen.

div.p: Revelation and prophecy on war, given through Joseph Smith the Prophet, at or near Kirtland, Ohio, December 25, 1832. At this time disputes in the United States over slavery and South Carolina’s nullification of federal tariffs were prevalent. Joseph Smith’s history states that “appearances of troubles among the nations” were becoming “more visible” to the Prophet “than they had previously been since the Church began her journey out of the wilderness.”
1–4, War is foretold between the Northern States and the Southern States; 5–8, Great calamities will fall upon all the inhabitants of the earth.

chapter.verse: Verily, thus saith the Lord concerning the D&C 45:26.wars that will JS—H 1:41 (40–41).shortly come to pass, beginning at the rebellion of D&C 130:12 (12–13).South Carolina, which will eventually terminate in the death and misery of many souls;
And the TG Last Days.time will come that Joel 3:9 (9–16); Matt. 24:6 (6–7); D&C 38:29; 45:63 (26, 63); 63:33; 130:12.war will be poured out upon all nations, beginning at this place.

For behold, the Southern States shall be divided against the Northern States, and the Southern States will call on other nations, even the nation of Great Britain, as it is called, and they shall also call upon other nations, in order to defend themselves against other nations; and then D&C 45:69.war shall be poured out upon all nations.
And it shall come to pass, after many days, D&C 134:12.slaves shall rise up against their masters, who shall be marshaled and disciplined for war.

And it shall come to pass also that the Micah 5:8 (8–15); 3 Ne. 16:15 (7–15); 20:16 (15–21); 21:12 (12–21); D&C 109:65 (65–67). TG Israel, Remnant of.remnants who are left of the land will marshal themselves, and shall become exceedingly angry, and shall vex the Gentiles with a sore vexation.

And thus, with the Deut. 32:41; 2 Ne. 1:12.sword and by bloodshed the inhabitants of the earth shall D&C 29:15 (14–21); 45:49. TG Mourning.mourn; and with Joel 1:10; D&C 43:25 (24–25); JS—M 1:29. TG Drought; Famine; Plague.famine, and plague, and earthquake, and the thunder of heaven, and the fierce and vivid lightning also, shall the inhabitants of the earth be made to feel the wrath, and indignation, and TG Chastening.chastening Ether 1:1.hand of an Almighty God, until the consumption decreed hath made a full TG World, End of.end of all Mark 13:8; 1 Ne. 14:15.nations;

That the cry of the saints, and of the Rev. 6:10 (1, 10); 19:2; 2 Ne. 28:10; Morm. 8:27 (27, 40–41); Ether 8:22 (22–24).blood of the saints, shall cease to come up into the ears of the Lord of James 5:4; D&C 88:2; 95:7.Sabaoth, from the earth, to be avenged of their enemies.

Wherefore, Matt. 24:15; D&C 45:32; 101:22 (21–22, 64).stand ye in holy places, and be not moved, until the day of the Lord come; for behold, it cometh Rev. 3:11; D&C 1:12.quickly, saith the Lord. Amen.

div.p: Revelation given through Joseph Smith the Prophet at Kirtland, Ohio, December 27 and 28, 1832, and January 3, 1833. The Prophet designated it as the “‘olive leaf’ … plucked from the Tree of Paradise, the Lord’s message of peace to us.” The revelation was given after high priests at a conference prayed “separately and vocally to the Lord to reveal his will unto us concerning the upbuilding of Zion.”

1–5, Faithful Saints receive that Comforter, which is the promise of eternal life; 6–13, All things are controlled and governed by the Light of Christ; 14–16, The Resurrection comes through the Redemption; 17–31, Obedience to celestial, terrestrial, or telestial law prepares men for those respective kingdoms and glories; 32–35, Those who will to abide in sin remain filthy still; 36–41, All kingdoms are governed by law; 42–45, God has given a law unto all things; 46–50, Man will comprehend even God; 51–61, The parable of the man sending his servants into the field and visiting them in turn; 62–73, Draw near unto the Lord, and ye will see His face; 74–80, Sanctify yourselves and teach one another the doctrines of the kingdom; 81–85, Every man who has been warned should warn his neighbor; 86–94, Signs, upheavals of the elements, and angels prepare the way for the coming of the Lord; 95–102, Angelic trumps call forth the dead in their order; 103–16, Angelic trumps proclaim the restoration of the gospel, the fall of Babylon, and the battle of the great God; 117–26, Seek learning, establish a house of God (a temple), and clothe yourselves with the bond of charity; 127–41, The order of the School of the Prophets is set forth, including the ordinance of washing of feet.

chapter.verse: Verily, thus saith the Lord unto you who have assembled yourselves together to receive his will concerning you:

Behold, this is pleasing unto your Lord, and the angels Luke 15:7 (7–10).rejoice over you; the Acts 10:2 (1–4); D&C 112:1. TG Almsgiving; Prayer.alms of your prayers have come up into the ears of the Lord of James 5:4; D&C 87:7; 95:7.Sabaoth, and are recorded in the TG Book of Life.book of the names of the sanctified, even them of the celestial world.

Wherefore, I now send upon you another John 14:16.Comforter, even upon you my friends, that it may abide in your hearts, even the Holy Spirit of 2 Pet. 1:19; D&C 76:53; 132:19 (19, 26, 49).promise; which other Comforter is the same that I promised unto my disciples, as is recorded in the testimony of John.
This Comforter is the 1 Jn. 2:25.promise which I give unto you of D&C 14:7; 131:5.eternal life, even the TG Celestial Glory.glory of the celestial kingdom;
Which glory is that of the church of the D&C 77:11.Firstborn, even of God, the holiest of all, through Jesus Christ his Son—

He that Ps. 68:18; Eph. 4:8.ascended up on high, as also he D&C 122:8. TG Jesus Christ, Condescension of.descended below all things, in that he TG God, Omniscience of.comprehended all things, that he might be in all and through all things, the D&C 93:2 (2, 8–39). TG Light [noun]; Truth.light of truth;

Which truth shineth. This is the Moro. 7:19 (15–19); D&C 84:45; 93:36. TG God, Spirit of; Light of Christ.light of Christ. As also he is in the sun, and the light of the sun, and the power thereof by which it was Gen. 1:16. TG Creation; Jesus Christ, Creator; Jesus Christ, Power of.made.
As also he is in the moon, and is the light of the moon, and the power thereof by which it was made;
As also the light of the stars, and the power thereof by which they were made;
And the earth also, and the power thereof, even the earth upon which you Moses 2:1.stand.
And the light which shineth, which giveth you light, is through him who enlighteneth your eyes, which is the same light that quickeneth your TG Understanding.understandings;
Which 1 Tim. 6:16. TG Light of Christ.light proceedeth forth from the presence of God to Ps. 139:7 (7–12); Jer. 23:24.fill the immensity of space—

The Col. 1:17 (16–17).light which is in all things, which giveth Deut. 30:20; D&C 10:70. TG Jesus Christ, Creator.life to all things, which is the Job 38:33 (1–41); D&C 88:38 (36–38). TG God, Law of.law by which all things are governed, even the 2 Cor. 4:7. TG Jesus Christ, Power of.power of God who Ps. 47:8; Rev. 7:15.sitteth upon his throne, who is in the bosom of eternity, who is in the midst of all things.
Now, verily I say unto you, that through the TG Jesus Christ, Atonement through; Redemption; Resurrection.redemption which is made for you is brought to pass the resurrection from the dead.
And the TG Man, a Spirit Child of Heavenly Father; Spirit Body; Spirit Creation.spirit and the TG Body, Sanctity of.body are the Gen. 2:7; Ezek. 37:14 (6–14); Alma 40:23 (16–24). TG Soul.soul of man.
And the Alma 11:45 (40–45).resurrection from the dead is the redemption of the soul.

And the redemption of the soul is through him that 1 Tim. 6:13.quickeneth all things, in whose bosom it is decreed that the Luke 6:20; D&C 104:16. TG Poor.poor and the Zeph. 2:3 (1–3); Matt. 5:5; D&C 38:20. TG Meek.meek of the TG Earth, Destiny of.earth shall inherit it.
Therefore, it must needs be TG Earth, Purpose of.sanctified from all Gen. 6:11; Moses 8:28 (28–30).unrighteousness, that it may be prepared for the TG Celestial Glory.celestial glory;
For after it hath filled the measure of its creation, it shall be crowned with D&C 130:7 (7–9). TG Earth, Destiny of.glory, even with the presence of God the Father;

That bodies who are of the TG Earth, Purpose of.celestial kingdom may D&C 38:20.possess it forever and ever; for, for this Moses 1:39. TG Man, Potential to Become like Heavenly Father.intent was it made and created, and for this intent are they TG Man, New, Spiritually Reborn.sanctified.

And they who are not TG Sanctification.sanctified through the TG God, Law of.law which I have given unto you, even the law of Christ, must inherit D&C 43:33 (18, 33); 76:112 (102, 112).another kingdom, even that of a terrestrial kingdom, or that of a telestial kingdom.
For he who is not able to abide the D&C 105:5.law of a celestial kingdom cannot D&C 76:118.abide a TG Celestial Glory.celestial glory.
And he who cannot abide the law of a TG Terrestrial Glory.terrestrial kingdom cannot abide a terrestrial glory.

And he who cannot abide the law of a TG Telestial Glory.telestial D&C 76:44 (44–48).kingdom cannot abide a telestial Moses 1:13.glory; therefore he is not meet for a kingdom of glory. Therefore he must abide a kingdom which is not a kingdom of glory.
And again, verily I say unto you, the TG Earth, Destiny of.earth abideth the law of a celestial kingdom, for it filleth the 2 Ne. 2:12.measure of its creation, and transgresseth not the law—

Wherefore, it shall be TG Sanctification.sanctified; yea, notwithstanding it shall TG World, End of.die, it shall be TG Earth, Renewal of.quickened again, and shall abide the power by which it is quickened, and the 2 Pet. 3:13 (11–13). TG Righteousness.righteous shall Matt. 5:5; D&C 38:20; 45:58; 56:20; 57:5; 59:2; 63:20 (20, 48–49).inherit it.
For notwithstanding they die, they also shall TG Resurrection.rise again, a 1 Cor. 15:44.spiritual body.

They who are of a celestial TG Spirit Body.spirit shall receive the same TG Body, Sanctity of.body which was a natural body; even ye shall receive your bodies, and your TG Judgment, the Last.glory shall be that glory by which your bodies are 1 Cor. 15:35; D&C 43:32; 63:51; 101:31.quickened.
Ye who are D&C 76:70 (50–70). TG Celestial Glory.quickened by a portion of the TG Man, Potential to Become like Heavenly Father.celestial glory shall then receive of the same, even a fulness.
And they who are quickened by a portion of the D&C 76:71 (71–80). TG Terrestrial Glory.terrestrial glory shall then receive of the same, even a fulness.
And also they who are quickened by a portion of the D&C 76:81 (81–90, 98). TG Telestial Glory.telestial glory shall then receive of the same, even a fulness.

And they who remain shall also be Alma 11:41 (41–45); D&C 76:39.quickened; nevertheless, they shall return again to their own place, to enjoy that which they are TG Agency.willing to receive, because they were not willing to enjoy that which they might have received.

For what doth it profit a man if a gift is bestowed upon him, and he receive not the gift? Behold, he rejoices not in that which is given unto him, neither rejoices in him who is the giver of the gift.
And again, verily I say unto you, that which is TG Governments; Order.governed by law is also preserved by law and perfected and TG Sanctification.sanctified by the same.

That which TG Disobedience.breaketh a law, and TG Rebellion.abideth not by Prov. 28:9.law, but seeketh to become a law unto itself, and willeth to abide in sin, and altogether abideth in sin, cannot be sanctified by law, neither by mercy, TG God, Justice of.justice, nor TG Judgment.judgment. Therefore, they must remain Rev. 22:11; 1 Ne. 15:33 (33–35); 2 Ne. 9:16; Alma 7:21; Morm. 9:14. TG Filthiness.filthy still.
All kingdoms have a law given;

And there are many D&C 76:24; Moses 1:33.kingdoms; for there is no TG Astronomy.space in the which there is no TG Order.kingdom; and there is no kingdom in which there is no space, either a greater or a lesser kingdom.
And unto every kingdom is given a Job 38:33 (1–41); D&C 88:13. TG God, Law of.law; and unto every law there are certain bounds also and conditions.
All beings who abide not in those D&C 130:21 (20–21).conditions are not TG Justification.justified.

For TG Intelligence.intelligence cleaveth unto intelligence; TG Wisdom.wisdom receiveth wisdom; TG Truth.truth embraceth truth; TG Chastity; Virtue.virtue loveth virtue; TG Light [noun].light cleaveth unto light; TG Mercy.mercy hath TG Compassion.compassion on mercy and claimeth her own; TG God, Justice of; Justice.justice continueth its course and claimeth its own; judgment goeth before the face of him who sitteth upon the throne and governeth and executeth all things.

He Job 22:13 (13–14); Ps. 94:9 (7–10); D&C 38:2; Moses 1:35 (35–37); 7:36; Abr. 3:2 (1–16). TG God, Intelligence of; God, Omniscience of; God, Perfection of.comprehendeth all things, and all things are before him, and all things are round about him; and he is above all things, and in all things, and is through all things, and is round about all things; and all things are by him, and of him, even God, forever and ever.

And again, verily I say unto you, he hath given a Dan. 2:21 (19–22, 28); Abr. 3:9. TG God, Law of; Order.law unto all things, by which they move in their Abr. 3:4 (4–19). TG Nature, Earth; Time.times and their seasons;
And their courses are fixed, even the courses of the heavens and the earth, which comprehend the earth and all the planets.

And they give TG Light [noun].light to each other in their times and in their seasons, in their minutes, in their hours, in their days, in their weeks, in their months, in their years—all these are Ps. 90:4; 2 Pet. 3:8.one year with God, but not with man.

The earth Hel. 12:15 (8–15).rolls upon her wings, and the Gen. 1:16; D&C 76:71 (70–71); Abr. 4:16.sun giveth his light by day, and the moon giveth her light by night, and the stars also give their light, as they roll upon their wings in their glory, in the midst of the D&C 88:13 (7–13).power of God.
Unto what shall I liken these kingdoms, that ye may understand?

Behold, all these are D&C 88:61.kingdoms, and any man who hath Moses 1:27 (27–28); 7:23; Abr. 3:21 (21–23).seen any or the least of these hath Alma 30:44; Moses 6:63.seen God D&C 76:114.moving in his majesty and power.
I say unto you, he hath seen him; nevertheless, he who came unto his John 1:11.own was not comprehended.

The D&C 6:21; 50:24 (23–24); 67:9; 84:45 (45–47).light shineth in darkness, and the darkness comprehendeth it not; nevertheless, the day shall come when you shall John 17:3; D&C 93:28; 101:32 (32–34). TG Revelation.comprehend even God, being quickened in him and by him.
Then shall ye know that ye have John 14:7.seen me, that I am, and that I am the true Dan. 2:22. TG Light of Christ.light that is in you, and that you are in me; otherwise ye could not abound.
Behold, I will liken these kingdoms unto a man having a field, and he sent forth his servants into the field to dig in the field.
And he said unto the first: Go ye and labor in the field, and in the first hour I will come unto you, and ye shall behold the joy of my countenance.
And he said unto the second: Go ye also into the field, and in the second hour I will visit you with the joy of my countenance.
And also unto the third, saying: I will visit you;
And unto the fourth, and so on unto the twelfth.
And the lord of the field went unto the first in the first hour, and tarried with him all that hour, and he was made glad with the light of the countenance of his lord.
And then he withdrew from the first that he might visit the second also, and the third, and the fourth, and so on unto the twelfth.
And thus they all received the light of the countenance of their lord, every man in his hour, and in his time, and in his season—
Beginning at the first, and so on unto the Matt. 20:8 (1–16).last, and from the last unto the first, and from the first unto the last;

Every man in his own TG Order.order, until his hour was finished, even according as his lord had commanded him, that his lord might be glorified in him, and he in his lord, that they all might be glorified.

Therefore, unto this parable I will liken all these D&C 88:47.kingdoms, and the D&C 76:24.inhabitants thereof—every kingdom in its hour, and in its time, and in its season, even according to the decree which God hath made.

And again, verily I say unto you, my Ex. 33:11; Ether 12:39; D&C 84:63; 93:45.friends, I leave these Deut. 6:6.sayings with you to TG Meditation.ponder in your hearts, with this commandment which I give unto you, that ye shall Isa. 55:6; James 1:5 (5–6); D&C 46:7.call upon me while I am near—

Ps. 69:18; Zech. 1:3 (3–4); James 4:8; Rev. 3:20 (20–21). TG God, Access to; God, Presence of.Draw Deut. 4:7; Lam. 3:57; Ezek. 36:9 (8–15).near unto me and I will draw near unto you; 1 Chr. 28:9; Ezra 8:22 (22–23); Ether 12:41; D&C 101:38; Abr. 2:12.seek me diligently and ye shall 2 Chr. 15:15; D&C 4:7; 49:26.find me; ask, and ye shall receive; knock, and it shall be opened unto you.
Whatsoever ye TG Communication; Prayer.ask the Father in my name it shall be given unto you, that is D&C 18:18.expedient for you;
And if ye ask anything that is not Rom. 8:26 (26–27); James 4:3; D&C 46:28 (28–30).expedient for you, it shall turn unto your D&C 63:11 (7–12).condemnation.

Behold, that which you hear is as the Ps. 95:7; Isa. 40:3; 1 Ne. 17:13; Alma 5:37 (37–38); D&C 65:3; 97:1; 128:20.voice of one crying in the wilderness—in the wilderness, because you cannot see him—my voice, because my voice is TG God, Spirit of.Spirit; my Spirit is truth; TG Truth.truth abideth and hath no end; and if it be in you it shall abound.

And if your eye be Matt. 6:22; D&C 82:19. TG Dedication; Motivations; Priesthood, Magnifying Callings within.single to my John 7:18.glory, your whole bodies shall be filled with light, and there shall be no darkness in you; and that body which is filled with light Prov. 28:5; D&C 93:28. TG Discernment, Spiritual.comprehendeth all things.

Therefore, TG Man, New, Spiritually Reborn; Sanctification.sanctify yourselves that your TG Mind.minds become Luke 11:34 (34–36). TG Commitment.single to God, and the days will come that you shall Lev. 9:4; D&C 67:10 (10–12); 93:1; 97:16. TG God, Privilege of Seeing.see him; for he will unveil his face unto you, and it shall be in his own time, and in his own way, and according to his own will.

Remember the great and last promise which I have made unto you; cast away your Matt. 12:36; Alma 12:14.idle thoughts and your TG Rioting and Reveling; Temperance.excess of 1 Pet. 4:3; D&C 59:15; 88:121. TG Laughter; Levity.laughter far from you.
Tarry ye, tarry ye in this place, and call a D&C 88:117 (117–19); 124:39. TG Solemn Assembly.solemn assembly, even of those who are the first TG Missionary Work.laborers in this last kingdom.
And let those whom they have warned in their traveling call on the Lord, and D&C 101:78. TG Agency.ponder the TG Warn.warning in their hearts which they have received, for a little season.
Behold, and lo, I will take care of your TG Church; Sheep; Shepherd.flocks, and will raise up elders and send unto them.
Behold, I will Isa. 60:22 (1–22).hasten my work in its time.

And I give unto you, who are the first Matt. 9:37; 20:1.laborers in this last kingdom, a commandment that you assemble yourselves together, and organize yourselves, and prepare yourselves, and Lev. 20:7 (7–8); 2 Chr. 35:6; Neh. 12:30; 3 Ne. 19:28 (28–29); D&C 50:29 (28–29); 133:62.sanctify yourselves; yea, purify your hearts, and D&C 88:139; 124:37. TG Cleanliness; Wash.cleanse your hands and your feet before me, that I may make you Ether 12:37; D&C 135:5 (4–5).clean;

That I may testify unto your TG Man, a Spirit Child of Heavenly Father.Father, and your God, and my TG Jesus Christ, Relationships with the Father.God, that you are clean from the Acts 2:40; D&C 88:138.blood of this wicked generation; that I may fulfil this promise, this great and last TG Promise.promise, which I have made unto you, when I will.
Also, I give unto you a commandment that ye shall continue in 4 Ne. 1:12; Moro. 6:5. TG Fast, Fasting; Prayer.prayer and fasting from this time forth.
And I give unto you a commandment that you shall Ex. 35:34; Moro. 10:9 (9–10); D&C 38:23; 43:8; 107:85 (85–89). TG Teaching.teach one another the Titus 2:1.doctrine of the kingdom.

Teach ye diligently and my TG Grace.grace shall attend you, that you may be D&C 88:118; 90:15; 93:53. TG Education.instructed more perfectly in theory, in principle, in doctrine, in the law of the gospel, in all things that pertain unto the kingdom of God, that are expedient for you to understand;

Of things both in TG Heaven.heaven and in the earth, and under the earth; things which have been, things which are, things which must Rev. 1:1; 22:6.shortly come to pass; things which are at home, things which are abroad; the wars and the perplexities of the TG Nations; War.nations, and the judgments which are on the land; and a TG Knowledge.knowledge also of countries and of kingdoms—

That ye may be prepared in all things when I shall send you again to TG Priesthood, Magnifying Callings within.magnify the calling whereunto I have called you, and the TG Mission of Latter-day Saints.mission with which I have commissioned you.

Behold, I sent you out to TG Testimony.testify and warn the people, and it becometh every man who hath been warned to Ex. 18:20; Ezra 7:25; Ezek. 33:6 (6–9); D&C 63:58. TG Warn; Watchman.warn his neighbor.
Therefore, they are left Rom. 1:20 (17–21).without excuse, and their sins are upon their Acts 18:6.own heads.
He that Deut. 4:29 (29–31); Isa. 26:9; Jer. 29:13 (10–14); D&C 54:10.seeketh me TG Procrastination.early shall find me, and shall not be forsaken.

Therefore, tarry ye, and labor diligently, that you may be perfected in your ministry to go forth among the Isa. 42:6; D&C 133:37 (37–60); JS—H 1:41.Gentiles for the last time, as many as the mouth of the Lord shall name, to Isa. 8:16 (16–17).bind up the law and TG Seal.seal up the testimony, and to prepare the saints for the hour of judgment which is to come;

That their souls may escape the wrath of God, the Dan. 9:27; Matt. 24:15. TG Abomination of Desolation.desolation of abomination which awaits the wicked, both in this world and in the world to come. Verily, I say unto you, let those who are not the D&C 20:2 (2, 5); 105:7 (7, 33).first elders continue in the vineyard until the mouth of the Lord shall D&C 11:15.call them, for their time is not yet come; their garments are not Lev. 6:11 (10–11); 1 Ne. 12:10 (10–11); Jacob 1:19; 2:2; D&C 4:2; 112:33. TG Purification.clean from the blood of this generation.

Abide ye in the 1 Cor. 7:22; Mosiah 5:8. TG Liberty.liberty wherewith ye are made John 8:36; Alma 61:9 (9, 21). TG Agency.free; Gal. 5:1.entangle not yourselves in TG Bondage, Spiritual; Sin.sin, but let your hands be Job 17:9; 2 Ne. 25:16. TG Chastity; Cleanliness; Purification; Worthiness.clean, until the Lord comes.

For not many days hence and the Isa. 13:13 (4–13); 14:26 (22–26).earth shall D&C 43:18; 45:33; 84:118; 133:22.tremble and reel to and fro as a drunken man; and the Joel 2:10; D&C 45:42; 133:49.sun shall D&C 29:14.hide his face, and shall refuse to give light; and the moon shall be bathed in Rev. 6:12.blood; and the stars shall become exceedingly angry, and shall Joel 3:15; Rev. 8:12.cast themselves down as a fig that falleth from off a fig tree.
And after your TG Testimony.testimony cometh wrath and indignation upon the people.

For after your testimony cometh the testimony of Rev. 11:13 (13–14); D&C 45:33.earthquakes, that shall cause groanings in the midst of her, and men shall fall upon the ground and shall not be able to stand.

And also cometh the testimony of the Joel 2:11; Rev. 8:5; D&C 35:21; 43:18 (17–25); 133:50 (50–52).voice of thunderings, and the voice of lightnings, and the voice of tempests, and the voice of the waves of the sea heaving themselves beyond their bounds.
And all things shall be in D&C 45:26; 63:33.commotion; and surely, men’s Luke 21:26. TG Fearful; Last Days.hearts shall fail them; for fear shall come upon all people.

And Rev. 8:13; D&C 43:18 (18, 25); 133:17.angels shall fly through the midst of heaven, crying with a loud voice, sounding the trump of God, saying: Prepare ye, prepare ye, O inhabitants of the earth; for the TG Jesus Christ, Judge.judgment of our God is come. Behold, and lo, the Matt. 25:6; D&C 33:17; 133:19 (10, 19).Bridegroom cometh; go ye out to meet him.
And immediately there shall appear a Matt. 24:30; Luke 21:25 (25–27). TG Signs.great sign in heaven, and all people shall see it together.

And another angel shall sound his trump, saying: That 1 Ne. 13:6 (4–9).great TG Devil, Church of.church, the Rev. 17:5 (5–15).mother of abominations, that made all nations drink of the wine of the wrath of her Rev. 14:8.fornication, that TG Hate; Persecution.persecuteth the saints of God, that shed their blood—she who sitteth upon many waters, and upon the islands of the sea—behold, she is the Matt. 13:38; D&C 64:24 (23–24); 101:24 (23–25). TG Earth, Cleansing of.tares of the earth; she is bound in bundles; her bands are made strong, no man can loose them; therefore, she is ready to be burned. And he shall sound his trump both long and loud, and all nations shall hear it.

And there shall be D&C 38:12.silence in D&C 133:40 (40–46).heaven for the space of half an hour; and immediately after shall the curtain of heaven be unfolded, as a Rev. 6:14.scroll is unfolded after it is rolled up, and the TG Jesus Christ, Second Coming.face of the Lord shall be unveiled;
And the saints that are upon the earth, who are alive, shall be quickened and be 1 Thes. 4:16 (16–17); D&C 76:102; 84:100; 101:31.caught up to meet him.

And they who have slept in their graves shall 1 Thes. 2:19; D&C 29:13; 45:45 (45–46); 133:56. TG Resurrection.come forth, for their graves shall be opened; and they also shall be caught up to meet him in the midst of the D&C 78:21.pillar of heaven—

They are Christ’s, the 1 Cor. 15:23 (1–58).first fruits, they who shall descend with him first, and they who are on the earth and in their graves, who are first caught up to meet him; and all this by the voice of the sounding of the trump of the angel of God.

And after this another angel shall sound, which is the second trump; and then cometh the redemption of those who are Christ’s at his TG Jesus Christ, Second Coming.coming; who have received their part in that D&C 76:73 (71–74); 138:8; Moses 7:57. TG Salvation for the Dead; Spirits in Prison.prison which is prepared for them, that they might receive the gospel, and be 1 Pet. 4:6.judged according to men in the flesh.

And again, another trump shall sound, which is the third trump; and then come the Rev. 20:13 (12–13); Alma 11:41; D&C 43:18; 76:85.spirits of men who are to be judged, and are found under TG Damnation.condemnation;
And these are the rest of the Rev. 20:5.dead; and they live not again until the TG Millennium.thousand years are ended, neither again, until the end of the earth.

And another trump shall sound, which is the fourth trump, saying: There are found among those who are to remain until that great and last day, even the end, who shall D&C 43:18.remain TG Filthiness.filthy still.

And another trump shall sound, which is the fifth trump, which is the fifth angel who committeth the Rev. 14:6 (6–7). TG Restoration of the Gospel.everlasting gospel—flying through the midst of heaven, unto all nations, kindreds, tongues, and people;

And this shall be the sound of his trump, saying to all people, both in heaven and in earth, and that are under the earth—for Ps. 86:9; Rev. 5:13.every ear shall hear it, and every knee shall Ps. 66:4; 72:9 (9–10); Mal. 1:11; Philip. 2:10 (9–11).bow, and every tongue shall confess, while they hear the sound of the trump, saying: Rev. 14:7. TG Reverence.Fear God, and give glory to him who sitteth upon the throne, Heb. 1:8.forever and ever; for the hour of his judgment is come.
