And again, another angel shall sound his trump, which is the sixth angel, saying: She is Rev. 14:8; D&C 1:16.fallen who made all nations drink of the wine of the wrath of her fornication; she is fallen, is fallen!

And again, another angel shall sound his trump, which is the seventh angel, saying: It is finished; it is finished! The TG Jesus Christ, Lamb of God.Lamb of God hath 1 Cor. 15:25.overcome and Isa. 63:3 (3–4); Rev. 14:20 (15–20); 19:15; D&C 76:107; 133:50 (46–53).trodden the wine-press alone, even the wine-press of the fierceness of the wrath of Almighty God.

And then shall the angels be crowned with the glory of his might, and the TG Angels; Saints.saints shall be filled with his TG Celestial Glory.glory, and receive their TG Exaltation.inheritance and be made John 5:18; D&C 76:95.equal with him.

And then shall the first angel again sound his trump in the ears of all living, and Ps. 64:5 (4–6); Alma 37:25; D&C 1:3.reveal the secret acts of men, and the mighty works of God in the D&C 77:6 (6–7, 12).first thousand years.

And then shall the second angel sound his trump, and reveal the secret acts of men, and the thoughts and intents of their hearts, and the mighty TG God, Works of.works of God in the second thousand years—

And so on, until the seventh angel shall sound his trump; and he shall Rev. 10:5.stand forth upon the land and upon the sea, and Dan. 12:7.swear in the name of him who sitteth upon the throne, that there shall be D&C 84:100.time no longer; and Rev. 20:2 (1–10); 1 Ne. 22:26; D&C 101:28. TG Devil.Satan shall be bound, that old serpent, who is called the devil, and shall not be loosed for the space of a TG Millennium.thousand years.
And then he shall be Rev. 20:3 (3–10); Jacob 5:77 (76–77); D&C 29:22; 43:31 (30–31).loosed for a little season, that he may gather together his armies.
And TG Adam.Michael, the seventh angel, even the archangel, shall gather together his armies, even the hosts of heaven.
And the devil shall gather together his Rev. 20:8.armies; even the hosts of hell, and shall come up to battle against Michael and his armies.

And then cometh the Rev. 16:14.battle of the great God; and the devil and his armies shall be Rom. 16:20; 1 Jn. 3:8; Rev. 20:10 (9–10); D&C 19:3; 29:28 (27–30, 44).cast away into their own place, that they shall not have power over the saints any more at all.
For Michael shall fight their battles, and shall overcome him who Isa. 14:14 (12–15); Moses 4:1 (1–4).seeketh the throne of him who sitteth upon the throne, even the Lamb.
This is the glory of God, and the TG Saints; Sanctification.sanctified; and they shall not any more see Rev. 21:4; Alma 11:45; 12:18 (18, 20); D&C 63:49. TG Immortality.death.
Therefore, verily I say unto you, my D&C 109:6.friends, call your solemn assembly, as I have D&C 88:70 (70–75).commanded you.

And as all have not TG Faith.faith, seek ye diligently and Prov. 9:9; 23:12; D&C 88:78 (76–80); 90:15; 93:53. TG Education; Learn; Study; Teaching.teach one another words of TG Wisdom.wisdom; yea, seek ye out of the best D&C 55:4; 97:3 (3–6); 109:7 (7, 14).books words of wisdom; seek learning, even by study and also by faith.

TG Family, Managing Finances in; Priesthood, Magnifying Callings within.Organize yourselves; prepare every needful thing; and establish a Hag. 1:8; D&C 95:3; 97:12 (10–17); 109:8 (2–9); 115:8. TG Temple.house, even a house of prayer, a house of fasting, a house of faith, a house of learning, a house of glory, a house of order, a house of God;

That your Ezek. 43:11 (11–12).incomings may be in the name of the Lord; that your outgoings may be in the name of the Lord; that all your salutations may be in the name of the Lord, with Ps. 63:4; 134:2 (1–3).uplifted hands unto the Most High.

Therefore, D&C 43:34; 84:61; 100:7.cease from all your light speeches, from all D&C 59:15; 88:69.laughter, from all your TG Carnal Mind; Lust; Sensuality.lustful desires, from all your TG Levity; Pride.pride and light-mindedness, and from all your wicked doings.

Appoint among yourselves a teacher, and let TG Order.not all be spokesmen at once; but let one speak at a time and let all listen unto his sayings, that when all have spoken that all may be TG Edification.edified of all, and that every man may have an equal privilege.
See that ye TG Love.love one another; cease to be TG Covet.covetous; learn to impart one to another as the gospel requires.

Cease to be TG Idleness; Laziness.idle; cease to be TG Uncleanness.unclean; cease to Isa. 29:20; D&C 64:8 (7–10). TG Backbiting; Gossip.find fault one with another; cease to TG Sleep.sleep longer than is needful; retire to thy bed early, that ye may not be weary; arise early, that your bodies and your minds may be TG Health.invigorated.
And above all things, clothe yourselves with the bond of TG Charity.charity, as with a mantle, which is the bond of perfectness and TG Peace; Peacemakers.peace.
TG Prayer.Pray always, that ye may not faint, until I Rev. 22:7 (7, 20); D&C 1:12.come. Behold, and lo, I will come quickly, and receive you unto myself. Amen.

And again, the order of the house prepared for the D&C 90:13.presidency of the D&C 90:7; 95:10 (10, 17); 97:5 (5–6). TG Education.school of the 2 Kgs. 2:3 (3–15).prophets, established for their instruction in all things that are expedient for them, even for all the Num. 11:16; D&C 107:21.officers of the church, or in other words, those who are called to the ministry in the church, beginning at the high priests, even down to the deacons—

And this shall be the order of the house of the presidency of the school: He that is appointed to be president, or teacher, shall be found standing in his place, in the house which shall be prepared for him.
Therefore, he shall be first in the house of God, in a place that the congregation in the house may hear his words carefully and distinctly, not with loud speech.
And when he cometh into the house of God, for he should be first in the house—behold, this is Isa. 52:7.beautiful, that he may be an TG Example.example—
Let him offer himself in prayer upon his knees before God, in TG New and Everlasting Covenant; Prayer; Worship.token or remembrance of the everlasting covenant.
And when any shall come in after him, let the teacher arise, and, with Lev. 9:22.uplifted hands to heaven, yea, even directly, salute his brother or brethren with these words:

Art thou a brother or brethren? I salute you in the name of the Lord Jesus Christ, in token or remembrance of the everlasting covenant, in which covenant I receive you to 2 Cor. 8:4. TG Fellowshipping.fellowship, in a determination that is fixed, immovable, and unchangeable, to be your TG Friendship.friend and TG Brotherhood and Sisterhood.brother through the grace of God in the bonds of love, to walk in all the commandments of God blameless, in thanksgiving, forever and ever. Amen.
And he that is found Jer. 7:10 (9–10).unworthy of this salutation shall not have place among you; for ye shall not suffer that mine house shall be D&C 97:15 (15–17); 110:8 (7–8).polluted by him.

And he that cometh in and is faithful before me, and is a brother, or if they be brethren, they shall salute the president or teacher with uplifted hands to heaven, with this same prayer and covenant, or by saying Amen, in token of the same.
Behold, verily, I say unto you, this is an ensample unto you for a salutation to one another in the house of God, in the school of the prophets.

And ye are called to do this by prayer and thanksgiving, as the Spirit shall give utterance in all your doings in the house of the Lord, in the school of the prophets, that it may become a sanctuary, a tabernacle of the Holy Spirit to your TG Edification; Worship.edification.
And ye shall not receive any among you into this school save he is clean from the D&C 88:75 (75, 85).blood of this generation;
And he shall be received by the ordinance of the D&C 88:74. TG Wash.washing of feet, for unto this end was the ordinance of the washing of feet instituted.
And again, the ordinance of washing feet is to be administered by the president, or presiding elder of the church.

It is to be commenced with prayer; and after partaking of TG Sacrament.bread and wine, he is to gird himself according to the John 13:5 (4–17).pattern given in the thirteenth chapter of John’s testimony concerning me. Amen.

div.p: Revelation given through Joseph Smith the Prophet, at Kirtland, Ohio, February 27, 1833. As a consequence of the early brethren using tobacco in their meetings, and how that concerned his wife, Emma Smith, the Prophet inquired of the Lord concerning it. This revelation, known as the Word of Wisdom, was the result.

1–9, The use of wine, strong drinks, tobacco, and hot drinks is proscribed; 10–17, Herbs, fruits, flesh, and grain are ordained for the use of man and of animals; 18–21, Obedience to gospel law, including the Word of Wisdom, brings temporal and spiritual blessings.

chapter.verse: A TG Word of Wisdom. Word of Wisdom, for the benefit of the council of high priests, assembled in Kirtland, and the church, and also the saints in Zion—

To be sent greeting; not by commandment or constraint, but by revelation and the 1 Cor. 12:8; D&C 84:44.word of wisdom, showing forth the order and D&C 29:34. TG Commandments of God; God, Will of.will of God in the temporal salvation of all saints in the last days—

Given for a principle with Eph. 2:12; 6:2; D&C 89:18 (18–21).promise, adapted to the capacity of the 2 Cor. 12:10; D&C 1:19. TG Humility.weak and the weakest of all TG Saints.saints, who are or can be called saints.

Behold, verily, thus saith the Lord unto you: In consequence of TG Deceit; Evil.evils and designs which do and will exist in the hearts of TG Conspiracy; Wickedness.conspiring men in the last days, I have TG Warn.warned you, and forewarn you, by giving unto you this word of wisdom by revelation—

That inasmuch as any man TG Abstain.drinketh Lev. 10:9 (9–11); Isa. 5:22 (11, 22); Rom. 14:21 (20–23); D&C 27:3. TG Drunkenness; Temperance; Word of Wisdom.wine or strong drink among you, behold it is not good, neither meet in the sight of your Father, only in assembling yourselves together to offer up your sacraments before him.
And, behold, this should be wine, yea, D&C 27:3 (1–14).pure wine of the grape of the vine, of your own make.
And, again, Prov. 20:1; 23:30 (29–35); Luke 1:15.strong drinks are not for the belly, but for the washing of your bodies.

And again, tobacco is not for the TG Body, Sanctity of; Health.body, neither for the belly, and is not good for man, but is an herb for bruises and all sick cattle, to be used with judgment and skill.
And again, hot drinks are not for the body or belly.
And again, verily I say unto you, all wholesome IE plants. Gen. 1:29; D&C 59:17 (17–20).herbs God hath ordained for the constitution, nature, and use of man—
Every herb in the season thereof, and every fruit in the season thereof; all these to be used with TG Prudence; Temperance.prudence and 1 Tim. 4:3 (3–4). TG Thanksgiving.thanksgiving.

Yea, Gen. 9:3; Lev. 11:2 (1–8). TG Meat.flesh also of D&C 49:19.beasts and of the fowls of the air, I, the Lord, have ordained for the use of man with thanksgiving; nevertheless they are to be used TG Temperance.sparingly;
And it is pleasing unto me that they should not be D&C 59:20 (16–20).used, only in times of winter, or of cold, or TG Famine.famine.

All Dan. 1:12 (6–20). BD Corn.grain is ordained for the use of man and of beasts, to be the staff of life, not only for man but for the beasts of the field, and the fowls of heaven, and all wild animals that run or creep on the earth;
And D&C 49:18; 89:13.these hath God made for the use of man only in times of famine and excess of hunger.
All grain is good for the TG Food.food of man; as also the Gen. 1:29.fruit of the vine; that which yieldeth fruit, whether in the ground or above the ground—

Nevertheless, wheat for man, and corn for the ox, and oats for the horse, and rye for the fowls and for swine, and for all beasts of the field, and barley for all useful animals, and for mild drinks, as also other grain.

And all saints who remember to keep and do these sayings, walking in obedience to the commandments, D&C 89:3.shall receive Prov. 3:8; Dan. 1:13 (6–20). TG Health.health in their navel and marrow to their bones;
And shall D&C 84:80.find TG Wisdom.wisdom and great TG Treasure.treasures of Dan. 1:17 (6–20). TG Knowledge; Testimony.knowledge, even hidden treasures;
And shall Prov. 4:12; Isa. 40:31. TG Strength.run and not be Prov. 24:10 (10–12); D&C 84:80.weary, and shall walk and not faint.
And I, the Lord, give unto them a promise, that the TG Protection, Divine.destroying angel shall Ex. 12:23 (23, 29).pass by them, as the children of Israel, and not slay them. Amen.

div.p: Revelation to Joseph Smith the Prophet, given at Kirtland, Ohio, March 8, 1833. This revelation is a continuing step in the establishment of the First Presidency (see the heading to section 81); as a consequence thereof, the counselors mentioned were ordained on March 18, 1833.

1–5, The keys of the kingdom are committed to Joseph Smith and through him to the Church; 6–7, Sidney Rigdon and Frederick G. Williams are to serve in the First Presidency; 8–11, The gospel is to be preached to the nations of Israel, to the Gentiles, and to the Jews, every man hearing in his own tongue; 12–18, Joseph Smith and his counselors are to set the Church in order; 19–37, Various individuals are counseled by the Lord to walk uprightly and serve in His kingdom.

chapter.verse: Thus saith the Lord, verily, verily I say unto you my son, thy sins are TG Forgive.forgiven thee, according to thy petition, for thy prayers and the prayers of thy brethren have come up into my ears.
Therefore, thou art blessed from henceforth that bear the TG Priesthood, Keys of.keys of the kingdom given unto you; which TG Jesus Christ, Prophecies about.kingdom is coming forth for the last time.
Verily I say unto you, the keys of this Matt. 21:43; D&C 65:2.kingdom shall D&C 43:3 (3–4); 84:17; 122:9; 124:130.never be taken from you, while thou art in the world, neither in the world to come;
Nevertheless, through you shall the Acts 7:38; Rom. 3:2; Heb. 5:12; D&C 124:39, 126. TG Prophets, Mission of.oracles be given to another, yea, even unto the church.

And all they who receive the Acts 7:38. TG Prophets, Rejection of.oracles of God, let them beware how they hold them lest they are accounted as a light thing, and are brought under condemnation thereby, and stumble and fall when the storms descend, and the winds blow, and the Matt. 7:25.rains descend, and beat upon their house.

And again, verily I say unto thy brethren, Sidney Rigdon and D&C 92:1.Frederick G. Williams, their sins are forgiven them also, and they are accounted as D&C 35:22 (3–23); 81:1 (1–7); 107:24 (22–24).equal with thee in holding the keys of this last kingdom;
As also through your administration the keys of the D&C 88:127.school of the prophets, which I have commanded to be organized;
That thereby they may be Eph. 4:12 (11–13).perfected in their ministry for the salvation of Zion, and of the nations of Israel, and of the Gentiles, as many as will believe;

That through your administration they may receive the word, and through their administration the word may go forth unto the ends of the earth, unto the 1 Ne. 13:42; D&C 18:6 (6, 26–27); 19:27; 21:12; 107:33; 112:4; 133:8.Gentiles Matt. 19:30; Acts 13:46 (46–51); Ether 13:12 (10–12).first, and then, behold, and lo, they shall turn unto the Jews.

And then cometh the day when the arm of the Lord shall be D&C 42:58 (58–60); 43:25 (23–27); 58:64 (63–64); 88:84 (84, 87–92); 133:37 (37–60).revealed in power in convincing the nations, the Ps. 98:2; 1 Ne. 15:13. TG Heathen.heathen nations, the house of Gen. 49:22 (22–26); Deut. 33:17 (13–17); Hosea 14:8 (4–9); D&C 133:26 (26–30).Joseph, of the gospel of their salvation.

For it shall come to pass in that day, that every man shall TG Missionary Work.hear the fulness of the gospel in his own tongue, and in his own TG Language.language, through those who are TG Called of God.ordained unto this TG Priesthood, Power of.power, by the administration of the TG Holy Ghost, Comforter.Comforter, shed forth upon them for the TG Testimony.revelation of Jesus Christ.
And now, verily I say unto you, I give unto you a commandment that you continue in the TG Leadership; Service.ministry and presidency.
And when you have finished the D&C 73:4 (3–4).translation of the prophets, you shall from thenceforth D&C 88:127 (127–38); 90:32 (32–33); 107:91.preside over the affairs of the church and the school;
And from time to time, as shall be manifested by the Comforter, receive TG Revelation.revelations to unfold the TG Mysteries of Godliness.mysteries of the kingdom;

And set in order the churches, and D&C 88:78 (76–80), 118; 93:53. TG Education; Scriptures, Study of; Study.study and Mal. 2:7 (7–9); D&C 107:99 (99–100); 131:6. TG Learn.learn, and become acquainted with all good books, and with D&C 90:11. TG Language.languages, tongues, and people.
And this shall be your business and mission in all your lives, to preside in council, and set in Titus 1:5. TG Order.order all the affairs of this church and kingdom.

Be not Micah 3:7 (6–7); 2 Ne. 6:13 (7, 13). TG Shame.ashamed, neither confounded; but be admonished in all your high-mindedness and D&C 88:121. TG Pride.pride, for it bringeth a snare upon your souls.
Set in TG Order.order your houses; keep TG Apathy; Idleness; Laziness; Procrastination.slothfulness and D&C 94:9; 97:15. TG Uncleanness.uncleanness far from you.
Now, verily I say unto you, let there be a D&C 41:7 (7–8).place provided, as soon as it is possible, for the family of thy counselor and scribe, even Frederick G. Williams.
And let mine aged servant, D&C 124:19.Joseph Smith, Sen., continue with his family upon the place where he now lives; and let it not be sold until the mouth of the Lord shall name.
And let my counselor, even D&C 93:51.Sidney Rigdon, remain where he now resides until the mouth of the Lord shall name.
And let the bishop search diligently to obtain an D&C 84:113.agent, and let him be a man who has got Jacob 2:19 (17–19).riches in store—a man of God, and of strong faith—
That thereby he may be enabled to discharge every debt; that the storehouse of the Lord may not be brought into disrepute before the eyes of the people.

Search TG Dedication; Diligence.diligently, TG Prayer.pray always, and be believing, and Deut. 23:5; Ezra 8:22; Rom. 8:28; D&C 97:18 (18–20); 100:15.all things shall work together for your good, if ye walk uprightly and remember the Ps. 132:12. TG Commitment; Covenants.covenant wherewith ye have covenanted one with another.
Let your families be Mosiah 4:27 (26–27).small, especially mine aged servant Joseph Smith’s, Sen., as pertaining to those who do not belong to your families;
That those things that are provided for you, to bring to pass my work, be not taken from you and given to those that are not worthy—
And thereby you be hindered in accomplishing those things which I have commanded you.
And again, verily I say unto you, it is my will that my handmaid Vienna Jaques should receive D&C 60:10 (10–11).money to bear her expenses, and go up unto the land of Zion;
And the residue of the money may be consecrated unto me, and she be rewarded in mine own due time.
Verily I say unto you, that it is meet in mine eyes that she should go up unto the land of Zion, and receive an D&C 51:3 (1–3); 85:1 (1–3).inheritance from the hand of the bishop;
That she may settle down in peace inasmuch as she is faithful, and not be idle in her days from thenceforth.

And behold, verily I say unto you, that ye shall TG Record Keeping; Scriptures, Writing of.write this commandment, and say unto your brethren in Zion, in love greeting, that I have called you also to D&C 90:13; 107:91.preside over Zion in mine own due time.
Therefore, let them cease wearying me concerning this matter.
Behold, I say unto you that your brethren in Zion begin to repent, and the angels rejoice over them.

Nevertheless, I am not well pleased with many things; and I am not well pleased with my servant D&C 66:1; 68:7; 75:6.William E. McLellin, neither with my servant Sidney Gilbert; and the bishop also, and others have many things to repent of.

But verily I say unto you, that I, the Lord, will contend with TG Zion.Zion, and plead with her strong ones, and D&C 84:58. TG Chastening.chasten her until she overcomes and is TG Cleanliness; Purification.clean before me.
For she shall not be removed out of her place. I, the Lord, have spoken it. Amen.

div.p: Revelation given through Joseph Smith the Prophet, at Kirtland, Ohio, March 9, 1833. The Prophet was at this time engaged in the translation of the Old Testament. Having come to that portion of the ancient writings called the Apocrypha, he inquired of the Lord and received this instruction.
1–3, The Apocrypha is mostly translated correctly but contains many interpolations by the hands of men that are not true; 4–6, It benefits those enlightened by the Spirit.

chapter.verse: Verily, thus saith the Lord unto you concerning the BD Apocrypha.Apocrypha—There are many things contained therein that are true, and it is mostly translated correctly;
There are many things contained therein that are not true, which are TG Record Keeping; Scriptures, Preservation of.interpolations by the hands of men.
Verily, I say unto you, that it is not needful that the Apocrypha should be D&C 45:60 (60–61); 93:53.translated.
Therefore, whoso readeth it, let him TG Understanding.understand, for the Spirit manifesteth truth;
And whoso is enlightened by the TG Holy Ghost, Mission of; Inspiration.Spirit shall obtain benefit therefrom;
And whoso receiveth not by the Spirit, cannot be benefited. Therefore it is not needful that it should be translated. Amen.

div.p: Revelation given to Joseph Smith the Prophet, at Kirtland, Ohio, March 15, 1833. The revelation instructs Frederick G. Williams, who had recently been appointed a counselor to Joseph Smith, on his duties in the United Firm (see the headings to sections 78 and 82).
1–2, The Lord gives a commandment relative to admission to the united order.

chapter.verse: Verily, thus saith the Lord, I give unto the D&C 82:11 (11, 15–21); 96:8 (6–9).united order, organized agreeable to the commandment previously given, a revelation and commandment concerning my servant D&C 90:6.Frederick G. Williams, that ye shall receive him into the order. What I say unto one I say unto all.

And again, I say unto you my servant Frederick G. Williams, you shall be a lively member in this order; and inasmuch as you are faithful in keeping all former commandments you shall be blessed forever. Amen.

div.p: Revelation given through Joseph Smith the Prophet, at Kirtland, Ohio, May 6, 1833.

1–5, All who are faithful will see the Lord; 6–18, John bore record that the Son of God went from grace to grace until He received a fulness of the glory of the Father; 19–20, Faithful men, going from grace to grace, will also receive of His fulness; 21–22, Those who are begotten through Christ are the Church of the Firstborn; 23–28, Christ received a fulness of all truth, and man by obedience may do likewise; 29–32, Man was in the beginning with God; 33–35, The elements are eternal, and man may receive a fulness of joy in the Resurrection; 36–37, The glory of God is intelligence; 38–40, Children are innocent before God because of the redemption of Christ; 41–53, The leading brethren are commanded to set their families in order.

chapter.verse: Verily, thus saith the Lord: It shall come to pass that every soul who Rom. 12:1 (1–3).forsaketh his TG Worthiness.sins and cometh unto me, and Joel 2:32.calleth on my name, and TG Obedience.obeyeth my voice, and keepeth my commandments, shall Lev. 9:4; John 14:23 (18, 21–23); D&C 38:8; 67:10 (10–12); 88:68; 101:23; 130:3. TG God, Presence of; Jesus Christ, Appearances, Postmortal.see my 1 Jn. 4:12 (7–21); JST 1 Jn. 4:12 (1 Jn. 4:12 note a). TG God, Privilege of Seeing.face and TG God, Access to; God, Knowledge about.know that I am;
And that I am the true John 1:4 (4, 7–9); D&C 14:9; 84:46 (45–47); 88:6. TG Light of Christ.light that lighteth every man that cometh into the world;
And that I am John 10:30 (28–31); 14:10; 17:22; D&C 50:43.in the Father, and the Father in me, and the Father and I are one—

The Father Mosiah 15:3 (2–5).because he TG Jesus Christ, Authority of; Jesus Christ, Relationships with the Father.gave me of his fulness, and the Son because I was in the world and made TG Flesh and Blood; Jesus Christ, Birth of; Jesus Christ, Condescension of.flesh my 2 Pet. 1:13.tabernacle, and dwelt among the sons of men.
I was in the world and received of my Father, and the John 5:36; 10:25; 14:10 (10–12).works of him were plainly manifest.
And John 1:34.John saw and bore record of the fulness of my TG Jesus Christ, Glory of.glory, and the fulness of John 20:30 (30–31).John’s record is hereafter to be revealed.
And he bore record, saying: I saw his glory, that he was in the John 1:1 (1–3, 14); D&C 76:39.beginning, before the world was;
Therefore, in the beginning the TG Jesus Christ, Jehovah; Jesus Christ, Messenger of the Covenant; Salvation.Word was, for he was the Word, even the messenger of salvation—

The TG Jesus Christ, Creator; Jesus Christ, Light of the World; Jesus Christ, Mission of.light and the Redeemer of the world; the Spirit of truth, who came into the world, because the world was made by him, and in him was the life of men and the light of men.
The worlds were John 1:3 (1–3); Rom. 11:36; Heb. 1:2 (1–3); D&C 76:24.made by him; men were made by him; all things were made by him, and through him, and of him.

And I, John, John 1:14 (14, 32).bear record that I beheld his TG Jesus Christ, Glory of.glory, as the glory of the Only Begotten of the Father, full of grace and truth, even the Spirit of truth, which came and dwelt in the flesh, and dwelt among us.
And I, John, saw that he received not of the Philip. 2:8 (6–9); Heb. 5:8 (8–9).fulness at the first, but received John 1:16.grace for grace;
And he received not of the fulness at first, but continued from Luke 2:52. TG Grace.grace to grace, until he received a fulness;
And thus he was called the Matt. 1:16 (1–16); Luke 20:41. TG Jesus Christ, Divine Sonship.Son of God, because he received not of the fulness at the first.

And I, John 1:32 (15–32). TG Holy Ghost, Dove, Sign of.John, bear record, and lo, the heavens were opened, and the Holy Ghost descended upon him in the form of a dove, and sat upon him, and there came a voice out of heaven saying: This is my TG Witness of the Father.beloved Son.
And I, John, bear record that he received a fulness of the glory of the Father;

And he received Matt. 28:18; John 17:2; 1 Cor. 15:27; Heb. 1:2; 1 Pet. 3:22; D&C 84:28.all TG God, Power of; God the Father, Elohim; Jesus Christ, Authority of; Jesus Christ, Power of.power, both in heaven and on earth, and the glory of the Father was with him, for he dwelt in him.
And it shall come to pass, that if you are faithful you shall receive the TG Scriptures to Come Forth.fulness of the record of John.

I give unto you these sayings that you may understand and know how to worship, and Luke 4:8; John 4:22 (5–26); Acts 17:23 (22–25); Alma 21:22 (21–22); D&C 43:8 (8–9); 134:4 (1–4); A of F 1:11. TG Discernment, Spiritual; Worship.know what you worship, that you may come unto the Father in my name, and in due time receive of his fulness.

For if you keep my Deut. 8:1 (1–2).commandments you shall receive of his John 1:16.fulness, and be John 17:22 (5, 22). TG Man, Potential to Become like Heavenly Father.glorified in me as I am in the Father; therefore, I say unto you, you shall receive Isa. 28:13 (9–13). TG Grace.grace for grace.

And now, verily I say unto you, I was in the TG Jesus Christ, Foreordained; Jesus Christ, Relationships with the Father.beginning with the Father, and am the TG Jesus Christ, Divine Sonship; Jesus Christ, Firstborn.Firstborn;
And all those who are begotten through me are 1 Pet. 5:1; D&C 133:57.partakers of the TG Celestial Glory.glory of the same, and are the D&C 76:54 (53–54).church of the Firstborn.
Ye were also in the beginning with the Father; that which is TG Man, a Spirit Child of Heavenly Father; Spirit Creation.Spirit, even the Spirit of truth;
And Ps. 117:2. TG Truth.truth is TG Knowledge.knowledge of things as they are, and as they were, and as they are to come;
And whatsoever is D&C 20:35; 68:34.more or less than this is the spirit of that wicked one who was a John 8:44; Moses 4:4 (1–4).liar from the beginning.

The Spirit of John 14:6. TG God, Intelligence of.truth is of God. I am the Spirit of truth, and John bore record of me, saying: He John 1:16 (16–17).received a fulness of truth, yea, even of all truth;
And no man receiveth a TG Abundant Life; Perfection.fulness unless he keepeth his commandments.

He that TG Obedience.keepeth his commandments receiveth TG Truth.truth and Matt. 6:22 (22–23); D&C 50:24.light, until he is glorified in truth and John 17:3; 2 Pet. 1:4; D&C 88:67 (49, 67); 101:32 (32–34). TG Mysteries of Godliness.knoweth all things.

Man was also in the Prov. 8:23; Abr. 3:18. TG Man, Antemortal Existence of; Man, Potential to Become like Heavenly Father.beginning with God. TG God, Intelligence of; Intelligence.Intelligence, or the TG Light [noun].light of TG Truth.truth, was not TG Creation.created or made, neither indeed can be.
All truth is independent in that D&C 77:3.sphere in which God has placed it, to 2 Ne. 2:14 (13–26).act for itself, as all intelligence also; otherwise there is no existence.

Behold, here is the TG Agency.agency of man, and here is the condemnation of man; because that which was from the beginning is Deut. 30:11 (11–14); 1 Ne. 20:16; D&C 84:23.plainly manifest unto them, and they receive not the light.
And every man whose spirit receiveth not the TG Conscience; Ignorance.light is under condemnation.

For man is John 4:24; D&C 77:2; Abr. 5:7 (7–8). TG Man, a Spirit Child of Heavenly Father; Spirit Body; Spirit Creation.spirit. The elements are TG Eternity.eternal, and D&C 138:17. TG Eternal Life; Joy; Resurrection.spirit and element, inseparably connected, receive a fulness of joy;
And when 2 Ne. 9:8 (8–10). TG Spirits, Disembodied.separated, man cannot receive a fulness of joy.

The Gen. 2:7; Morm. 9:17; D&C 77:12.elements are the 2 Pet. 1:13; D&C 88:41 (12, 41, 45).tabernacle of God; yea, man is the tabernacle of God, even TG Body, Sanctity of.temples; and whatsoever temple is Mark 7:15; 1 Cor. 3:17 (16–17); Titus 1:15; 2 Ne. 19:17.defiled, God shall destroy that temple.
The TG God, Glory of; Jesus Christ, Glory of.glory of God is D&C 130:18; Abr. 3:19. TG God, Intelligence of; Intelligence.intelligence, or, in other words, D&C 88:7 (6–13).light and truth.
Light and truth forsake that 2 Ne. 30:18; Moses 1:15 (12–16). TG Devil.evil one.

Every TG Spirit Body.spirit of man was TG Conceived in Sin; Purity.innocent in the beginning; and God having Ps. 49:15; Mosiah 27:24 (24–26); Moses 5:9; A of F 1:3. TG Redemption.redeemed man from the TG Fall of Man.fall, men became again, in their infant state, Moro. 8:12 (8, 12, 22); D&C 29:46. TG Justification; Salvation of Little Children; Sanctification.innocent before God.

And that TG Devil.wicked one cometh and Matt. 13:19 (18–19); 2 Cor. 4:4 (3–4); Mosiah 27:9 (8–9); Alma 12:10 (9–11); D&C 50:7; 84:50 (49–53).taketh away light and truth, through TG Disobedience.disobedience, from the children of men, and because of the Jer. 16:19; Ezek. 20:18; Alma 3:8. TG Traditions of Men.tradition of their fathers.
But I have commanded you to bring up your TG Family, Children, Responsibilities toward; Marriage, Fatherhood; Marriage, Motherhood.children in TG Children of Light.light and truth.
But verily I say unto you, my servant Frederick G. Williams, you have continued under this condemnation;

You have not Ex. 10:2 (1–2); 1 Sam. 3:13 (11–13); D&C 68:25 (25–31); Moses 6:58.taught your children light and truth, according to the commandments; and that wicked one hath power, as yet, over you, and this is the cause of your TG Accountability.affliction.

And now a commandment I give unto you—if you will be delivered you shall set in Esth. 1:22; 1 Tim. 3:5 (4–5). TG Order.order your own house, for there are many things that are not right in your house.
Verily, I say unto my servant Sidney Rigdon, that in some things he hath not kept the commandments concerning his children; therefore, first set in order thy house.

Verily, I say unto my servant Joseph Smith, Jun., or in other words, I will call you Ex. 33:11; D&C 84:63; 88:62; 94:1. TG Friendship.friends, for you are my friends, and ye shall have an inheritance with me—
I called you Lev. 25:55; 1 Ne. 21:3 (3–6). TG Servant.servants for the world’s sake, and ye are their servants for my sake—
And now, verily I say unto Joseph Smith, Jun.—You have not kept the commandments, and must needs stand D&C 95:1 (1–2). TG Chastening.rebuked before the Lord;
Your TG Family, Children, Duties of.family must needs repent and forsake some things, and give more earnest heed unto your sayings, or be removed out of their place.
What I say unto one I say unto all; 3 Ne. 18:15 (15–21).pray always lest that wicked one have power in you, and remove you out of your place.

My servant Newel K. Whitney also, a bishop of my church, hath need to be TG Reproof; Warn.chastened, and set in TG Order.order his family, and see that they are more TG Diligence.diligent and concerned at home, and pray always, or they shall be removed out of their D&C 64:40.place.

Now, I say unto you, my friends, let my servant Sidney Rigdon go on his journey, and make haste, and also proclaim the Luke 4:19; 2 Cor. 6:2.acceptable year of the Lord, and the Eph. 1:13.gospel of salvation, as I shall give him utterance; and by your prayer of faith with one consent I will uphold him.

And let my servants Joseph Smith, Jun., and Frederick G. Williams make haste also, and it shall be given them even according to the prayer of faith; and inasmuch as you keep my sayings you shall not be confounded in this world, nor in the world to come.

And, verily I say unto you, that it is my will that you should D&C 45:60 (60–61); 73:3; 76:15; 91:3; 94:10.hasten to IE the translation of the Bible.translate my scriptures, and to D&C 88:78 (76–80), 118; 90:15.obtain a TG Education; Knowledge; Learn.knowledge of history, and of countries, and of kingdoms, of TG God, Law of.laws of God and man, and all this for the salvation of Zion. Amen.

div.p: Revelation given through Joseph Smith the Prophet, at Kirtland, Ohio, August 2, 1833, outlining the layout of Kirtland, a stake of Zion, and appointing Hyrum Smith, Reynolds Cahoon, and Jared Carter as a Church building committee.
1–9, The Lord gives a commandment relative to the erection of a house for the work of the Presidency; 10–12, A printing house is to be built; 13–17, Certain inheritances are assigned.

chapter.verse: And again, verily I say unto you, my D&C 93:45.friends, a commandment I give unto you, that ye shall commence a work of laying out and preparing a beginning and foundation of the city of the D&C 68:26; 82:13; 96:1; 104:48; 109:59.stake of Zion, here in the land of Kirtland, beginning at my house.
And behold, it must be done according to the Heb. 8:5; D&C 52:14; 95:14; 97:10; 115:14 (14–16).pattern which I have given unto you.

And let the first lot on the south be consecrated unto me for the building of a house for the presidency, for the work of the presidency, in obtaining revelations; and for the work of the ministry of the D&C 107:9 (8–78).presidency, in all things pertaining to the church and kingdom.
Verily I say unto you, that it shall be built fifty-five by sixty-five feet in the width thereof and in the length thereof, in the inner court.
And there shall be a lower court and a higher court, according to the pattern which shall be given unto you hereafter.

And it shall be TG Dedication; Sacred.dedicated unto the Lord from the foundation thereof, according to the 1 Chr. 6:32.order of the priesthood, according to the pattern which shall be given unto you hereafter.
And it shall be wholly dedicated unto the Lord for the work of the D&C 107:9 (8–78).presidency.

And ye shall not suffer any Luke 19:46 (46–48); D&C 109:20 (19–20). TG Uncleanness.unclean thing to come in unto it; and my 1 Kgs. 8:11 (10–11); Ezek. 43:2; D&C 101:25. TG Glory.glory shall be there, and my TG God, Presence of.presence shall be there.
But if there shall come into it any Lev. 15:31; D&C 90:18; 97:15.unclean thing, my glory shall not be there; and my presence shall not come into it.

And again, verily I say unto you, the second lot on the south shall be dedicated unto me for the building of a house unto me, for the work of the D&C 104:58. TG Scriptures, Writing of.printing of the IE the translation of the Bible. D&C 93:53; 124:89.translation of my scriptures, and all things whatsoever I shall command you.
And it shall be fifty-five by sixty-five feet in the width thereof and the length thereof, in the inner court; and there shall be a lower and a higher court.

And this house shall be wholly dedicated unto the Lord from the foundation thereof, for the work of the printing, in all things whatsoever I shall command you, to be holy, undefiled, according to the pattern in all things as it shall be given unto you.
And on the third lot shall my servant Hyrum Smith receive his TG Inheritance. See also “Inherit” in Index.inheritance.
And on the first and second lots on the north shall my servants Reynolds Cahoon and Jared Carter receive their inheritances—
That they may do the work which I have appointed unto them, to be a committee to build mine houses, according to the commandment, which I, the Lord God, have given unto you.
These two houses are not to be built until I give unto you a commandment concerning them.
And now I give unto you no more at this time. Amen.

div.p: Revelation given through Joseph Smith the Prophet, at Kirtland, Ohio, June 1, 1833. This revelation is a continuation of divine directions to build a house for worship and instruction, the house of the Lord (see section 88:119–36).

1–6, The Saints are chastened for their failure to build the house of the Lord; 7–10, The Lord desires to use His house to endow His people with power from on high; 11–17, The house is to be dedicated as a place of worship and for the school of the Apostles.

chapter.verse: Verily, thus saith the Lord unto you whom I love, and whom I 2 Sam. 7:14; Prov. 13:18; Heb. 12:6 (5–12); Hel. 15:3; D&C 101:4 (4–5); 103:4. TG Chastening; God, Love of.love I also chasten that their sins may be TG Forgive.forgiven, for with the Deut. 11:2 (1–8); D&C 105:6.chastisement I prepare a way for their 2 Sam. 14:14; 1 Cor. 10:13. TG Deliver; Forgive.deliverance in all things out of TG Temptation.temptation, and I have loved you—
Wherefore, ye must needs be chastened and stand rebuked before my face;

For ye have sinned against me a very grievous sin, in that ye have not considered the great commandment in all things, that I have given unto you concerning the building of mine Hag. 1:8; D&C 88:119.house;

For the preparation wherewith I design to prepare mine apostles to Jacob 5:61 (61–75); D&C 24:19; 33:3 (3–4); 39:17.prune my vineyard for the last time, that I may bring to pass my Isa. 28:21; D&C 101:95.strange act, that I may Prov. 1:23; Ezek. 36:27; Joel 2:28; Acts 2:17; D&C 19:38. TG Holy Ghost, Gifts of.pour out my Spirit upon all flesh—

But behold, verily I say unto you, that there are many who have been ordained among you, whom I have called but few of them are Matt. 20:16; D&C 63:31; 105:36 (35–36); 121:34 (34–40). TG Called of God; Election; Worthiness.chosen.
They who are not chosen have sinned a very grievous sin, in that they are TG Walking in Darkness.walking in Deut. 28:29 (15–45). TG Darkness, Spiritual.darkness at noon-day.

And for this cause I gave unto you a commandment that you should call your D&C 88:117 (70–119). TG Solemn Assembly.solemn assembly, that your TG Fast, Fasting.fastings and your TG Mourning; Repent.mourning might come up into the ears of the Lord of James 5:4; D&C 87:7; 88:2.Sabaoth, which is by interpretation, the TG Jesus Christ, Creator.creator of the first day, the beginning and the end.

Yea, verily I say unto you, I gave unto you a commandment that you should D&C 88:119; 104:43; 115:8.build a house, in the which house I design to D&C 38:32; 39:15; 43:16; 110:9 (9–10). TG Genealogy and Temple Work.endow those whom I have TG Election.chosen with power from on high;
For this is the Luke 24:49; Acts 1:4.promise of the Father unto you; therefore I command you to tarry, even as mine apostles at Jerusalem.

Nevertheless, my servants sinned a very grievous sin; and D&C 88:134 (133–34). TG Contention; Disputations.contentions arose in the D&C 88:127; 97:5 (5–6).school of the prophets; which was very grievous unto me, saith your Lord; therefore I sent them forth to be chastened.
Verily I say unto you, it is my will that you should build a house. Hag. 1:8 (2–11).If you keep my commandments you shall have power to build it.

If you John 15:10; Mosiah 2:4.keep not my commandments, the 1 Jn. 2:10 (10, 15).love of the Father shall not continue with you, therefore you shall 2 Pet. 2:17. TG Walking in Darkness.walk in darkness.
Now here is wisdom, and the D&C 68:4.mind of the Lord—let the house be built, not after the manner of the world, for I give not unto you that ye shall live after the manner of the world;
Therefore, let it be built after the D&C 94:2; 124:42.manner which I shall show unto three of you, whom ye shall appoint and ordain unto this power.
And the size thereof shall be fifty and five feet in width, and let it be sixty-five feet in length, in the inner court thereof.

And let the lower part of the inner court be dedicated unto me for your sacrament offering, and for your preaching, and your fasting, and your praying, and the D&C 59:9 (9–14).offering up of your most holy desires unto me, saith your Lord.

And let the higher part of the inner court be dedicated unto me for the D&C 88:127 (127–41).school of mine apostles, saith Son D&C 78:20.Ahman; or, in other words, Alphus; or, in other words, Omegus; even Jesus Christ your TG Jesus Christ, Lord.Lord. Amen.

div.p: Revelation given to Joseph Smith the Prophet, June 4, 1833. The occasion was a conference of high priests, and the chief subject of consideration was the disposal of certain lands, known as the French farm, possessed by the Church near Kirtland. Since the conference could not agree who should take charge of the farm, all agreed to inquire of the Lord concerning the matter.
1, The Kirtland Stake of Zion is to be made strong; 2–5, The bishop is to divide the inheritances for the Saints; 6–9, John Johnson is to be a member of the united order.

chapter.verse: Behold, I say unto you, here is wisdom, whereby ye may know how to act concerning this matter, for it is expedient in me that this Isa. 33:20; 54:2; D&C 94:1; 104:48.stake that I have set for the strength of Zion should be made strong.
Therefore, let my servant D&C 72:8 (7–8).Newel K. Whitney take charge of the place which is named among you, upon which I design to build mine 2 Chr. 3:8.holy house.
And again, let it be divided into lots, according to wisdom, for the benefit of those who seek D&C 38:20.inheritances, as it shall be determined in council among you.
Therefore, take heed that ye see to this matter, and that portion that is necessary to benefit mine D&C 78:3.order, for the purpose of bringing forth my word to the children of men.

For behold, verily I say unto you, this is the most expedient in me, that my word should go forth unto the children of men, for the purpose of subduing the hearts of the children of men for your good. Even so. Amen.

And again, verily I say unto you, it is wisdom and expedient in me, that my servant D&C 102:3 (3, 34).John Johnson whose offering I have accepted, and whose prayers I have heard, unto whom I give a promise of eternal life inasmuch as he keepeth my commandments from henceforth—
For he is a descendant of Gen. 49:26 (22–26); Rev. 7:8; 1 Ne. 5:14 (14–16); D&C 133:32.Joseph and a partaker of the blessings of the promise made unto his fathers—
Verily I say unto you, it is expedient in me that he should become a member of the D&C 92:1; 104:1 (1, 47–53).order, that he may assist in bringing forth my word unto the children of men.

Therefore ye shall ordain him unto this blessing, and he shall seek diligently to take away D&C 90:23 (22–23).incumbrances that are upon the house named among you, that he may dwell therein. Even so. Amen.

div.p: Revelation given through Joseph Smith the Prophet, at Kirtland, Ohio, August 2, 1833. This revelation deals particularly with the affairs of the Saints in Zion, Jackson County, Missouri, in response to the Prophet’s inquiry of the Lord for information about a school of the prophets in Missouri. Members of the Church in Missouri were at this time subjected to severe persecution and, on July 23, 1833, had been forced to sign an agreement to leave Jackson County.

1–2, Many of the Saints in Zion (Jackson County, Missouri) are blessed for their faithfulness; 3–5, Parley P. Pratt is commended for his labors in the school in Zion; 6–9, Those who observe their covenants are accepted by the Lord; 10–17, A house is to be built in Zion in which the pure in heart will see God; 18–21, Zion is the pure in heart; 22–28, Zion will escape the Lord’s scourge if she is faithful.

chapter.verse: Verily I say unto you my friends, I speak unto you with my TG Revelation; Wisdom.voice, even the voice of my Spirit, that I may show unto you my will concerning your brethren in the land of D&C 64:26 (18–41); 66:6.Zion, many of whom are truly humble and are seeking diligently to learn wisdom and to find truth.

Verily, verily I say unto you, blessed are such, for they shall obtain; for I, the Lord, show mercy unto all the Matt. 5:5.meek, and upon all whomsoever I will, that I may be Ps. 51:4.justified when I shall bring them unto judgment.

Behold, I say unto you, concerning the school in Zion, I, the Lord, am well pleased that there should be a D&C 55:4; 88:118; 109:7 (7, 14).school in Zion, and also with my servant D&C 32:1; 50:37; 52:26; 103:30 (30–37).Parley P. Pratt, for he abideth in me.
And inasmuch as he continueth to abide in me he shall continue to preside over the school in the land of Zion until I shall give unto him other commandments.
And I will bless him with a multiplicity of blessings, in expounding all scriptures and mysteries to the edification of the D&C 88:127.school, and of the church in Zion.
And to the residue of the school, I, the Lord, am willing to show mercy; nevertheless, there are those that must needs be TG Chastening.chastened, and their works shall be made known.

The Matt. 3:10; 7:19; Luke 3:9; 6:44; Alma 5:52 (36–52); 3 Ne. 14:19.ax is laid at the root of the trees; and every tree that bringeth not forth good fruit shall be hewn down and cast into the fire. I, the Lord, have spoken it.

Verily I say unto you, all among them who know their hearts are TG Contrite Heart; Honesty.honest, and are broken, and their spirits contrite, and are Ex. 25:2 (1–7); D&C 64:34.willing to observe their covenants by TG Commitment; Sacrifice.sacrifice—yea, every sacrifice which I, the Lord, shall command—they are Gen. 4:7; D&C 52:15; 132:50; Moses 5:23.accepted of me.
For I, the Lord, will cause them to bring forth as a very fruitful Ps. 1:3 (1–3); Jer. 17:8.tree which is planted in a goodly land, by a pure stream, that yieldeth much precious fruit.

Verily I say unto you, that it is my will that a D&C 57:3; 58:57; 84:3 (3–5, 31); 88:119; 124:31 (25–51).house should be built unto me in the land of Zion, like unto the Num. 8:4; D&C 52:14; 94:2; 115:14 (14–16).pattern which I have given you.
Yea, let it be built speedily, by the tithing of my people.
Behold, this is the TG Tithing.tithing and the TG Sacrifice.sacrifice which I, the Lord, require at their hands, that there may be a 1 Kgs. 5:5.house built unto me for the salvation of Zion—

For a place of 2 Chr. 5:13. TG Thanksgiving.thanksgiving for all saints, and for a place of instruction for all those who are called to the work of the ministry in all their several callings and offices;

That they may be perfected in the TG Understanding.understanding of their ministry, in theory, in principle, and in doctrine, in all things pertaining to the TG Kingdom of God, on Earth.kingdom of God on the earth, the TG Priesthood, Keys of.keys of which kingdom have been TG Delegation of Responsibility.conferred upon you.

And inasmuch as my people 2 Chr. 2:4.build a TG Temple.house unto me in the 1 Kgs. 8:29.name of the Lord, and do not suffer any D&C 94:9; 109:20; 110:8 (7–8).unclean thing to come into it, that it be not defiled, my 2 Chr. 5:14 (13–14); Hag. 2:7; D&C 84:5 (4–5, 31). TG Glory.glory shall rest upon it;

Yea, and my Lev. 16:2; D&C 124:27. TG God, Presence of.presence shall be there, for I will come into it, and all the Matt. 5:8; D&C 67:10; 88:68. TG God, Knowledge about; God, Privilege of Seeing; Purity.pure in heart that shall come into it shall see God.
But if it be defiled I will not come into it, and my glory shall not be there; for I will not come into 1 Cor. 3:16 (16–17); Eph. 5:5.unholy temples.
And, now, behold, if Zion do these things she shall Josh. 1:7; D&C 90:24; 100:15.prosper, and spread herself and become very glorious, very great, and very terrible.

And the Isa. 60:14; Zech. 2:11 (10–12); D&C 45:69 (66–69); 49:10.nations of the earth shall honor her, and shall say: Surely TG Jerusalem, New.Zion is the city of our God, and surely Zion cannot fall, neither be moved out of her place, for God is there, and the hand of the Lord is there;
And he hath sworn by the power of his might to be her salvation and her high 2 Sam. 22:3.tower.
Therefore, verily, thus saith the Lord, let Zion rejoice, for this is Moses 7:18 (18–19). TG Purity; Zion.Zion—the pure in heart; therefore, let Zion rejoice, while all the wicked shall mourn.
For behold, and lo, Prov. 10:25; Isa. 66:15; Jer. 23:19. TG Vengeance.vengeance cometh speedily upon the ungodly as the whirlwind; and who shall escape it?
The Lord’s Isa. 28:15; D&C 45:31. TG World, End of.scourge shall pass over by night and by day, and the report thereof shall vex all people; yea, it shall not be stayed until the Lord come;
For the TG God, Indignation of; Wickedness.indignation of the Lord is kindled against their abominations and all their wicked works.
Nevertheless, Zion shall D&C 63:34; JS—M 1:20. TG Protection, Divine.escape if she observe to do all things whatsoever I have commanded her.

But if she Deut. 28:15.observe not to do whatsoever I have commanded her, I will TG Punish; Reproof.visit her D&C 84:58.according to all her works, with sore affliction, with Luke 21:11 (10–13); 2 Ne. 6:15; Mosiah 12:4.pestilence, with TG Plague.plague, with sword, with Isa. 34:8; 61:2; Mal. 4:1 (1, 3); 3 Ne. 21:21 (20–21).vengeance, with Isa. 29:6; Joel 1:19 (19–20); Mal. 3:11; D&C 85:3.devouring fire.
Nevertheless, let it be read this once to her ears, that I, the Lord, have accepted of her offering; and if she sin no more Jer. 18:8; Jonah 3:10 (9–10).none of these things shall come upon her;
And I will bless her with TG Blessing.blessings, and multiply a multiplicity of blessings upon her, and upon her generations forever and ever, saith the Lord your God. Amen.

div.p: Revelation given through Joseph Smith the Prophet, at Kirtland, Ohio, August 6, 1833. This revelation came in consequence of the persecution upon the Saints in Missouri. Increased settlement of Church members in Missouri troubled some other settlers, who felt threatened by the Saints’ numbers, political and economic influence, and cultural and religious differences. On July 20, 1833, a mob destroyed Church property, tarred and feathered two Church members, and demanded that the Saints leave Jackson County. Three days later, Church leaders signed an agreement to do so. Although some news of the problems in Missouri had no doubt reached the Prophet in Kirtland (nine hundred miles away), the seriousness of the situation could have been known to him at this date only by revelation.

1–3, The afflictions of the Saints will be for their good; 4–8, The Saints are to befriend the constitutional law of the land; 9–10, Honest, wise, and good men should be supported for secular government; 11–15, Those who lay down their lives in the Lord’s cause will have eternal life; 16–18, Renounce war and proclaim peace; 19–22, The Saints in Kirtland are reproved and commanded to repent; 23–32, The Lord reveals His laws governing the persecutions and afflictions imposed on His people; 33–38, War is justified only when the Lord commands it; 39–48, The Saints are to forgive their enemies, who, if they repent, will also escape the Lord’s vengeance.

chapter.verse: Verily I say unto you my friends, D&C 68:6.fear not, let your hearts be comforted; yea, rejoice evermore, and in everything give TG Thanksgiving.thanks;

Gen. 49:18; Ps. 27:14; 37:34; 40:1; Prov. 20:22; Isa. 30:18 (18–19); 1 Ne. 21:23; 2 Ne. 6:13; D&C 133:45.Waiting patiently on the Lord, for your prayers have entered into the ears of the Lord of Sabaoth, and are recorded with this seal and testament—the Lord hath sworn and decreed that they shall be granted.

Therefore, he giveth this promise unto you, with an immutable covenant that they shall be fulfilled; and all things wherewith you have been TG Affliction.afflicted shall work together for your D&C 122:7.good, and to my name’s glory, saith the Lord.
And now, verily I say unto you concerning the D&C 20:1; 58:21. TG Citizenship.laws of the land, it is my will that my people should observe to do all things whatsoever I command them.

And that 1 Pet. 2:13 (13–14); D&C 44:4; 51:6; 58:21; 109:54; 134:5.law of the land which is TG Governments; Liberty.constitutional, supporting that principle of freedom in maintaining rights and privileges, belongs to all mankind, and is justifiable before me.
Therefore, I, the Lord, justify you, and your brethren of my church, in befriending that law which is the D&C 44:4; 101:80.constitutional law of the land;
And as pertaining to law of man, whatsoever is more or less than this, cometh of evil.
I, the Lord God, make you John 8:32; 2 Cor. 3:17. TG Agency; Liberty.free, therefore ye are free indeed; and the law also maketh you free.
Nevertheless, when the Prov. 28:28; 29:2.wicked TG Tyranny.rule the people mourn.

Wherefore, TG Citizenship; Honesty.honest men and wise men should be sought for diligently, and good men and wise men ye should observe to uphold; otherwise whatsoever is less than these cometh of evil.

And I give unto you a commandment, that ye shall forsake all evil and cleave unto all TG Good Works.good, that ye shall live by every Deut. 8:3; Matt. 4:4; D&C 84:44 (43–44).word which proceedeth forth out of the mouth of God.

For he will Isa. 28:10; D&C 42:61; 59:4; 76:7; 101:32; 121:28 (26–33).give unto the faithful line upon line, precept upon precept; and I will Judg. 7:4; Alma 27:15; Abr. 3:25.try you and prove you herewith.
And whoso Luke 9:24; 21:17 (15–19); Philip. 2:17; D&C 101:35; 103:27 (27–28). TG Martyrdom.layeth down his life in my cause, for my name’s sake, shall find it again, even life eternal.

Therefore, be not Neh. 4:14; D&C 122:9.afraid of your enemies, for I have decreed in my heart, saith the Lord, that I will D&C 124:55; 132:51. TG Test.prove you in all things, whether you will abide in my covenant, Rev. 2:10; D&C 136:39 (31, 39).even unto death, that you may be found worthy.
For if ye will not abide in my covenant ye are not worthy of me.

Therefore, Alma 48:14. TG Peace; Peacemakers; War.renounce war and proclaim peace, and seek diligently to Mal. 4:6 (5–6); D&C 2:2.turn the hearts of the children to their fathers, and the hearts of the fathers to the children;

And again, the hearts of the TG Israel, Judah, People of; Israel, Restoration of.Jews unto the prophets, and the prophets unto the Jews; lest I come and smite the whole earth with a curse, and all flesh be consumed before me.

Let not your hearts be troubled; for in my Father’s house are John 14:2; D&C 59:2; 76:111; 81:6; 106:8.many mansions, and I have prepared a place for you; and where my Father and I am, there ye shall be also.
Behold, I, the Lord, am not well Rom. 8:8 (5–9); 1 Cor. 10:5 (1–6).pleased with many who are in the church at Kirtland;

For they do not TG Repent.forsake their sins, and their wicked ways, the pride of their hearts, and their covetousness, and all their detestable things, and observe the words of wisdom and eternal life which I have given unto them.

Verily I say unto you, that I, the Lord, will Deut. 11:2 (1–8); Mosiah 23:21; Hel. 12:3.chasten them and will do whatsoever I list, if they do not repent and observe all things whatsoever I have said unto them.

And again I say unto you, if ye observe to TG Obedience.do whatsoever I command you, I, the Lord, will turn away all Isa. 60:10; D&C 101:9.wrath and indignation from you, and the Matt. 16:18; D&C 33:13; 109:26.gates of hell shall not prevail against you.

Now, I speak unto you concerning your families—if men will Luke 6:29; Alma 43:46 (46–47). TG Persecution.smite you, or your families, once, and ye TG Forbear.bear it patiently and TG Reviling.revile not against them, neither seek Deut. 19:6. TG Retribution.revenge, ye shall be 2 Sam. 16:12. TG Reward.rewarded;
But if ye bear it not patiently, it shall be accounted unto you as being Matt. 7:2 (1–2).meted out as a just measure unto you.
And again, if your enemy shall smite you the second time, and you revile not against your enemy, and bear it patiently, your reward shall be an Gen. 26:12.hundred-fold.
And again, if he shall smite you the third time, and ye bear it TG Patience.patiently, your reward shall be doubled unto you four-fold;
And these three TG Judgment.testimonies shall stand against your enemy if he repent not, and shall not be blotted out.

And now, verily I say unto you, if that enemy shall escape my vengeance, that he be not brought into judgment before me, then ye shall see to it that ye TG Warn.warn him in my name, that he come no more upon you, neither upon your family, even your children’s children unto the third and fourth generation.
And then, if he shall come upon you or your children, or your children’s children unto the third and fourth generation, I have delivered thine Deut. 7:23 (16–23).enemy into thine hands;
And then if thou wilt spare him, thou shalt be rewarded for thy TG Reward; Righteousness.righteousness; and also thy children and thy children’s children unto the third and fourth generation.

Nevertheless, thine enemy is in thine hands; and if thou rewardest him according to his works thou art justified; if he has sought thy life, and thy life is endangered by him, thine enemy is in thine hands and thou art justified.
Behold, this is the law I gave unto my servant Nephi, and thy D&C 27:10.fathers, Joseph, and Jacob, and Isaac, and Abraham, and all mine ancient prophets and apostles.

And again, this is the Deut. 20:10 (10–12); Josh. 8:2 (1–29); 1 Kgs. 8:44; Alma 48:16 (10–25).law that I gave unto mine ancients, that they should not go out unto battle against any nation, kindred, tongue, or people, save I, the Lord, commanded them.
And if any nation, tongue, or people should proclaim war against them, they should first lift a standard of TG Peace; Peacemakers.peace unto that people, nation, or tongue;
And if that people did not accept the offering of peace, neither the second nor the third time, they should bring these testimonies before the Lord;
Then I, the Lord, would give unto them a commandment, and justify them in going out to battle against that nation, tongue, or people.

And I, the Lord, would Josh. 23:5 (5–11); Ps. 35:1; Isa. 49:25; D&C 105:14. TG Protection, Divine.fight their battles, and their children’s battles, and their children’s children’s, until they had avenged themselves on all their enemies, to the third and fourth generation.
Behold, this is an TG Example.ensample unto all people, saith the Lord your God, for justification before me.

And again, verily I say unto you, if after thine TG Enemies.enemy has come upon thee the first time, he repent and come unto thee praying thy forgiveness, thou shalt forgive him, and shalt hold it no more as a testimony against thine enemy—

And so on unto the second and third time; and as oft as thine enemy repenteth of the trespass wherewith he has trespassed against thee, thou shalt Matt. 18:22 (21–22). TG Forgive.forgive him, until seventy times seven.
And if he trespass against thee and repent not the first time, nevertheless thou shalt forgive him.
And if he trespass against thee the second time, and repent not, nevertheless thou shalt forgive him.
And if he trespass against thee the third time, and repent not, thou shalt also forgive him.

But if he trespass against thee the fourth time thou shalt not forgive him, but shalt bring these testimonies before the Lord; and they shall not be blotted out until he repent and TG Recompence.reward thee four-fold in all things wherewith he has trespassed against thee.
And if he do this, thou shalt forgive him with all thine heart; and if he do not this, I, the Lord, will Deut. 32:35.avenge thee of thine enemy an hundred-fold;
And upon his children, and upon his children’s TG Accountability.children of all them that TG Hate.hate me, unto the Deut. 5:9.third and fourth generation.

But if the Ex. 20:5 (5–6); Ezek. 18:20 (19–23).children shall repent, or the children’s children, and Lam. 5:21; Mosiah 7:33; Morm. 9:6.turn to the Lord their God, with all their hearts and with all their might, mind, and strength, and Lev. 5:16; 6:4; 24:21 (18–21).restore four-fold for all their trespasses wherewith they have trespassed, or wherewith their fathers have trespassed, or their fathers’ fathers, then thine indignation shall be turned away;
And vengeance shall TG Forgive.no more come upon them, saith the Lord thy God, and their trespasses shall never be brought any more as a testimony before the Lord against them. Amen.

div.p: Revelation given through Joseph Smith the Prophet to John Murdock, August 29, 1832, at Hiram, Ohio. For over a year, John Murdock had been preaching the gospel while his children—motherless after the death of his wife, Julia Clapp, in April 1831—resided with other families in Ohio.
1–8, John Murdock is called to proclaim the gospel, and those who receive him receive the Lord and will obtain mercy.

chapter.verse: Behold, thus saith the Lord unto my servant John Murdock—thou art Luke 10:1 (1–20).called to go into the eastern countries from house to house, from village to village, and from city to city, to proclaim mine everlasting gospel unto the inhabitants thereof, in the midst of TG Persecution.persecution and wickedness.
And who Matt. 10:40 (40–42).receiveth you receiveth me; and you shall have power to declare my word in the 1 Cor. 2:4.demonstration of my Holy Spirit.
And who receiveth you Matt. 18:4 (1–14).as a little child, receiveth my TG Kingdom of God, in Heaven; Kingdom of God, on Earth.kingdom; and blessed are they, for they shall obtain TG Mercy.mercy.

And whoso rejecteth you shall be John 12:49 (44–49).rejected of my Father and his house; and you shall cleanse your D&C 75:20 (19–22).feet in the secret places by the way for a testimony against them.

And behold, and lo, I D&C 1:12.come quickly to Jude 1:15 (14–15). TG Jesus Christ, Judge.judgment, to convince all of their ungodly deeds which they have committed against me, as it is written of me in the volume of the book.
And now, verily I say unto you, that it is not expedient that you should go until your children are D&C 75:24 (24–26).provided for, and sent up kindly unto the bishop of Zion.
And after a few years, if thou desirest of me, thou mayest go up also unto the goodly land, to possess thine D&C 85:7 (1–3, 7, 9); 101:18 (1, 6, 18).inheritance;
Otherwise thou shalt continue proclaiming my gospel Matt. 19:29. TG Self-Sacrifice.until thou be taken. Amen.

div.p: Revelation given to Joseph Smith the Prophet and Sidney Rigdon, at Perrysburg, New York, October 12, 1833. The two brethren, having been absent from their families for several days, felt some concern about them.

1–4, Joseph and Sidney to preach the gospel for the salvation of souls; 5–8, It will be given them in the very hour what they should say; 9–12, Sidney is to be a spokesman and Joseph is to be a revelator and mighty in testimony; 13–17, The Lord will raise up a pure people, and the obedient will be saved.

chapter.verse: Verily, thus saith the Lord unto you, my friends Sidney and Joseph, your families are well; they are in TG Protection, Divine.mine hands, and I will do with them as seemeth me good; for in me there is all power.
Therefore, follow me, and listen to the counsel which I shall give unto you.
Behold, and lo, I have much people in this place, in the regions round about; and an effectual door shall be opened in the regions round about in this eastern land.
Therefore, I, the Lord, have suffered you to come unto this place; for thus it was expedient in me for the TG Mission of Latter-day Saints.salvation of souls.

Therefore, verily I say unto you, lift up your voices unto this people; Ex. 4:12 (12–16); Prov. 16:1; Hel. 5:18 (18–19); 13:3; D&C 68:3 (3–4).speak the thoughts that I shall put into your hearts, and you shall not be Acts 6:10.confounded before men;
For it shall be Matt. 10:19 (19–20); D&C 84:85.given you in the very hour, yea, in the very moment, what ye shall say.

But a commandment I give unto you, that ye shall declare whatsoever thing ye D&C 18:21; 43:34; 84:61; 88:121. TG Meek; Sincere.declare in my name, in solemnity of heart, in the spirit of meekness, in all things.
And I give unto you this promise, that inasmuch as ye do this the Rom. 10:17 (13–17); 2 Ne. 33:1 (1–4).Holy Ghost shall be shed forth in bearing record unto all things whatsoever ye shall say.

And it is expedient in me that you, my servant Sidney, should be a Ex. 4:16 (14–16); 2 Ne. 3:17 (17–18); D&C 124:104.spokesman unto this people; yea, verily, I will ordain you unto this calling, even to be a spokesman unto my servant Joseph.
And I will give unto him power to be mighty in TG Testimony.testimony.

And I will give unto thee power to be Acts 18:24; Alma 17:3 (2–3).mighty in expounding all scriptures, that thou mayest be a spokesman unto him, and he shall be a TG Revelation.revelator unto thee, that thou mayest know the certainty of all things Acts 1:3.pertaining to the things of my kingdom on the earth.
Therefore, continue your journey and let your hearts rejoice; for behold, and lo, I am with you even unto the end.
And now I give unto you a word concerning Zion. TG Zion.Zion shall be D&C 84:99; 103:15 (15, 20, 29).redeemed, although she is chastened for a little season.
Thy brethren, my servants Orson Hyde and John Gould, are in my hands; and inasmuch as they keep my commandments they shall be saved.
Therefore, let your hearts be comforted; for Rom. 8:28; D&C 90:24; 105:40.all things shall work together for good to them that walk uprightly, and to the sanctification of the church.
For I will raise up unto myself a TG Peculiar People; Purity.pure people, that will serve me in righteousness;
And all that Ps. 50:15; Joel 2:32; Alma 38:5 (4–5).call upon the name of the Lord, and keep his commandments, shall be saved. Even so. Amen.

div.p: Revelation given to Joseph Smith the Prophet, at Kirtland, Ohio, December 16 and 17, 1833. At this time the Saints who had gathered in Missouri were suffering great persecution. Mobs had driven them from their homes in Jackson County; and some of the Saints had tried to establish themselves in Van Buren, Lafayette, and Ray Counties, but persecution followed them. The main body of the Saints was at that time in Clay County, Missouri. There had been many threats of death against members of the Church. The Saints in Jackson County had lost household furniture, clothing, livestock, and other personal property; and many of their crops had been destroyed.

1–8, The Saints are chastened and afflicted because of their transgressions; 9–15, The Lord’s indignation will fall upon the nations, but His people will be gathered and comforted; 16–21, Zion and her stakes will be established; 22–31, The nature of life during the Millennium is set forth; 32–42, The Saints will be blessed and rewarded then; 43–62, The parable of the nobleman and the olive trees signifies the troubles and eventual redemption of Zion; 63–75, The Saints are to continue gathering together; 76–80, The Lord established the Constitution of the United States; 81–101, The Saints are to importune for the redress of grievances, according to the parable of the woman and the unjust judge.

chapter.verse: Verily I say unto you, concerning your brethren who have been afflicted, and TG Persecution.persecuted, and D&C 103:2 (2, 11); 104:51; 109:47; 121:23.cast out from the land of their inheritance—

I, the Lord, have suffered the Ps. 119:67; D&C 58:4. TG Affliction.affliction to come upon them, wherewith they have been afflicted, in consequence of their Jer. 30:15; 40:3; Lam. 1:5; Mosiah 7:29; D&C 103:4; 105:9 (2–10).transgressions;
Yet I will own them, and they shall be Isa. 62:3; Mal. 3:17; D&C 60:4.mine in that day when I shall come to make up my jewels.
Therefore, they must needs be 2 Sam. 7:14; D&C 95:1 (1–2); 136:31.chastened and tried, even as Gen. 22:2 (1–14); Jacob 4:5; D&C 132:51.Abraham, who was commanded to offer up his only son.
For all those who will not Prov. 5:12; Alma 61:9.endure chastening, but Prov. 30:9; Matt. 10:33 (32–33); Rom. 1:16 (15–18); 2 Tim. 2:12 (10–15); 2 Ne. 31:14 (12–21).deny me, cannot be sanctified.

Behold, I say unto you, there were jarrings, and TG Contention; Disputations.contentions, and TG Envy.envyings, and TG Strife.strifes, and TG Carnal Mind; Lust; Sensuality.lustful and covetous desires among them; therefore by these things they polluted their inheritances.

They were slow to Lev. 26:14 (14–20); 1 Sam. 8:18; Isa. 1:15; 59:2; Jer. 2:27; 11:11; Ezek. 20:3; Mosiah 11:24 (22–25); 21:15; Alma 5:38 (38–42).hearken unto the voice of the Lord their God; therefore, the Lord their God is slow to hearken unto their prayers, to answer them in the day of their trouble.

In the day of their peace they esteemed lightly my counsel; but, in the day of their Neh. 9:33; Mosiah 13:29; Alma 46:8; Hel. 12:3.trouble, of necessity they Hosea 5:15; Acts 17:27; Alma 32:6.feel after me.

Verily I say unto you, notwithstanding their sins, my bowels are filled with TG Compassion; Mercy.compassion towards them. I will not utterly Jer. 30:11.cast them off; and in the day of Isa. 60:10; D&C 98:22 (21–22).wrath I will remember mercy.

I have sworn, and the decree hath gone forth by a former commandment which I have given unto you, that I would let fall the D&C 1:13 (13–14).sword of mine indignation in behalf of my people; and even as I have said, it shall come to pass.
Mine indignation is soon to be poured out without measure upon all nations; and this will I do when the cup of their iniquity is Gen. 15:16; Alma 37:31; Hel. 13:14; D&C 61:31.full.
And in that day all who are found upon the Ezek. 3:17; 33:9 (6–10).watch-tower, or in other words, all mine Israel, shall be saved.
And they that have been scattered shall be Deut. 30:3; Ps. 60:1 (1–3); Micah 7:12 (11–12); 1 Ne. 10:14.gathered.
And all they who have Matt. 5:4. TG Comfort; Israel, Restoration of; Mourning.mourned shall be comforted.
And all they who have given their Matt. 5:10 (10–11); 10:39. TG Martyrdom.lives for my name shall be crowned.
Therefore, let your hearts be comforted concerning Zion; for all flesh is in mine Moses 6:32.hands; be still and Ex. 14:13; Ps. 46:10; Jer. 9:3.know that I am God.
D&C 101:20 (20–22). TG Zion.Zion shall not be moved out of her place, notwithstanding her children are scattered.

They that remain, and are pure in heart, shall return, and come to their D&C 99:7; 103:14 (11, 14).inheritances, they and their children, with Isa. 35:10; D&C 45:71. TG Singing.songs of everlasting joy, to Amos 9:14; D&C 84:3 (2–5); 103:11.build up the waste places of Zion—
And all these things that the prophets might be fulfilled.

And, behold, there is none other D&C 57:2 (1–4).place appointed than that which I have appointed; neither shall there be any other place appointed than that which I have appointed, for the work of the gathering of my saints—

Until the day cometh when there is found no more room for them; and then I have other places which I will appoint unto them, and they shall be called D&C 82:13; 115:18 (6, 17–18). TG Stake.stakes, for the curtains or the strength of Zion.

Behold, it is my will, that all they who call on my name, and worship me according to mine everlasting gospel, should D&C 10:65. TG Israel, Gathering of; Mission of Latter-day Saints.gather together, and 2 Chr. 35:5; Matt. 24:15; D&C 45:32; 87:8; JS—M 1:12.stand in holy places;

And TG Millennium, Preparing a People for.prepare for the revelation which is to come, when the TG Veil.veil of the covering of my temple, in my tabernacle, which hideth the earth, shall be taken off, and all flesh shall Isa. 40:5; D&C 38:8; 93:1.see me together.

And every Ps. 72:4; D&C 29:24.corruptible thing, both of man, or of the beasts of the field, or of the fowls of the heavens, or of the fish of the sea, that dwells upon all the face of the earth, shall be Zeph. 1:2 (2–3); Mal. 4:1; D&C 88:94; JS—H 1:37.consumed;

And also that of element shall Amos 9:5; 2 Pet. 3:10 (10–12). TG Earth, Cleansing of; World, End of.melt with fervent heat; and all things shall become Rev. 21:5. TG Earth, Renewal of.new, that my knowledge and Isa. 6:3; Ezek. 43:2; Rev. 18:1; D&C 94:8. TG Glory.glory may dwell upon all the TG Earth, Destiny of.earth.
And in that day the enmity of man, and the Isa. 11:9 (6–9).enmity of beasts, yea, the TG Peace.enmity of all flesh, shall cease from before my face.
And in that day Isa. 65:24; D&C 4:7.whatsoever any man shall ask, it shall be given unto him.
And in that day Rev. 20:2 (2–3); 1 Ne. 22:26; D&C 88:110.Satan shall not have power to tempt any man.
And there shall be no TG Immortality; Sorrow.sorrow because there is no death.
In that day an Isa. 65:20 (20–22); D&C 45:58; 63:50 (49–50).infant shall not die until he is old; and his life shall be as the age of a tree;

And when he dies he shall not sleep, that is to say in the earth, but shall be 1 Cor. 15:52; D&C 43:32; 88:28 (20, 28). TG Resurrection.changed in the twinkling of an eye, and shall be 1 Thes. 4:16 (16–17); D&C 76:102; 88:96 (96–98).caught up, and his rest shall be glorious.
Yea, verily I say unto you, in that D&C 29:11; 43:30. TG Jesus Christ, Second Coming; Millennium.day when the Lord shall come, he shall D&C 98:12; 121:28 (26–33).reveal all things—

Things which have passed, and TG Mysteries of Godliness.hidden things which no man knew, things of the TG Earth, Destiny of; Earth, Purpose of.earth, by which it was made, and the purpose and the end thereof—
Things most precious, things that are above, and things that are beneath, things that are in the earth, and upon the earth, and in heaven.

And all they who suffer 2 Cor. 1:6; 4:9; D&C 63:20. TG Malice; Persecution; Perseverance.persecution for my name, and endure in faith, though they are called to lay down their lives for my Luke 21:17 (15–19); D&C 98:13.sake yet shall they partake of all this glory.
Wherefore, D&C 98:13. TG Comfort; Death, Power over.fear not even unto death; for in this world your joy is not full, but in me your 2 Ne. 27:30. TG Joy.joy is full.
Therefore, care not for the body, neither the life of the body; but care for the TG Soul; Worth of Souls.soul, and for the life of the soul.
And 2 Chr. 7:14; Ps. 27:8; 69:32; Amos 5:6; D&C 45:46.seek the face of the Lord always, that in TG Patience.patience ye may possess your souls, and ye shall have eternal life.

When men are called unto mine TG New and Everlasting Covenant.everlasting gospel, and covenant with an everlasting covenant, they are accounted as the Matt. 5:13; 1 Tim. 4:16; D&C 103:10. TG Mission of Latter-day Saints; Peculiar People; Salt.salt of the earth and the savor of men;

They are called to be the savor of men; therefore, if that D&C 103:10.salt of the earth lose its savor, behold, it is thenceforth good for nothing only to be cast out and trodden under the feet of men.
Behold, here is wisdom concerning the children of Zion, even many, but not all; they were found transgressors, therefore they must needs be TG Chastening; Reproof.chastened—

He that Obad. 1:3 (3–4); Luke 14:11; 2 Ne. 20:33; Hel. 4:12 (12–13). TG Leadership.exalteth himself shall be abased, and he that Luke 18:14; D&C 67:10; 104:82; 124:114.abaseth himself shall be exalted.
And now, I will show unto you a parable, that you may know my will concerning the D&C 103:1.redemption of Zion.

A certain D&C 103:21 (21–22).nobleman had a spot of land, very choice; and he said unto his servants: Go ye unto my Isa. 5:1 (1–7); Matt. 21:33 (33–41); Jacob 5:3 (3–77).vineyard, even upon this very choice piece of land, and plant twelve olive trees;

And set Ezek. 33:2 (2, 7); 3 Ne. 16:18. TG Watchman.watchmen round about them, and build a tower, that one may overlook the land round about, to be a watchman upon the tower, that mine olive trees may not be broken down when the enemy shall come to spoil and take upon themselves the fruit of my vineyard.
Now, the servants of the nobleman went and did as their lord commanded them, and planted the olive trees, and built a hedge round about, and set watchmen, and began to build a tower.
And while they were yet laying the foundation thereof, they began to say among themselves: And what need hath my lord of this tower?
And consulted for a long time, saying among themselves: What need hath my lord of this tower, seeing this is a time of peace?
Might not this money be given to the exchangers? For there is no need of these things.
And while they were at variance one with another they became very TG Dependability; Laziness.slothful, and they hearkened not unto the commandments of their lord.

And the enemy came by night, and broke down the Isa. 5:5 (1–7).hedge; and the servants of the nobleman arose and were affrighted, and fled; and the enemy destroyed their works, and broke down the olive trees.
Now, behold, the nobleman, the lord of the TG Vineyard of the Lord.vineyard, called upon his servants, and said unto them, Why! what is the cause of this great evil?

Ought ye not to have done even as I commanded you, and—after ye had planted the vineyard, and built the hedge round about, and set watchmen upon the walls thereof—built the tower also, and set a Ezek. 33:2 (2–7).watchman upon the tower, and watched for my vineyard, and not have fallen asleep, lest the enemy should come upon you?

And behold, the watchman upon the tower would have seen the enemy while he was yet afar off; and then ye could have made ready and kept the enemy from breaking down the hedge thereof, and saved my vineyard from the hands of the destroyer.

And the lord of the vineyard said unto one of his D&C 103:21.
