And now, Holy Father, we ask thee to assist us, thy people, with thy grace, in calling our TG Solemn Assembly.solemn assembly, that it may be done to thine honor and to thy divine acceptance;

And in a manner that we may be found worthy, in thy sight, to secure a fulfilment of the D&C 38:32; 105:33 (11–12, 18, 33); 110:9.promises which thou hast made unto us, thy people, in the revelations given unto us;

That thy Ex. 40:34; 1 Kgs. 8:11 (10–13); 2 Chr. 7:2 (2–3); D&C 84:5; 109:37.glory may rest down upon thy people, and upon this thy house, which we now dedicate to thee, that it may be sanctified and consecrated to be holy, and that thy holy presence may be continually in this house;

And that all people who shall enter upon the threshold of the Lord’s house may feel thy power, and feel constrained to acknowledge that thou hast sanctified it, and that it is thy house, a Lev. 16:23.place of thy holiness.

And do thou grant, Holy Father, that all those who shall worship in this house may be taught words of wisdom out of the best D&C 88:118 (118–19).books, and that they may seek learning even by study, and also by faith, as thou hast said;
And that they may grow up in thee, and receive a fulness of the Holy Ghost, and be organized according to thy laws, and be prepared to obtain every needful thing;
And that this house may be a house of prayer, a house of fasting, a house of faith, a house of glory and of God, even thy house;
That all the incomings of thy people, into this house, may be in the name of the Lord;
That all their outgoings from this house may be in the name of the Lord;
And that all their salutations may be in the name of the Lord, with holy hands, uplifted to the Most High;
And that no Luke 19:46 (46–48); D&C 94:8 (8–9); 97:15 (15–17).unclean thing shall be permitted to come into thy house to TG Pollution.pollute it;

And when thy people 1 Kgs. 8:31 (31–34).transgress, any of them, they may speedily repent and return unto thee, and find favor in thy sight, and be restored to the blessings which thou hast ordained to be poured out upon those who shall TG Reverence.reverence thee in thy house.

And we ask thee, Holy Father, that thy servants may go forth from this house armed with thy power, and that thy TG Jesus Christ, Power of; Jesus Christ, Taking the Name of.name may be upon them, and thy glory be round about them, and thine TG Angels; Protection, Divine.angels have charge over them;

And from this place they may bear exceedingly great and glorious tidings, in truth, unto the D&C 1:2.ends of the earth, that they may know that this is thy work, and that thou hast put forth thy hand, to fulfil that which thou hast spoken by the mouths of the prophets, concerning the last days.
We ask thee, Holy Father, to establish the people that shall worship, and honorably hold a name and standing in this thy house, to all generations and for eternity;
That no weapon Isa. 54:17.formed against them shall prosper; that he who diggeth a Prov. 26:27; 1 Ne. 14:3; 22:14.pit for them shall fall into the same himself;
That no combination of wickedness shall have power to rise up and Matt. 16:18; D&C 98:22.prevail over thy people upon whom thy 1 Kgs. 8:29; D&C 112:12 (11–12).name shall be put in this house;
And if any people shall rise against this people, that thine anger be kindled against them;

And if they shall smite this people thou wilt smite them; thou wilt TG Protection, Divine.fight for thy people as thou didst in the day of battle, that they may be delivered from the hands of all their enemies.

We ask thee, Holy Father, to confound, and astonish, and to bring to TG Shame.shame and confusion, all those who have spread TG Lying.lying reports abroad, over the world, against thy servant or servants, if they will not repent, when the everlasting gospel shall be proclaimed in their ears;

And that all their works may be brought to naught, and be swept away by the Ex. 9:18 (13–35); Isa. 28:17 (15–19); Mosiah 12:6; D&C 29:16 (16–21).hail, and by the judgments which thou wilt send upon them in thine anger, that there may be an end to Ps. 109:2; 3 Ne. 21:19 (11, 19–21).lyings and slanders against thy people.
For thou knowest, O Lord, that thy servants have been innocent before thee in Rev. 12:17.bearing record of thy name, for which they have suffered these things.
Therefore we plead before thee for a full and complete TG Deliver.deliverance from under this TG Bondage, Spiritual.yoke;
Break it off, O Lord; break it off from the necks of thy servants, by thy power, that we may rise up in the midst of this generation and do thy work.
O Jehovah, have mercy upon this people, and as all men Rom. 3:23; 5:1–12, 21. TG Sin.sin, forgive the transgressions of thy people, and let them be blotted out forever.
Let the Ex. 25:6; Lev. 8:12 (12–13); D&C 68:20. TG Anointing.anointing of thy ministers be sealed upon them with power from on high.

Let it be fulfilled upon them, as upon those on the day of Pentecost; let the gift of TG Holy Ghost, Gifts of.tongues be poured out upon thy people, even Acts 2:3.cloven tongues as of fire, and the interpretation thereof.
And let thy house be filled, as with a rushing mighty Acts 2:2.wind, with thy 2 Chr. 7:2 (2–3); D&C 84:5; 109:12. TG God, Manifestations of.glory.

Put upon thy servants the TG Testimony.testimony of the covenant, that when they go out and proclaim thy word they may Isa. 8:16; D&C 1:8.seal up the law, and prepare the hearts of thy saints for all those judgments thou art about to send, in thy wrath, upon the inhabitants of the TG Earth, Cleansing of.earth, because of their transgressions, that thy people may not faint in the day of trouble.

And whatsoever city thy servants shall enter, and the people of that city Matt. 10:13 (11–15).receive their testimony, let thy peace and thy salvation be upon that city; that they may gather out of that city the righteous, that they may come forth to Isa. 35:10.Zion, or to her stakes, the places of thine appointment, with songs of everlasting joy;
And until this be accomplished, let not thy judgments fall upon that city.

And whatsoever city thy servants shall enter, and the people of that city receive not the testimony of thy servants, and thy servants warn them to save themselves from this untoward generation, let it be upon that city according to that which thou hast spoken by the mouths of thy prophets.
But deliver thou, O Jehovah, we beseech thee, thy servants from their hands, and TG Purification.cleanse them from their blood.
O Lord, we delight not in the destruction of our fellow men; their TG Life, Sanctity of; Worth of Souls.souls are precious before thee;
But thy word must be fulfilled. Help thy servants to say, with thy TG God, Will of; Grace.grace assisting them: Thy will be done, O Lord, and not ours.
We know that thou hast spoken by the mouth of thy prophets terrible things concerning the TG Last Days; Wickedness.wicked, in the last days—that thou wilt pour out thy judgments, without measure;

Therefore, O Lord, deliver thy people from the calamity of the wicked; enable thy servants to seal up the law, and Isa. 8:16. TG Seal; Sealing; Testimony.bind up the testimony, that they may be prepared against the day of burning.

We ask thee, Holy Father, to remember those who have been D&C 101:1; 103:2 (2, 11); 104:51.driven by the inhabitants of Jackson county, Missouri, from the lands of their inheritance, and break off, O Lord, this TG Bondage, Physical.yoke of affliction that has been put upon them.

Thou knowest, O Lord, that they have been greatly TG Oppression.oppressed and afflicted by wicked men; and our TG Compassion.hearts flow out with sorrow because of their grievous Ps. 81:6 (5–6).burdens.

O Lord, Ps. 13:2 (1–6); 74:10 (10–23).how long wilt thou suffer this people to bear this affliction, and the Ex. 3:9 (7, 9); 2 Ne. 26:15; Mosiah 21:15.cries of their innocent ones to ascend up in thine ears, and their TG Martyrdom.blood come up in testimony before thee, and not make a display of thy testimony in their behalf?
Have TG Mercy; Repent.mercy, O Lord, upon the wicked mob, who have driven thy people, that they may cease to spoil, that they may repent of their sins if repentance is to be found;
But if they will not, make bare thine arm, O Lord, and D&C 100:13; 105:2.redeem that which thou didst appoint a Zion unto thy people.

And if it cannot be otherwise, that the cause of thy people may not fail before thee may thine anger be kindled, and thine TG God, Indignation of; Punish.indignation fall upon them, that they may be wasted away, both root and branch, from under heaven;
But inasmuch as they will repent, thou art TG Forgive; Grace.gracious and merciful, and wilt turn away thy wrath when thou lookest upon the face of thine Anointed.

Have mercy, O Lord, upon all the TG Governments; Nations.nations of the earth; have mercy upon the rulers of our land; may those principles, which were so honorably and nobly defended, namely, the 1 Pet. 2:13 (13–14); D&C 44:4; 51:6; 98:5 (5–7); 101:77 (77, 80).Constitution of our land, by our fathers, be established forever.
TG Compassion.Remember the kings, the princes, the nobles, and the great ones of the earth, and all people, and the churches, all the poor, the needy, and afflicted ones of the earth;

That their hearts may be softened when thy servants shall go out from thy house, O Jehovah, to bear testimony of thy name; that their prejudices may give way before the TG Hardheartedness; Teachable; Truth.truth, and thy people may obtain favor in the sight of all;
That all the ends of the earth may know that we, thy servants, have D&C 20:16; 76:14 (14, 22–24).heard thy voice, and that thou hast sent us;
That from among all these, thy servants, the sons of Jacob, may gather out the righteous to build a holy D&C 28:9.city to thy name, as thou hast commanded them.

We ask thee to appoint unto Zion other Isa. 54:2; D&C 104:48.stakes besides this one which thou hast appointed, that the gathering of thy D&C 86:11. TG Israel, Gathering of.people may roll on in great power and majesty, that thy work may be cut Matt. 24:22.short in righteousness.
Now these words, O Lord, we have spoken before thee, concerning the revelations and commandments which thou hast given unto us, who are identified with the 1 Ne. 13:4 (1–32); 15:13 (13–18).Gentiles.

But thou knowest that thou hast a great love for the children of Jacob, who have been Hosea 1:6; 13:9 (9–14).scattered upon the Gen. 49:26.mountains for a long time, in a Ezek. 30:3.cloudy and dark day.
We therefore ask thee to have mercy upon the children of Jacob, that 3 Ne. 20:29. TG Jerusalem.Jerusalem, from this hour, may begin to be redeemed;
And the yoke of bondage may begin to be broken off from the house of 1 Kgs. 11:39. TG Israel, Restoration of.David;

And the children of Hosea 1:7; Zech. 12:6 (6–9); Mal. 3:4; D&C 133:35 (13, 35). TG Israel, Judah, People of.Judah may begin to return to the Gen. 17:8 (1–8). TG Promised Lands.lands which thou didst give to Abraham, their father.

And cause that the 2 Ne. 30:3; Alma 46:23 (23–24); 3 Ne. 20:16 (15–21); D&C 3:18; 10:48 (46–52); 19:27; 52:2; 87:5 (4–5). TG Israel, Remnant of.remnants of Jacob, who have been cursed and smitten because of their transgression, be 2 Ne. 30:6; 3 Ne. 21:25 (22–25); D&C 49:24. TG Conversion.converted from their wild and savage condition to the fulness of the everlasting gospel;
That they may lay down their weapons of bloodshed, and cease their rebellions.

And may all the scattered remnants of TG Israel, Restoration of; Israel, Scattering of.Israel, who have been driven to the ends of the earth, come to a knowledge of the truth, believe in the Messiah, and be redeemed from TG Bondage, Spiritual; Oppression.oppression, and rejoice before thee.

O Lord, remember thy servant, Joseph Smith, Jun., and all his afflictions and persecutions—how he has TG Covenants.covenanted with TG Jesus Christ, Jehovah.Jehovah, and vowed to thee, O Mighty God of Jacob—and the commandments which thou hast given unto him, and that he hath sincerely striven to do thy will.
Have mercy, O Lord, upon his TG Family; Family, Eternal.wife and children, that they may be exalted in thy presence, and preserved by thy fostering hand.

Have mercy upon all their IE close relatives.immediate connections, that their prejudices may be broken up and swept away as with a flood; that they may be TG Conversion.converted and redeemed with Israel, and know that thou art God.

Remember, O Lord, the presidents, even all the presidents of thy church, that thy right hand may exalt them, with all their families, and their immediate connections, that their names may be perpetuated and had in everlasting remembrance from generation to generation.

Remember all thy church, O Lord, with all their families, and all their immediate connections, with all their sick and afflicted ones, with all the poor and meek of the earth; that the Dan. 2:44 (44–45); D&C 35:27; 65:2.kingdom, which thou hast set up without hands, may become a great mountain and fill the whole earth;

That thy TG Mission of Latter-day Saints.church may come forth out of the wilderness of darkness, and shine forth fair as the Song 6:10; D&C 5:14; 105:31.moon, clear as the sun, and terrible as an army with banners;

And be adorned as a bride for that day when thou shalt unveil the heavens, and cause the mountains to Judg. 5:5; D&C 133:22 (21–22, 40); Moses 6:34.flow down at thy presence, and the Isa. 40:4; Luke 3:5; Hel. 14:23; D&C 49:23.valleys to be exalted, the rough places made smooth; that thy glory may fill the earth;
That when the trump shall sound for the dead, we shall be 1 Thes. 4:17.caught up in the cloud to meet thee, that we may ever be with the Lord;

That our garments may be pure, that we may be clothed upon with Lev. 8:7; 2 Ne. 9:14.robes of TG Righteousness.righteousness, with palms in our hands, and TG Exaltation.crowns of glory upon our heads, and reap eternal Heb. 12:2 (1–11); D&C 58:4; 103:12 (11–14).joy for all our Heb. 11:40; JST Heb. 11:40 (Heb. 11:40 note a). TG Suffering.sufferings.

O Lord God Almighty, hear us in these our petitions, and answer us from heaven, thy holy habitation, where thou sittest enthroned, with TG God, Glory of.glory, honor, power, majesty, might, dominion, truth, justice, judgment, mercy, and an infinity of fulness, from everlasting to everlasting.
O hear, O hear, O hear us, O Lord! And answer these petitions, and accept the TG Dedication.dedication of this house unto thee, the TG Industry.work of our hands, which we have built unto thy name;

And also this church, to put upon it thy TG Jesus Christ, Taking the Name of.name. And help us by the power of thy Spirit, that we may D&C 20:16, 36 (35–36).mingle our voices with those bright, shining Isa. 6:2 (1–6).seraphs around thy throne, with acclamations of Ezra 3:11 (11–13); D&C 136:28.praise, singing Hosanna to God and the TG Jesus Christ, Lamb of God.Lamb!
And let these, thine Ps. 18:50; 20:6. TG Anointing; Salvation.anointed ones, be clothed with salvation, and thy saints Ps. 132:16.shout aloud for joy. Amen, and Amen.

div.p: Visions manifested to Joseph Smith the Prophet and Oliver Cowdery in the temple at Kirtland, Ohio, April 3, 1836. The occasion was that of a Sabbath day meeting on Easter Sunday. Joseph Smith’s history states: “In the afternoon, I assisted the other Presidents in distributing the Lord’s Supper to the Church, receiving it from the Twelve, whose privilege it was to officiate at the sacred desk this day. After having performed this service to my brethren, I retired to the pulpit, the veils being dropped, and bowed myself, with Oliver Cowdery, in solemn and silent prayer. After rising from prayer, the following vision was opened to both of us.”

1–10, The Lord Jehovah appears in glory and accepts the Kirtland Temple as His house; 11–12, Moses and Elias each appear and commit their keys and dispensations; 13–16, Elijah returns and commits the keys of his dispensation as promised by Malachi.

chapter.verse: The TG Veil.veil was taken from our minds, and the D&C 76:12 (10, 12, 19); 136:32; 138:11.eyes of our TG Understanding.understanding were opened.

We TG God, Privilege of Seeing; Jesus Christ, Appearances, Postmortal.saw the Lord 1 Sam. 3:10; 1 Ne. 1:11 (8–15); JS—H 1:17.standing upon the breastwork of the pulpit, before us; and under his feet was a paved work of pure Ezek. 1:27 (26–28); Rev. 21:21; D&C 137:4.gold, in color like amber.

His Rev. 1:14; 2:18. TG God, Body of, Corporeal Nature.eyes were as a flame of fire; the hair of his head was white like the pure snow; his Ex. 34:29 (29–35); Rev. 1:16; Hel. 5:36; JS—H 1:32.countenance shone above the brightness of the sun; and his Ezek. 1:24; 43:2; Rev. 1:15; D&C 133:22.voice was as the sound of the rushing of great waters, even the voice of TG Jesus Christ, Jehovah.Jehovah, saying:

I am the TG Jesus Christ, Firstborn.first and the last; I am he who Deut. 5:24 (22–24); Zech. 13:6; D&C 45:52 (51–52).liveth, I am he who was slain; I am your TG God, Access to; Jesus Christ, Authority of; Jesus Christ, Relationships with the Father.advocate with the Father.
Behold, your sins are Luke 5:21. TG Forgive.forgiven you; you are clean before me; therefore, lift up your heads and TG Joy.rejoice.
Let the hearts of your brethren rejoice, and let the hearts of all my people rejoice, who have, with their might, D&C 109:4 (4–5).built this house to my name.
For behold, I have 2 Chr. 7:16. TG Dedication.accepted this TG Temple.house, and my name shall be here; and I will TG God, Manifestations of.manifest myself to my people in mercy in this house.

Yea, I will Ex. 19:11 (10–11); D&C 50:45.appear unto my servants, and speak unto them with mine own voice, if my people will keep my commandments, and do not D&C 88:134; 97:15 (15–17). TG Pollution.pollute this TG Holiness.holy house.

Yea the hearts of thousands and tens of thousands shall greatly rejoice in consequence of the Gen. 12:3 (1–3); D&C 39:15; Abr. 2:11 (8–11).blessings which shall be poured out, and the D&C 95:8 (8–9).endowment with which my servants have been endowed in this house.
And the fame of this house shall spread to foreign lands; and this is the beginning of the blessing which shall be Zech. 12:10; D&C 105:12.poured out upon the heads of my people. Even so. Amen.

After this TG Vision.vision closed, the heavens were again Ezek. 1:1.opened unto us; and Matt. 17:3; D&C 133:55.Moses appeared before us, and committed unto us the D&C 113:6. TG Priesthood, Keys of.keys of the 1 Ne. 22:12 (10–12); Jacob 6:2; D&C 29:7; 45:43; 127:6. TG Israel, Gathering of.gathering of Israel from the four parts of the earth, and the leading of the ten tribes from the land of the D&C 133:26. TG Israel, Ten Lost Tribes of.north.

After this, BD Elias.Elias appeared, and committed the TG Dispensations.dispensation of the Gal. 3:8 (6–29); D&C 124:58. TG Abrahamic Covenant.gospel of Abraham, saying that in us and our seed all Gen. 18:18 (17–19).generations after us should be TG Mission of Latter-day Saints.blessed.

After this vision had closed, another great and glorious TG Vision.vision burst upon us; for 1 Kgs. 17:1; Mal. 4:5 (5–6); Matt. 17:3 (1–4); Luke 4:25; 3 Ne. 25:5 (5–6); D&C 2:1; 35:4; 128:17; 138:46. TG Translated Beings.Elijah the prophet, who was taken to heaven without tasting death, stood before us, and said:
Behold, the time has fully come, which was spoken of by the mouth of Malachi—testifying that he [Elijah] should be sent, before the great and dreadful day of the Lord come—

To JS—H 1:39 (38–39).turn the TG Family, Love within; Genealogy and Temple Work; Salvation for the Dead.hearts of the fathers to the children, and the children to the fathers, lest the whole earth be smitten with a curse—

Therefore, the TG Authority; Priesthood, Keys of; Sealing.keys of this TG Dispensations; Restoration of the Gospel.dispensation are committed into your hands; and by this ye may know that the great and dreadful Zeph. 1:14. TG Jesus Christ, Second Coming; Last Days.day of the Lord is near, even at the doors.

div.p: Revelation given through Joseph Smith the Prophet, at Salem, Massachusetts, August 6, 1836. At this time, the Church and its leaders were heavily in debt due to the construction of the Kirtland Temple and persecutions in Missouri. Hearing that a large amount of money might be available to them in Salem, the Prophet, Sidney Rigdon, Hyrum Smith, and Oliver Cowdery traveled there from Kirtland, Ohio, to investigate this claim, along with preaching the gospel. The brethren transacted several items of Church business and did some preaching. When it became apparent that no money was to be forthcoming, they returned to Kirtland.
1–5, The Lord looks to the temporal needs of His servants; 6–11, He will deal mercifully with Zion and arrange all things for the good of His servants.

chapter.verse: I, the Lord your God, am TG God, Mercy of.not displeased with your coming this journey, notwithstanding your follies.

I have much Acts 18:10 (9–11); Rom. 1:13. TG Treasure.treasure in this city for you, for the benefit of Zion, and many people in this city, whom I will gather out in due time for the benefit of Zion, through your instrumentality.
Therefore, it is expedient that you should form TG Fellowshipping; Missionary Work.acquaintance with men in this city, as you shall be led, and as it shall be given you.

And it shall come to pass in due time that I will TG Conversion.give this city into your hands, that you shall have power over it, insomuch that they shall not TG Shame.discover your secret parts; and its wealth pertaining to gold and silver shall be yours.
Concern not yourselves about your D&C 64:27 (27–29).debts, for I will give you power to pay them.
Concern not yourselves about Zion, for I will deal mercifully with her.
Tarry in this place, and in the regions round about;

And the place where it is my will that you should tarry, for the main, shall be signalized unto you by the Micah 5:5; D&C 27:16. TG Peace of God.peace and power of my TG God, Spirit of.Spirit, that shall flow unto you.
This place you may obtain by hire. And inquire diligently concerning the more ancient inhabitants and founders of this city;
For there are more treasures than one for you in this city.
Therefore, be ye as Matt. 10:16.wise as serpents and yet without Heb. 4:15.sin; and I will order all things for your Rom. 8:28; D&C 100:15.good, as fast as ye are able to receive them. Amen.

div.p: Revelation given through Joseph Smith the Prophet to Thomas B. Marsh, President of the Quorum of the Twelve Apostles, at Kirtland, Ohio, July 23, 1837. At this time there was significant dissension in the Church over Joseph Smith’s leadership, including among some of the Apostles. In the months before this revelation was given, Thomas B. Marsh had worked to end the conflict among Church leaders and unite the Apostles.

1–10, The Twelve are to send the gospel and raise the warning voice to all nations and people; 11–15, They are to take up their cross, follow Jesus, and feed His sheep; 16–20, Those who receive the First Presidency receive the Lord; 21–29, Darkness covers the earth, and only those who believe and are baptized will be saved; 30–34, The First Presidency and the Twelve hold the keys of the dispensation of the fulness of times.

chapter.verse: Verily thus saith the Lord unto you my servant Thomas: I have heard thy prayers; and thine TG Almsgiving.alms have come up as a Acts 10:4.memorial before me, in behalf of those, thy brethren, who were chosen to bear testimony of my name and to D&C 18:28.send it abroad among all nations, kindreds, tongues, and people, and ordained through the instrumentality of my servants.
Verily I say unto you, there have been some few things in thine heart and with thee with which I, the Lord, was not well pleased.
Nevertheless, inasmuch as thou hast Matt. 23:12; Luke 14:11.abased thyself thou shalt be exalted; therefore, all thy sins are forgiven thee.

Let thy heart be of good Matt. 9:2; John 16:33.cheer before my face; and thou shalt bear record of my name, not only unto the 1 Ne. 13:42; D&C 18:6 (6–26); 19:27; 21:12; 90:9 (8–9); 107:33.Gentiles, but also unto the Jews; and thou shalt send forth my word unto the ends of the earth.

Jude 1:3.Contend thou, therefore, morning by morning; and day after day let thy Ether 12:3 (2–3). TG Zeal.warning voice go forth; and when the night cometh let not the inhabitants of the earth slumber, because of thy TG Missionary Work.speech.
Let thy habitation be known in Zion, and Ps. 125:1; Micah 4:2.remove not thy house; for I, the Lord, have a great work for thee to do, in publishing my name among the children of men.
Therefore, Eph. 6:15 (14–17).gird up thy loins for the work. Let thy feet be shod also, for thou art chosen, and thy path lieth among the mountains, and among many nations.
And by thy word many high ones shall be brought low, and by thy word many low ones shall be Ezek. 17:24; Matt. 23:12; 2 Ne. 20:33.exalted.
Thy voice shall be a rebuke unto the transgressor; and at thy Luke 17:2; 2 Tim. 4:2.rebuke let the tongue of the slanderer cease its perverseness.
Be thou Prov. 18:12. TG Humility; Meek.humble; and the Lord thy God shall Isa. 57:18 (16–18). TG Guidance, Divine.lead thee by the hand, and give thee answer to thy prayers.

I know thy heart, and have heard thy prayers concerning thy brethren. Be not partial towards them in love above many others, but let thy Matt. 5:43 (43–48). TG Charity; Love.love be for them as for thyself; and let thy love abound unto all men, and unto all who love my name.

And pray for thy brethren of the Twelve. Rom. 15:14; 2 Thes. 3:15 (14–15).Admonish them sharply for my name’s sake, and let them be admonished for all their sins, and be ye faithful before me unto my TG Jesus Christ, Taking the Name of.name.

And after their TG Temptation; Test.temptations, and much John 16:33; Rev. 7:14 (13–14); D&C 103:12. TG Tribulation.tribulation, behold, I, the Lord, will feel after them, and if they harden not their hearts, and TG Stiffnecked.stiffen not their necks against me, they shall be John 12:40; 2 Ne. 26:9; 3 Ne. 9:13 (13–14); 18:32. TG Conversion; Man, New, Spiritually Reborn.converted, and I will heal them.
Now, I say unto you, and what I say unto you, I say unto all the Twelve: Arise and gird up your loins, take up your Matt. 16:24; Luke 9:23.cross, follow me, and John 21:16 (15–17).feed my sheep.

Exalt not yourselves; TG Rebellion.rebel not against my servant Joseph; for verily I say unto you, I am with him, and my hand shall be over him; and the D&C 28:7.keys which I have given unto him, and also to youward, shall not be taken from him till I come.
Verily I say unto you, my servant Thomas, thou art the man whom I have chosen to hold the TG Priesthood, Keys of.keys of my kingdom, as pertaining to the Twelve, abroad among all nations—
That thou mayest be my servant to unlock the door of the kingdom in all places where my servant Joseph, and my servant Sidney, and my servant Hyrum, Luke 10:1.cannot come;
For on them have I laid the burden of all the churches for a little season.

Wherefore, whithersoever they shall send you, go ye, and I will be with you; and in whatsoever place ye shall proclaim my name an 1 Cor. 16:9.effectual door shall be opened unto you, that they may receive my word.

Whosoever D&C 84:35. TG Teachable.receiveth my word receiveth me, and whosoever receiveth me, receiveth those, the First Presidency, whom I have sent, whom I have made counselors for my name’s sake unto you.

And again, I say unto you, that whosoever ye shall send in my name, by the voice of your brethren, the D&C 107:34 (34–35).Twelve, duly recommended and TG Authority; Delegation of Responsibility.authorized by you, shall have power to open the door of my kingdom unto any nation whithersoever ye shall send them—
Inasmuch as they shall humble themselves before me, and abide in my word, and TG God, Spirit of; Obedience.hearken to the voice of my Spirit.

Verily, verily, I say unto you, Isa. 60:2; Micah 3:6; D&C 38:11 (11–12); Moses 7:61 (61–62). TG Apostasy of the Early Christian Church; Darkness, Spiritual.darkness covereth the earth, and gross darkness the minds of the people, and all flesh has become Gen. 6:11 (5–6, 11–13); Ps. 14:1; D&C 10:21 (20–23).corrupt before my face.

Behold, TG Day of the Lord; Retribution; Vengeance.vengeance cometh speedily upon the inhabitants of the earth, a day of wrath, a day of burning, a day of Mal. 4:5.desolation, of Matt. 8:12; D&C 101:91; 124:8. TG Mourning.weeping, of mourning, and of lamentation; and as a whirlwind it shall come upon all the face of the earth, saith the Lord.
And upon my TG Chastening.house shall it 1 Pet. 4:17 (17–18).begin, and from my house shall it go forth, saith the Lord;

First among those among you, saith the Lord, who have D&C 41:1; 50:4; 56:1. TG Hypocrisy.professed to know my TG Jesus Christ, Taking the Name of.name and have not Hosea 8:2 (1–4); Luke 6:46; Mosiah 26:25 (24–27); 3 Ne. 14:23 (21–23).known me, and have D&C 105:15. TG Apostasy of Individuals; Blaspheme.blasphemed against me in the midst of my house, saith the Lord.
Therefore, see to it that ye trouble not yourselves concerning the affairs of my church D&C 107:33 (33–39).in this place, saith the Lord.
But TG Purification; Purity.purify your hearts before me; and then Mark 16:15 (15–16); D&C 18:28.go ye into all the world, and preach my gospel unto every creature who has not received it;
And he that Morm. 9:23 (22–24); D&C 20:25.believeth and is TG Baptism, Essential.baptized shall be saved, and he that believeth not, and is not baptized, shall be TG Damnation.damned.

For unto you, the TG Apostles; Church Organization.Twelve, and those, the First Presidency, who are appointed with you to be your TG Counselor; Leadership.counselors and your leaders, is the TG Priesthood, Power of.power of this priesthood given, for the last days and for the last time, in the which is the dispensation of the Eph. 1:10 (9–10); D&C 27:13; 76:106; 124:41. TG Restoration of the Gospel.fulness of times,
Which power you hold, in connection with all those who have received a TG Dispensations.dispensation at any time from the beginning of the creation;

For verily I say unto you, the TG Priesthood, Keys of.keys of the dispensation, which ye have received, have Abr. 1:3. TG Priesthood, History of.come down from the fathers, and last of all, being sent down from heaven unto you.

Verily I say unto you, behold how great is your calling. Jacob 1:19; 2:2; D&C 88:85.Cleanse your hearts and your garments, lest the blood of this generation be Ezek. 34:10; D&C 72:3.required at your hands.

Be faithful until I come, for I Rev. 3:11; 22:7 (7, 12); D&C 1:12.come quickly; and my reward is with me to recompense every man according as his TG Good Works; Reward.work shall be. I am Alpha and Omega. Amen.

div.p: Answers to certain questions on the writings of Isaiah, given by Joseph Smith the Prophet, at or near Far West, Missouri, March 1838.
1–6, The Stem of Jesse, the rod coming therefrom, and the root of Jesse are identified; 7–10, The scattered remnants of Zion have a right to the priesthood and are called to return to the Lord.

chapter.verse: Who is the Isa. 11:1 (1–5). TG Jesus Christ, Davidic Descent of.Stem of Jesse spoken of in the 1st, 2d, 3d, 4th, and 5th verses of the 11th chapter of Isaiah?
Verily thus saith the Lord: It is Christ.
What is the Ps. 110:2 (1–4).rod spoken of in the first verse of the 11th chapter of Isaiah, that should come of the Stem of Jesse?

Behold, thus saith the Lord: It is a servant in the hands of Christ, who is partly a descendant of Jesse as well as of Gen. 41:52 (50–52); D&C 133:34 (30–34). TG Israel, Joseph, People of.Ephraim, or of the house of Joseph, on whom there is laid much D&C 110:16 (11–16).power.
What is the 2 Ne. 21:10 (10–12).root of Jesse spoken of in the 10th verse of the 11th chapter?

Behold, thus saith the Lord, it is a Isa. 11:10.descendant of Jesse, as well as of Joseph, unto whom rightly belongs the TG Priesthood, Melchizedek.priesthood, and the D&C 110:11. TG Priesthood, Keys of.keys of the kingdom, for an Isa. 30:17; D&C 45:9. TG Ensign.ensign, and for the gathering of my people in the TG Israel, Gathering of; Last Days.last days.
Questions by Elias Higbee: What is meant by the command in Isaiah, 52d chapter, 1st verse, which saith: Put on thy strength, O Zion—and what people had Isaiah reference to?

He had reference to those whom God should call in the last days, who should hold the TG Priesthood, Power of.power of D&C 86:8. TG Priesthood, Keys of.priesthood to bring again TG Zion.Zion, and the redemption of Israel; and to put on her Isa. 45:24; 52:1; 1 Ne. 17:3; D&C 82:14. TG Israel, Restoration of; Strength.strength is to put on the TG Authority; Mission of Latter-day Saints; Priesthood, Authority.authority of the TG Priesthood, History of.priesthood, which she, Zion, has a TG Birthright.right to by lineage; also to return to that power which she had lost.
What are we to understand by Zion loosing herself from the bands of her neck; 2d verse?

We are to understand that the TG Israel, Scattering of.scattered Micah 5:3. TG Israel, Remnant of.remnants are exhorted to Hosea 3:5; 2 Ne. 6:11.return to the Lord from whence they have fallen; which if they do, the promise of the Lord is that he will speak to them, or give them revelation. See the 6th, 7th, and 8th verses. The Isa. 52:2 (2, 6–8). TG Bondage, Spiritual.bands of her neck are the curses of God upon her, or the remnants of Israel in their scattered condition among the Gentiles.

div.p: Revelation given through Joseph Smith the Prophet, at Far West, Missouri, April 11, 1838.
1–2, Church positions held by those who are not faithful will be given to others.

chapter.verse: Verily thus saith the Lord: It is wisdom in my servant David W. Patten, that he settle up all his business as soon as he possibly can, and make a disposition of his merchandise, that he may D&C 118:5.perform a mission unto me next spring, in company with others, even twelve including himself, to testify of my name and bear glad tidings unto all the world.

For verily thus saith the Lord, that inasmuch as there are those among you who TG Apostasy of Individuals.deny my name, others shall be 1 Sam. 2:35; D&C 118:1 (1, 6).planted in their D&C 35:18; 42:10; 64:40.stead and receive their Acts 1:20 (20–26).bishopric. Amen.

div.p: Revelation given through Joseph Smith the Prophet, at Far West, Missouri, April 26, 1838, identifying the name of the Church as The Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints. The revelation also approved of Far West as a new gathering place in the land of Zion.

1–4, The Lord names His church The Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints; 5–6, Zion and her stakes are places of defense and refuge for the Saints; 7–16, The Saints are commanded to build a house of the Lord at Far West; 17–19, Joseph Smith holds the keys of the kingdom of God on earth.

chapter.verse: Verily thus saith the Lord unto you, my servant Joseph Smith, Jun., and also my servant Sidney Rigdon, and also my servant Hyrum Smith, and your D&C 81:1; 112:20; 124:91.counselors who are and shall be appointed hereafter;
And also unto you, my servant D&C 64:17; 124:19.Edward Partridge, and his counselors;

And also unto my faithful servants who are of the high council of my TG Jesus Christ, Head of the Church.church in Zion, for thus it shall be called, and unto all the elders and people of my Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints, scattered abroad in all the world;

For thus shall 3 Ne. 27:8.my 1 Cor. 1:10 (10–13); D&C 20:1 (1–4); 136:2 (2–8). TG Kingdom of God, on Earth.church be called in the last days, even The Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day TG Saints.Saints.

Verily I say unto you all: Isa. 60:1 (1–3). TG Mission of Latter-day Saints.Arise and shine forth, that thy TG Light [noun]; Peculiar People.light may be a Gen. 18:18 (17–19); Isa. 11:12. TG Ensign; Example.standard for the TG Nations.nations;

And that the TG Last Days.gathering together upon the land of TG Zion.Zion, and upon her D&C 101:21; 115:18 (17–18). TG Stake.stakes, may be for a defense, and for a Isa. 25:4 (3–5); D&C 45:66 (62–71). TG Refuge.refuge from the storm, and from wrath when it shall be Rev. 14:10; D&C 1:13 (13–14).poured out without mixture upon the whole earth.
Let the city, Far West, be a holy and consecrated land unto me; and it shall be called most holy, for the ground upon which thou standest is Ex. 3:5; Josh. 5:15; D&C 101:64.holy.
Therefore, I command you to D&C 88:119; 95:8.build a house unto me, for the gathering together of my saints, that they may TG Worship.worship me.
And let there be a beginning of this work, and a foundation, and a preparatory work, this following summer;
And let the beginning be made on the fourth day of July next; and from that time forth let my people Neh. 4:6; D&C 52:39; 75:3. TG Industry.labor diligently to build a house unto my name;
And in D&C 118:5.one year from this day let them re-commence laying the foundation of my D&C 124:45 (45–54).house.
Thus let them from that time forth labor diligently until it shall be finished, from the cornerstone thereof unto the top thereof, until there shall not anything remain that is not finished.
Verily I say unto you, let not my servant Joseph, neither my servant Sidney, neither my servant Hyrum, get in D&C 104:86 (78–86).debt any more for the building of a house unto my name;
But let a house be built unto my name according to the Num. 8:4; Heb. 8:5; D&C 97:10.pattern which I will show unto them.
And if my people build it not according to the pattern which I shall show unto their presidency, I will not accept it at their hands.
But if my people do build it according to the pattern which I shall show unto their presidency, even my servant Joseph and his counselors, then I will accept it at the hands of my people.
And again, verily I say unto you, it is my will that the city of Far West should be built up speedily by the gathering of my saints;
And also that other places should be appointed for D&C 101:21; 115:6. TG Stake.stakes in the regions round about, as they shall be manifested unto my servant Joseph, from time to time.
For behold, I will be with him, and I will sanctify him before the people; for unto him have I given the TG Priesthood, Keys of.keys of this kingdom and ministry. Even so. Amen.

div.p: An extract from Joseph Smith’s history recounting a revelation given to Joseph Smith the Prophet, near Wight’s Ferry, at a place called Spring Hill, Daviess County, Missouri, May or June 1838.

chapter.verse: Spring Hill is named by the Lord Dan. 7:13 (13–14, 22); D&C 78:15 (15, 20); 95:17; 107:53; 117:8 (8, 11). Note that a song titled “Adam-ondi-Ahman,” written by William W. Phelps, was sung at the Kirtland Temple dedication.Adam-ondi-Ahman, because, said he, it is the place where TG Adam.Adam shall come to visit his people, or the Ancient of Days shall sit, as spoken of by Daniel the prophet.

div.p: Revelation given through Joseph Smith the Prophet, at Far West, Missouri, July 8, 1838, concerning the immediate duties of William Marks, Newel K. Whitney, and Oliver Granger.
1–9, The Lord’s servants should not covet temporal things, for “what is property unto the Lord?”; 10–16, They are to forsake littleness of soul, and their sacrifices will be sacred unto the Lord.

chapter.verse: Verily thus saith the Lord unto my servant D&C 124:79 (79–80).William Marks, and also unto my servant Newel K. Whitney, let them settle up their business speedily and journey from the land of Kirtland, before I, the Lord, Job 37:6 (5–6); Ps. 148:8; Hel. 11:17 (13–17); Ether 2:24.send again the snows upon the earth.
Let them awake, and arise, and IE from Kirtland.come forth, and not tarry, for I, the Lord, command it.
Therefore, if they TG Disobedience; Procrastination.tarry it shall not be well with them.
Let them repent of all their sins, and of all their covetous desires, before me, saith the Lord; for what is Mark 14:4 (3–4); D&C 104:14.property unto me? saith the Lord.
Let the properties of Kirtland be turned out for D&C 90:23 (22–23).debts, saith the Lord. Let them go, saith the Lord, and whatsoever remaineth, let it remain in your hands, saith the Lord.

For have I not the fowls of heaven, and also the fish of the sea, and the beasts of the mountains? Have I not TG Creation; God, Works of; Jesus Christ, Creator.made the earth? Do I not hold the Isa. 45:1 (1–3); 1 Ne. 17:37.destinies of all the armies of the nations of the earth?
Therefore, will I not make Isa. 35:1 (1–2).solitary places to bud and to D&C 35:24; 39:13; 49:25. TG Earth, Renewal of.blossom, and to bring forth in abundance? saith the Lord.

Is there not room enough on the mountains of D&C 116.Adam-ondi-Ahman, and on the plains of Olaha Abr. 3:13.Shinehah, or the land where TG Adam; Eden.Adam dwelt, that you should covet that which is but the drop, and neglect the more weighty matters?
Therefore, come up hither unto the land of my people, even Zion.

Let my servant William Marks be Matt. 25:23.faithful over a few things, and he shall be a ruler over many. Let him preside in the midst of my people in the city of Far West, and let him be blessed with the blessings of my people.

Let my servant Newel K. Whitney be ashamed of the Rev. 2:6 (6, 15).Nicolaitane band and of all their TG Secret Combinations.secret abominations, and of all his littleness of soul before me, saith the Lord, and come up to the land of Adam-ondi-Ahman, and be a D&C 72:8 (6–8).bishop unto my people, saith the Lord, not in name but in deed, saith the Lord.

And again, I say unto you, I remember my servant IE the agent left by the Prophet to settle his affairs in Kirtland.Oliver Granger; behold, verily I say unto him that his name shall be had in sacred remembrance from generation to generation, forever and ever, saith the Lord.

Therefore, let him contend earnestly for the redemption of the First Presidency of my Church, saith the Lord; and when he falls he shall rise again, for his TG Self-Sacrifice.sacrifice shall be more sacred unto me than his increase, saith the Lord.
Therefore, let him come up hither speedily, unto the land of Zion; and in the due time he shall be made a merchant unto my name, saith the Lord, for the benefit of my people.
Therefore let no man despise my servant Oliver Granger, but let the blessings of my people be on him forever and ever.

And again, verily I say unto you, let all my servants in the land of Kirtland remember the Lord their God, and mine house also, to keep and preserve it holy, and to overthrow the moneychangers in mine own due time, saith the Lord. Even so. Amen.

div.p: Revelation given through Joseph Smith the Prophet, at Far West, Missouri, July 8, 1838, in response to the supplication, “Show us thy will, O Lord, concerning the Twelve.”
1–3, The Lord will provide for the families of the Twelve; 4–6, Vacancies in the Twelve are filled.

chapter.verse: Verily, thus saith the Lord: Let a conference be held immediately; let the Twelve be organized; and let men be appointed to Acts 1:25 (23–26); D&C 114:2.supply the place of those who are fallen.
Let my servant IE Thomas B. Marsh.Thomas remain for a season in the land of Zion, to publish my word.

Let the residue continue to preach from that hour, and if they will do this in all Eph. 4:2.lowliness of heart, in meekness and humility, and TG Perseverance.long-suffering, I, the Lord, give unto them a TG Promise.promise that I will provide for their families; and an effectual door shall be opened for them, from henceforth.
And next spring let them depart to go over the great waters, and there promulgate my gospel, the fulness thereof, and bear record of my name.
Let them D&C 114:1.take leave of my saints in the city of Far West, on the D&C 115:11.twenty-sixth day of April next, on the building-spot of my house, saith the Lord.

Let my servant John Taylor, and also my servant John E. Page, and also my servant Wilford Woodruff, and also my servant Willard Richards, be appointed to fill the places of those who have D&C 114:2.fallen, and be officially notified of their appointment.

div.p: Revelation given through Joseph Smith the Prophet, at Far West, Missouri, July 8, 1838, in answer to his supplication: “O Lord! Show unto thy servants how much thou requirest of the properties of thy people for a tithing.” The law of tithing, as understood today, had not been given to the Church previous to this revelation. The term tithing in the prayer just quoted and in previous revelations (64:23; 85:3; 97:11) had meant not just one-tenth, but all free-will offerings, or contributions, to the Church funds. This revelation came after the Saints had suffered financial challenges in Missouri and Ohio due to the construction of the Kirtland Temple; persecutions in Jackson County, Missouri; and a nationwide financial crisis. The Saints were also continuing to build up communities in Missouri.
1–5, The Saints are to pay their surplus property and then give, as tithing, one-tenth of their interest annually; 6–7, Such a course will sanctify the land of Zion.

chapter.verse: Verily, thus saith the Lord, I require all their D&C 42:33 (33–34), 55; 51:13; 82:18 (17–19).surplus property to be put into the hands of the bishop of my church in Zion,
For the building of mine 2 Chr. 3:3 (3–4); D&C 115:8 (8–13).house, and for the laying of the foundation of Zion and for the priesthood, and for the debts of the Presidency of my Church.
And this shall be the beginning of the D&C 42:30; 64:23; 120.tithing of my people.

And after that, those who have thus been TG Tithing.tithed shall pay one-tenth of all their interest annually; and this shall be a standing law unto them forever, for my holy priesthood, saith the Lord.

Verily I say unto you, it shall come to pass that all those who gather unto the land of D&C 52:42 (42–43).Zion shall be tithed of their surplus properties, and shall observe this law, or they shall not be found worthy to abide among you.

And I say unto you, if my people observe not this law, to keep it holy, and by this law sanctify the land of Zion unto me, that my statutes and my judgments may be kept thereon, that it may be most holy, behold, verily I say unto you, it shall not be a land of TG Zion.Zion unto you.
And this shall be an ensample unto all the D&C 82:13.stakes of Zion. Even so. Amen.

div.p: Revelation given through Joseph Smith the Prophet, at Far West, Missouri, July 8, 1838, making known the disposition of the properties tithed as named in the preceding revelation, section 119.

chapter.verse: Verily, thus saith the Lord, the time is now come, that D&C 119:1 (1–4).it shall be TG Accountability; Church Organization.disposed of by a council, composed of the First Presidency of my Church, and of the bishop and his council, and by my high council; and by mine own voice unto them, saith the Lord. Even so. Amen.

div.p: Prayer and prophecies excerpted from two epistles to the Church dictated by Joseph Smith the Prophet while he was a prisoner in the jail at Liberty, Missouri, the first dated March 20, 1839, and the second composed around March 22. The Prophet and several companions had been months in prison. Their petitions and appeals directed to various government officials had failed to bring them relief.

1–6, The Prophet pleads with the Lord for the suffering Saints; 7–10, The Lord speaks peace to him; 11–17, Cursed are all those who raise false cries of transgression against the Lord’s people; 18–25, They will not have right to the priesthood and will be damned; 26–32, Glorious revelations promised those who endure valiantly; 33–40, Why many are called and few are chosen; 41–46, The priesthood should be used only in righteousness.

chapter.verse: O God, Matt. 27:46.where art thou? And where is the pavilion that covereth thy Ps. 13:1 (1–2); 18:11; 102:2; Isa. 45:15.hiding place?

Ex. 5:22; Ps. 35:17 (17–28); 89:46; Hab. 1:2 (2–4).How long shall thy hand be stayed, and thine eye, yea thy pure eye, behold from the eternal heavens the wrongs of thy people and of thy servants, and thine ear be penetrated with their cries?

Yea, O Lord, Ps. 69:14 (1–2, 14); Alma 14:26 (26–29).how long shall they suffer these wrongs and unlawful TG Cruelty; Oppression; Persecution.oppressions, before thine heart shall be softened toward them, and thy bowels be moved with TG Compassion.compassion toward them?

O Lord God TG God, Power of.Almighty, maker of Gen. 1:1.heaven, earth, and seas, and of all things that in them are, and who controllest and subjectest the devil, and the dark and benighted dominion of Sheol—stretch forth thy hand; let thine eye pierce; let thy 2 Sam. 22:7.pavilion be taken up; let thy D&C 101:89; 123:6.hiding place no longer be covered; let thine ear be inclined; let thine TG Compassion; God, Mercy of.heart be softened, and thy bowels moved with compassion toward us.
Let thine Ps. 119:84.anger be kindled against our enemies; and, in the fury of thine heart, with thy D&C 1:13 (13–14).sword Ps. 35:1 (1–10); Luke 18:7.avenge us of our wrongs.
Remember thy D&C 122:5 (5–7). TG Suffering.suffering saints, O our God; and thy servants will rejoice in thy name forever.
My son, Acts 23:11 (11–14). TG Comfort.peace be unto thy soul; thine TG Adversity; Affliction.adversity and thine afflictions shall be but a Isa. 54:7.small moment;
And then, if thou 1 Pet. 2:20 (19–23). TG Perseverance; Probation.endure it well, God shall exalt thee on high; thou shalt triumph over all thy TG Enemies.foes.
Thy D&C 122:3. TG Friendship.friends do stand by thee, and they shall hail thee again with warm hearts and friendly hands.
Thou art not yet as Job; thy Job 4:1 (1–8); 13:4; 16:2.friends do not contend against thee, neither charge thee with transgression, as they did Job.

And they who do charge thee with transgression, their hope shall be blasted, and their prospects shall Ps. 58:7 (3–11); Jer. 1:19 (7–19).melt away as the hoar frost melteth before the burning rays of the rising sun;

And also that God hath set his hand and seal to change the Dan. 2:21 (19–22, 28); Acts 1:7; D&C 68:11 (7–12). TG Time.times and seasons, and to blind their 2 Kgs. 6:18 (18–23). TG Mind.minds, that they may not understand his Ex. 8:19; 1 Ne. 19:22; Alma 23:6; D&C 84:3.marvelous workings; that he may TG Test.prove them also and take them in their own craftiness;

Also because their hearts are corrupted, and the things which they are willing to bring upon others, and love to have others suffer, may come upon Prov. 28:10; 1 Ne. 14:3.themselves to the very uttermost;
That they may be 3 Ne. 4:10.disappointed also, and their hopes may be cut off;
And not many years hence, that they and their Ps. 37:28; 109:13.posterity shall be D&C 124:52.swept from under heaven, saith God, that not one of them is left to stand by the wall.

TG Curse.Cursed are all those that shall lift up the Ps. 41:9; John 13:18. TG Prophets, Rejection of; Reviling.heel against mine 1 Sam. 26:9; Ps. 2:2 (2–4); 105:15.anointed, saith the Lord, and cry they have 2 Ne. 15:20; Moro. 7:14 (14, 18); D&C 64:16.sinned when they have not sinned before me, saith the Lord, but have done that which was meet in mine eyes, and which I commanded them.
But Rev. 12:10; Alma 15:15.those who cry transgression do it because they are the servants of sin, and are the Eph. 5:6 (2–6).children of disobedience themselves.
And those who TG Swearing.swear Mal. 3:5. TG Lying; Slander.falsely against my servants, that they might bring them into bondage and death—
Wo unto them; because they have Matt. 18:6. TG Offense.offended my little ones they shall be severed from the TG Ordinance; Salvation.ordinances of mine house.
Their Deut. 28:17 (15–26).basket shall not be full, their houses and their barns shall perish, and they themselves shall be Prov. 24:24 (23–26).despised by those that flattered them.
They shall not have right to the Alma 13:4 (3–5). TG Priesthood, Qualifying for.priesthood, nor their posterity after them from generation to generation.
It had been Matt. 18:6; D&C 54:5.better for them that a millstone had been hanged about their necks, and they drowned in the depth of the sea.

Wo unto all those that D&C 101:1.discomfort my people, and drive, and Esth. 3:13.murder, and testify against them, saith the Lord of Hosts; a Matt. 12:34; 23:33.generation of vipers shall not escape the damnation of hell.

Behold, mine eyes Job 10:4; Ps. 139:1; Jonah 3:10. TG God, Omniscience of.see and know all their works, and I have in reserve a swift Ps. 109:7 (3–7); Hel. 8:25; D&C 10:23 (20–23).judgment in the season thereof, for them all;
For there is a D&C 122:9.time Job 7:1; Alma 12:27 (26–28); D&C 42:48.appointed for every man, according as his TG Good Works.works shall be.

God shall give unto you Dan. 2:28 (22–29, 49); Amos 3:7; A of F 1:9. TG Mysteries of Godliness; Revelation.knowledge by his TG God, Spirit of.Holy Spirit, yea, by the unspeakable TG Holy Ghost, Gift of.gift of the Holy Ghost, that has not been Alma 12:10 (9–11); 40:3. TG Scriptures to Come Forth.revealed since the world was until now;

Which our forefathers have awaited with Rom. 16:25; D&C 6:7 (7–11); 42:65 (61–65).anxious expectation to be revealed in the last times, which their minds were pointed to by the angels, as held in reserve for the fulness of their glory;

A time to come in the which D&C 42:61; 59:4; 76:7; 98:12; 101:32.nothing shall be withheld, whether there be Rom. 3:30 (28–31); 1 Tim. 2:5.one God or many Ps. 82:6; John 10:34; 1 Cor. 8:6 (5–6); D&C 76:58; Abr. 4:1 (1–31); 5:1 (1–21).gods, they shall be manifest.

All thrones and dominions, principalities and powers, shall be D&C 101:32.revealed and set forth upon all who have endured D&C 76:79. TG Diligence; Trustworthiness.valiantly for the gospel of Jesus Christ.
And also, if there be Job 26:10 (7–14); 38:33 (1–41); Ps. 104:9 (1–35); Acts 17:26 (24–28).bounds set to the heavens or to the seas, or to the dry land, or to the sun, moon, or stars—

All the times of their revolutions, all the appointed days, months, and years, and all the days of their days, months, and years, and all their Abr. 3:4 (2–10). TG Glory; God, Law of.glories, laws, and set times, shall be revealed in the days of the TG Dispensations.dispensation of the fulness of times—

According to that which was TG Earth, Purpose of.ordained in the midst of the TG Council in Heaven.Council of the Eternal Deut. 10:17 (17–21); Dan. 11:36.God of all other gods before this TG Earth, Destiny of; World.world was, that should be reserved unto the finishing and the end thereof, when every man shall enter into his eternal TG Man, Potential to Become like Heavenly Father.presence and into his immortal Ps. 95:11; Heb. 3:11; 4:1; Alma 12:34; 3 Ne. 27:19; D&C 84:24.rest.

How long can rolling waters remain impure? What Rom. 8:39 (35–39).power shall stay the heavens? As well might man stretch forth his puny arm to stop the Missouri river in its decreed course, or to turn it up stream, as to Dan. 4:35.hinder the TG God, Power of.Almighty from pouring down Dan. 12:4. TG Knowledge.knowledge from heaven upon the heads of the Latter-day Saints.
Behold, there are many Matt. 20:16; 22:14 (1–14); Luke 13:23; D&C 63:31; 95:5 (5–6). TG Called of God.called, but few are chosen. And why are they not chosen?

Because their Luke 14:18 (18–20).hearts are set so much upon the things of this TG Selfishness; Worldliness.world, and TG Motivations.aspire to the Matt. 6:2; 2 Ne. 26:29; D&C 58:39. TG Honor.honors of men, that they do not learn this one lesson—

That the D&C 107:10. TG Priesthood, Authority; Priesthood, Keys of; Priesthood, Oath and Covenant; Priesthood, Power of.rights of the priesthood are inseparably connected with the powers of heaven, and that the powers of heaven cannot be TG Priesthood, Magnifying Callings within.controlled nor handled only upon the D&C 107:30. TG Righteousness.principles of righteousness.

That they may be conferred upon us, it is true; but when we undertake to Prov. 28:13. TG Apostasy of Individuals; Honesty; Hypocrisy.cover our TG Sin.sins, or to gratify our TG Haughtiness; Pride.pride, our vain ambition, or to exercise control or Gen. 1:26 (26–28); D&C 76:111 (110–12).dominion or compulsion upon the souls of the children of men, in any degree of unrighteousness, behold, the heavens 1 Sam. 18:12; D&C 1:33; 3:11; JS—H 1:46. TG Holy Ghost, Loss of.withdraw themselves; the Spirit of the Lord is grieved; and when it is withdrawn, Amen to the priesthood or the authority of that man.
Behold, ere he is aware, he is left unto himself, to Acts 9:5; 26:14.kick against the pricks, to TG Persecution.persecute the saints, and to Micah 3:5; 1 Ne. 11:35 (34–36).fight against God.

We have learned by sad experience that it is the TG Man, Natural, Not Spiritually Reborn.nature and disposition of almost all men, as soon as they get a little TG Authority.authority, as they suppose, they will immediately begin to exercise 1 Kgs. 16:2. TG Leadership; Unrighteous Dominion.unrighteous dominion.
Hence many are called, but Matt. 20:16; D&C 95:5.few are chosen.

No TG Priesthood, Power of.power or influence can or ought to be maintained by virtue of the 1 Pet. 5:3 (1–3). TG Priesthood; Priesthood, Magnifying Callings within.priesthood, only by Prov. 25:15. TG Communication; Marriage, Husbands.persuasion, by 2 Cor. 6:6. TG Forbear; Love; Meek; Patience.long-suffering, by gentleness and meekness, and by love unfeigned;

By TG Courtesy; Kindness.kindness, and pure 1 Pet. 3:7. TG Knowledge.knowledge, which shall greatly enlarge the TG Understanding.soul without James 3:17.hypocrisy, and without TG Guile; Sincere.guile—

TG Chastening; Reproof.Reproving betimes with D&C 15:2.sharpness, when TG Holy Ghost, Mission of.moved upon by the Holy Ghost; and then showing forth afterwards an increase of TG Charity.love toward him whom thou hast reproved, lest he esteem thee to be his enemy;
That he may know that thy faithfulness is stronger than the cords of TG Dependability.death.

Let thy 1 Thes. 3:12; 1 Jn. 3:17 (16–17). TG Benevolence; Charity; Priesthood, Magnifying Callings within.bowels also be full of charity towards all men, and to the household of faith, and let TG Chastity; Modesty; Virtue.virtue garnish thy thoughts unceasingly; then shall thy TG Trust in God.confidence wax strong in the TG God, Presence of; God, Privilege of Seeing.presence of God; and the doctrine of the priesthood shall distil upon thy soul as the Deut. 32:2; D&C 128:19.dews from heaven.

The Holy Ghost shall be thy constant TG Holy Ghost, Mission of; Spirituality.companion, and thy scepter an unchanging scepter of TG Righteousness.righteousness and truth; and thy Dan. 7:14.dominion shall be an everlasting dominion, and without compulsory means it shall flow unto thee forever and ever.

div.p: The word of the Lord to Joseph Smith the Prophet, while a prisoner in the jail at Liberty, Missouri. This section is an excerpt from an epistle to the Church written around March 22, 1839.

1–4, The ends of the earth will inquire after the name of Joseph Smith; 5–7, All his perils and travails will give him experience and be for his good; 8–9, The Son of Man has descended below them all.

chapter.verse: The ends of the earth shall inquire after thy JS—H 1:33.name, and fools shall have thee in Job 30:1; Ps. 119:51 (49–52); Jer. 20:7 (7–9).derision, and hell shall rage against thee;
While the pure in heart, and the wise, and the noble, and the virtuous, shall seek TG Counsel.counsel, and authority, and blessings constantly from under thy hand.
And thy D&C 121:9. TG Friendship.people shall never be turned against thee by the testimony of traitors.

And although their influence shall cast thee into trouble, and into bars and walls, thou shalt be had in TG Respect.honor; and but for a small D&C 121:7 (7–8).moment and thy voice shall be more terrible in the midst of thine enemies than the fierce 3 Ne. 20:16 (16–21). TG Protection, Divine.lion, because of thy righteousness; and thy God shall stand by thee forever and ever.

If thou art called to pass through D&C 121:6. TG Tribulation.tribulation; if thou art in perils among false brethren; if thou art in 2 Cor. 11:26 (23–28).perils among robbers; if thou art in perils by land or by sea;

If thou art Job 8:6 (1–22).accused with all manner of false accusations; if thine enemies fall upon thee; if they tear thee from the society of thy father and mother and brethren and sisters; and if with a drawn sword thine enemies tear thee from the bosom of thy wife, and of thine offspring, and thine elder son, although but six years of age, shall cling to thy garments, and shall say, My father, my father, why can’t you stay with us? O, my father, what are the men going to do with you? and if then he shall be thrust from thee by the sword, and thou be dragged to Jer. 36:5; Acts 5:18 (15, 18); 12:5; 16:23 (19–40); Alma 14:22 (22–28).prison, and thine enemies prowl around thee like Luke 10:3.wolves for the blood of the lamb;

And if thou shouldst be cast into the Gen. 37:24; Jer. 38:6 (6–13).pit, or into the hands of murderers, and the sentence of death passed upon thee; if thou be cast into the Ps. 69:2 (1–2, 14); Jonah 2:3 (3–9); 2 Cor. 11:25.deep; if the billowing surge conspire against thee; if fierce winds become thine enemy; if the heavens gather blackness, and all the elements combine to Lam. 3:7 (7–8). TG Despair.hedge up the way; and above all, if the very jaws of 2 Sam. 22:6 (5–7); JS—H 1:16 (15–16).hell shall gape open the mouth wide after thee, know thou, my son, that all these things shall give thee Job 2:10 (10–13); 3:20 (20–26); 5:27; Eccl. 3:10 (9–10); Jer. 24:5; 2 Cor. 4:17; Heb. 12:10 (10–11); 1 Pet. 2:20 (20–21); 2 Ne. 2:11.experience, and shall be for thy good.
The TG Jesus Christ, Son of Man.Son of Man hath Mark 14:37 (37, 40–41); Heb. 2:17 (9–18); D&C 76:107; 88:6.descended below them all. Art thou greater than he?

Therefore, TG Steadfastness.hold on thy way, and the priesthood shall D&C 13; 84:17; 90:3; 124:130.remain with thee; for their Deut. 32:8; Job 11:20 (19–20).bounds are set, they cannot pass. Thy D&C 121:25.days are known, and thy years shall not be numbered less; therefore, Neh. 4:14; Ps. 56:4 (4, 11); 118:6; Jer. 1:17 (17–19); Luke 12:5 (4–5); 2 Ne. 8:7 (7–12); D&C 3:7; 98:14.fear not what man can do, for God shall be with you forever and ever.

div.p: Duty of the Saints in relation to their persecutors, as written by Joseph Smith the Prophet while a prisoner in the jail at Liberty, Missouri. This section is an excerpt from an epistle to the Church written around March 22, 1839 (see the heading to section 122).

1–6, The Saints should collect and publish an account of their sufferings and persecutions; 7–10, The same spirit that established the false creeds also leads to persecution of the Saints; 11–17, Many among all sects will yet receive the truth.

chapter.verse: And again, we would suggest for your consideration the propriety of all the saints D&C 69:8.gathering up a knowledge of all the facts, and Ezra 4:1 (1–24).sufferings and abuses put upon them by the people of this State;
And also of all the property and amount of damages which they have sustained, both of character and personal TG Injustice.injuries, as well as real property;
And also the names of all persons that have had a hand in their TG Oppression.oppressions, as far as they can get hold of them and find them out.
And perhaps a committee can be appointed to find out these things, and to take TG Record Keeping.statements and affidavits; and also to gather up the libelous publications that are afloat;

And all that are in the magazines, and in the encyclopedias, and all the libelous histories that are published, and are writing, and by whom, and present the whole concatenation of diabolical rascality and nefarious and murderous impositions that have been practiced upon this people—

That we may not only publish to all the world, but present them to the Ezra 5:8 (8–17).heads of government in all their dark and hellish hue, as the last effort which is enjoined on us by our Heavenly Father, before we can fully and completely claim that promise which shall call him forth from his D&C 101:89; 121:4 (1, 4).hiding place; and also that the whole nation may be left without excuse before he can send forth the power of his mighty arm.

It is an imperative duty that we owe to God, to angels, with whom we shall be brought to stand, and also to ourselves, to our wives and TG Family, Children, Responsibilities toward.children, who have been made to bow down with grief, sorrow, and care, under the most damning hand of murder, tyranny, and TG Cruelty.oppression, supported and urged on and upheld by the influence of that spirit which hath so strongly riveted the Alma 24:7; JS—H 1:19. TG Traditions of Men.creeds of the fathers, who have inherited lies, upon the hearts of the children, and filled the world with confusion, and has been growing stronger and stronger, and is now the very mainspring of all corruption, and the whole Isa. 33:9; Moses 7:49.earth groans under the weight of its iniquity.
It is an iron TG Bondage, Physical; Bondage, Spiritual.yoke, it is a strong band; they are the very handcuffs, and chains, and shackles, and fetters of TG Hell.hell.

Therefore it is an imperative duty that we owe, not only to our own wives and children, but to the TG Children; Widows.widows and fatherless, whose husbands and fathers have been D&C 98:13; 103:27 (27–28); 124:54.murdered under its iron hand;
Which dark and blackening deeds are enough to make hell itself Isa. 14:9 (4–11).shudder, and to stand aghast and pale, and the hands of the very devil to tremble and palsy.
And also it is an imperative duty that we owe to all the rising generation, and to all the pure in heart—

For there are many yet on the earth among all sects, parties, and denominations, who are Col. 2:8; D&C 76:75. TG Spiritual Blindness.blinded by the subtle TG False Doctrine; False Prophets.craftiness of men, whereby they lie in wait to Col. 2:18 (16–23).deceive, and who are only kept from the truth because they 1 Ne. 8:21 (19–24). TG Mission of Latter-day Saints.know not where to find it—

Therefore, that we should waste and TG Dedication.wear out our lives in bringing to light all the 1 Cor. 4:5. TG Secret Combinations.hidden things of darkness, wherein we know them; and they are truly manifest from heaven—
These should then be attended to with great TG Zeal.earnestness.
Let no man count them as small things; for there is much which lieth in futurity, pertaining to the saints, which depends upon these things.

You know, brethren, that a very large ship is James 3:4; 1 Ne. 16:29; Alma 37:6 (6–8); D&C 64:33.benefited very much by a very small helm in the time of a storm, by being kept workways with the wind and the waves.

Therefore, dearly beloved brethren, let us TG Cheerful.cheerfully TG Initiative.do all things that lie in our power; and then may we stand still, with the utmost assurance, to see the Ex. 14:13; Isa. 30:15.salvation of God, and for his arm to be revealed.

div.p: Revelation given to Joseph Smith the Prophet, at Nauvoo, Illinois, January 19, 1841. Because of increasing persecutions and illegal procedures against them by public officers, the Saints had been compelled to leave Missouri. The exterminating order issued by Lilburn W. Boggs, governor of Missouri, dated October 27, 1838, had left them no alternative. In 1841, when this revelation was given, the city of Nauvoo, occupying the site of the former village of Commerce, Illinois, had been built up by the Saints, and here the headquarters of the Church had been established.

1–14, Joseph Smith is commanded to make a solemn proclamation of the gospel to the president of the United States, the governors, and the rulers of all nations; 15–21, Hyrum Smith, David W. Patten, Joseph Smith Sr., and others among the living and the dead are blessed for their integrity and virtues; 22–28, The Saints are commanded to build both a house for the entertainment of strangers and a temple in Nauvoo; 29–36, Baptisms for the dead are to be performed in temples; 37–44, The Lord’s people always build temples for the performance of holy ordinances; 45–55, The Saints are excused from building the temple in Jackson County because of the oppression of their enemies; 56–83, Directions are given for the building of the Nauvoo House; 84–96, Hyrum Smith is called to be a patriarch, to receive the keys, and to stand in the place of Oliver Cowdery; 97–122, William Law and others are counseled in their labors; 123–45, General and local officers are named, along with their duties and quorum affiliations.

chapter.verse: Verily, thus saith the Lord unto you, my servant Joseph Smith, I am well pleased with your Rom. 12:1; 1 Tim. 2:3 (1–4); 1 Pet. 2:5.offering and acknowledgments, which you have made; for unto this end have I raised you up, that I might show forth my TG God, Wisdom of.wisdom through the 1 Cor. 1:27 (26–29); D&C 1:19; 35:13.weak things of the earth.

Your prayers are acceptable before me; and in answer to them I say unto you, that you are now called immediately to make a solemn TG Mission of Latter-day Saints.proclamation of my gospel, and of this TG Stake.stake which I have planted to be a TG Cornerstone.cornerstone of Zion, which shall be polished with the refinement which is after the similitude of a palace.

This proclamation shall be made to all the Ps. 119:46; 138:4 (1–5); Matt. 10:18; Acts 9:15; D&C 1:23.kings of the world, to the four corners thereof, to the honorable president-elect, and the high-minded governors of the nation in which you live, and to all the nations of the earth scattered abroad.
Let it be TG Holy Ghost, Gifts of; Meek.written in the spirit of meekness and by the power of the Holy Ghost, which shall be in you at the time of the writing of the same;

For it shall be Dan. 2:22 (19–22, 28).given you by the Holy Ghost to know my TG God, Will of.will concerning those Gen. 41:25; Dan. 2:21 (20–22). TG Kings, Earthly.kings and authorities, even what shall befall them in a time to come.
For, behold, I am about to call upon them to give heed to the light and glory of Zion, for the set time has come to favor her.

Call ye, therefore, upon them with loud proclamation, and with your testimony, fearing them not, for they are as Ps. 90:5; 103:15 (15–16); Isa. 40:6 (6–8); 1 Pet. 1:24.grass, and all their glory as the flower thereof which soon falleth, that they may be left also without excuse—

And that I may Isa. 10:3 (1–4); 1 Pet. 2:12.visit them in the day of visitation, when I shall D&C 1:12. TG Veil.unveil the face of my covering, to appoint the portion of the TG Cruelty; Oppression.oppressor among hypocrites, where there is D&C 29:15 (15–20); 101:91; 112:24.gnashing of teeth, if they reject my servants and my testimony which I have revealed unto them.

And again, I will visit and soften their hearts, many of them for your good, that ye may find grace in their eyes, that they may come to the Isa. 60:3 (1–12). TG Light [noun].light of truth, and the Gentiles to the exaltation or lifting up of Zion.
For the day of my visitation cometh speedily, in an Matt. 24:44.hour when ye think not of; and where shall be the safety of my people, and refuge for those who shall be left of them?
Awake, O kings of the earth! Come ye, O, come ye, with your Isa. 60:6 (6, 11–17); 61:6 (4–6).gold and your silver, to the help of my people, to the house of the daughters of Zion.
And again, verily I say unto you, let my servant Robert B. Thompson help you to write this proclamation, for I am well pleased with him, and that he should be with you;
Let him, therefore, hearken to your counsel, and I will bless him with a multiplicity of blessings; let him be faithful and true in all things from henceforth, and he shall be great in mine eyes;
But let him remember that his TG Stewardship.stewardship will I require at his hands.

And again, verily I say unto you, blessed is my servant Hyrum Smith; for I, the Lord, love him because of the TG Integrity; Sincere.integrity of his heart, and because he loveth that which is right before me, saith the Lord.

Again, let my servant John C. Bennett help you in your labor in sending my word to the kings and people of the earth, and stand by you, even you my servant Joseph Smith, in the hour of affliction; and his reward shall not fail if he receive TG Counsel.counsel.

And for his love he shall be great, for he shall be mine if he do this, saith the Lord. I have seen the work which he hath done, which I accept if he continue, and will crown him with blessings and great glory.

And again, I say unto you that it is my will that my servant Lyman Wight should continue in preaching for Zion, in the spirit of meekness, confessing me before the world; and I will bear him up as on Ex. 19:4; Isa. 40:31.eagles’ wings; and he shall beget glory and honor to himself and unto my name.

That when he shall finish his work I may D&C 124:130.receive him unto myself, even as I did my servant David Patten, who is with me at this time, and also my servant D&C 115:2 (2–6).Edward Partridge, and also my aged servant Joseph Smith, Sen., who sitteth Luke 16:22; D&C 137:5.with Abraham at his right hand, and blessed and holy is he, for he is mine.

And again, verily I say unto you, my servant George Miller is without TG Guile; Sincere.guile; he may be trusted because of the Prov. 19:1. TG Integrity.integrity of his heart; and for the love which he has to my testimony I, the Lord, love him.

I therefore say unto you, I seal upon his head the office of a bishopric, like unto my D&C 41:9. TG Bishop.servant Edward Partridge, that he may receive the consecrations of mine house, that he may administer blessings upon the heads of the poor of my people, saith the Lord. Let no man despise my servant George, for he shall honor me.

Let my servant George, and my servant Lyman, and my servant John Snider, and others, build a D&C 124:56 (56–82, 111–22).house unto my name, such a one as my servant Joseph shall show unto them, upon the place which he shall show unto them also.

And it shall be for a house for boarding, a house that strangers may come from afar to lodge therein; therefore let it be a good house, worthy of all acceptation, that the weary Lev. 25:35 (35–38); Deut. 31:12 (12–13); Matt. 25:35 (35, 38, 43–44).traveler may find health and safety while he shall contemplate the word of the Lord; and the D&C 124:2.cornerstone I have appointed for Zion.

This house shall be a healthful habitation if it be built unto my name, and if the governor which shall be appointed unto it shall not suffer any pollution to come upon it. It shall be holy, or the Lord your God will not Alma 7:21 (20–21).dwell therein.
And again, verily I say unto you, let all my saints D&C 10:65.come from afar.

And send ye Isa. 18:2 (1–7).swift messengers, yea, chosen messengers, and say unto them: Come ye, with all your 1 Kgs. 6:21 (21–22); 2 Ne. 5:15.gold, and your silver, and your precious stones, and with all your antiquities; and with all who have D&C 93:53.knowledge of antiquities, that will come, may come, and bring the Ex. 26:15; Isa. 41:19; 60:13.box tree, and the fir tree, and the pine tree, together with all the precious trees of the earth;

And with iron, with copper, and with brass, and with zinc, and with all your precious things of the earth; and build a D&C 109:5. TG Temple.house to my name, for the Most High to Ex. 25:8; 1 Kgs. 6:13; D&C 97:16 (15–17); 104:59.dwell therein.

For there is not a place found on earth that he may come to and TG Priesthood, Melchizedek; Restoration of the Gospel.restore again that which was lost unto you, or which he hath taken away, even the fulness of the priesthood.

For a D&C 76:51; 128:13.baptismal font there is not upon the earth, that they, my saints, may be 1 Cor. 15:29; D&C 127:6; 138:33. TG Baptism for the Dead; Salvation for the Dead.baptized for those who are dead—
For this ordinance belongeth to my house, and cannot be acceptable to me, only in the days of your poverty, wherein ye are not able to build a house unto me.

But I command you, all ye my saints, to 2 Chr. 3:1 (1–17); 2 Ne. 5:16 (15–17); D&C 84:5 (5, 31); 97:10.build a house unto me; and I grant unto you a sufficient time to build a house unto me; and during this time your baptisms shall be acceptable unto me.

But behold, at the end of this appointment your baptisms for your dead shall not be acceptable unto me; and if you do not these things at the end of the appointment ye shall be rejected as a church, with your dead, saith the Lord your God.

For verily I say unto you, that IE Oct. 3, 1841.after you have had sufficient time to build a house to me, wherein the ordinance of baptizing for the dead belongeth, and for which the same was instituted from before the foundation of the world, your baptisms for your dead cannot be acceptable unto me;
For therein are the D&C 110:16 (14–16).keys of the holy priesthood ordained, that you may receive honor and glory.
And after this time, your baptisms for the dead, by those who are scattered abroad, are not acceptable unto me, saith the Lord.

For it is ordained that in Zion, and in her stakes, and in Jerusalem, those places which I have appointed for Isa. 4:6 (4–6); D&C 133:13 (4–14).refuge, shall be the places for your baptisms for your dead.
And again, verily I say unto you, how shall your Lev. 8:6; D&C 88:74. TG Wash.washings be acceptable unto me, except ye perform them in a house which you have built to my name?

For, for this cause I commanded Moses that he should build a Ex. 25:9 (1–9); 29:4; 33:7; 35:11; Ezek. 37:27. TG Temple.tabernacle, that they should bear it with them in the wilderness, and to build a house in the land of promise, that those ordinances might be revealed which had been hid from before the world was.

Therefore, verily I say unto you, that your Ex. 25:6; 29:7; Lev. 10:7; 14:28; Num. 18:8; D&C 68:21. TG Anointing.anointings, and your washings, and your TG Baptism for the Dead; Genealogy and Temple Work.baptisms for the dead, and your D&C 88:70 (70, 117). TG Solemn Assembly.solemn assemblies, and your Ex. 12:14 (14–17); Lev. 2:2 (2, 9, 16).memorials for your Deut. 10:8; D&C 13; 84:31; 128:24; JS—H 1:69.sacrifices by the sons of Levi, and for your D&C 90:4.oracles in your most Ex. 26:33; 1 Kgs. 6:16; Ezek. 41:4.holy places wherein you receive conversations, and your statutes and judgments, for the beginning of the revelations and foundation of Zion, and for the glory, honor, and endowment of all her municipals, are ordained by the ordinance of my holy house, which my people are always commanded to build unto my holy name.
And verily I say unto you, let this TG Ordinance; Temple.house be built unto my name, that I may reveal mine ordinances therein unto my people;

For I deign to A of F 1:9.reveal unto my church things which have been kept Alma 40:3; D&C 121:26 (26–31).hid from before the foundation of the world, things that pertain to the dispensation of the Eph. 1:10 (9–10); D&C 27:13; 76:106; 112:30.fulness of times.
And D&C 95:14 (14–17).I will show unto my servant Joseph all things pertaining to this house, and the priesthood thereof, and the place whereon it shall be built.
And ye shall build it on the place where you have contemplated building it, for that is the spot which I have chosen for you to build it.
If ye labor with all your might, I will consecrate that spot that it shall be made TG Holiness.holy.
