Call your TG Solemn Assembly.solemn assemblies, and Mal. 3:16 (16–18).speak often one to another. And let every man call upon the name of the Lord.

Yea, verily I say unto you again, the time has come when the voice of the Lord is unto you: Go ye out of Babylon; D&C 29:8. TG Israel, Gathering of; Mission of Latter-day Saints.gather ye out from among the nations, from the Zech. 2:6 (6–7).four winds, from one end of heaven to the other.

Send forth the elders of my church unto the TG Missionary Work; Nations.nations which are afar off; unto the Isa. 11:11; 51:5; 1 Ne. 22:4; 2 Ne. 10:8 (8, 20); 29:7.islands of the sea; send forth unto foreign lands; call upon all nations, first upon the Matt. 19:30; Acts 13:46; D&C 18:26 (26–27); 90:9 (8–9).Gentiles, and then upon the Jews.

And behold, and lo, this shall be their cry, and the voice of the Lord unto all people: Go ye forth unto the land of Zion, that the borders of my people may be enlarged, and that her Isa. 54:2. TG Stake.stakes may be strengthened, and that D&C 58:25 (25, 56); 63:24; 101:68 (68–71). TG Zion.Zion may go forth unto the regions round about.

Yea, let the cry go forth among all people: Awake and arise and go forth to meet the Matt. 25:6; D&C 33:17 (17–18); 45:56 (54–59).Bridegroom; behold and lo, the Bridegroom cometh; go ye out to meet him. Prepare yourselves for the D&C 1:12. TG Day of the Lord.great day of the Lord.
Prov. 27:18; Mark 13:35 (24–37); 1 Ne. 21:23; 2 Ne. 6:13.Watch, therefore, for ye D&C 49:7.know neither the day nor the hour.
Let them, therefore, who are Isa. 4:2; D&C 38:31 (31, 42).among the Gentiles flee unto TG Jerusalem, New; Zion.Zion.
And let them who be of D&C 45:25 (24–25). TG Israel, Judah, People of.Judah flee unto TG Jerusalem.Jerusalem, unto the Ezek. 38:8; Dan. 11:45; JS—M 1:13.mountains of the Lord’s Ps. 122:1 (1–9).house.
Go ye TG Israel, Gathering of; Separation.out from among the nations, even from D&C 1:16.Babylon, from the midst of TG Wickedness.wickedness, which is spiritual Babylon.

But verily, thus saith the Lord, let not your flight be in Isa. 52:12 (10–12); D&C 58:56. TG Haste.haste, but let all things be prepared before you; and he that goeth, let him Gen. 19:17; Luke 9:62; D&C 67:14.not look back lest sudden destruction shall come upon him.

Hearken and hear, O ye inhabitants of the earth. D&C 1:1 (1–6).Listen, ye elders of my church together, and hear the voice of the Lord; for he calleth upon all men, and he commandeth all men everywhere to TG Repent.repent.

For behold, the Lord God hath D&C 13; 27:5 (5–10); 88:92.sent forth the angel crying through the midst of heaven, saying: Prepare ye the way of the Lord, and make his paths straight, for the hour of his Isa. 40:3 (3–5); Mal. 3:1; Rev. 14:6 (6–8). TG Jesus Christ, Prophecies about.coming is nigh—
When the Rev. 14:1.Lamb shall stand upon D&C 84:2.Mount Zion, and with him a Rev. 7:4 (1–4).hundred and forty-four thousand, having his Father’s name written on their foreheads.
Wherefore, prepare ye for the Matt. 25:6 (1–13); D&C 33:17 (17–18); 88:92.coming of the Bridegroom; go ye, go ye out to meet him.

For behold, he shall Isa. 51:5 (3–6); Zech. 14:4 (4–9); 3 Ne. 20:22; 21:25 (23–25); D&C 45:48 (48–53). TG Jesus Christ, Second Coming.stand upon the mount of Olivet, and upon the mighty ocean, even the great deep, and upon the islands of the sea, and upon the land of Zion.
And he shall Joel 3:16; Amos 1:2.utter his voice out of Isa. 2:3 (2–4).Zion, and he shall speak from Jerusalem, and his D&C 45:49 (48–49).voice shall be heard among all people;

And it shall be a voice as the Ezek. 43:2; Rev. 1:15; 19:6; D&C 110:3.voice of many waters, and as the voice of a great Ps. 77:18; Rev. 14:2.thunder, which shall Judg. 5:5; Isa. 40:4; 64:1; Rev. 16:20 (17–21); D&C 49:23; 88:87; 109:74; Moses 6:34.break down the mountains, and the valleys shall not be found.
He shall command the great deep, and it shall be driven back into the north countries, and the Rev. 6:14 (13–17).islands shall become one land;

And the TG Israel, Land of.land of Jerusalem and the land of TG Zion.Zion shall be turned back into their own place, and the TG Earth, Destiny of.earth shall be like as it was in the days before it was Gen. 10:25. TG Earth, Dividing of; Earth, Renewal of.divided.
And the Lord, even the Savior, shall Isa. 12:6.stand in the midst of his people, and shall TG Jesus Christ, King; Jesus Christ, Second Coming; Millennium.reign over all flesh.

And they who are in the Jer. 16:15 (14–15); D&C 110:11. TG Israel, Bondage of, in Other Lands; Israel, Ten Lost Tribes of.north countries shall come in remembrance before the Lord; and their prophets shall hear his voice, and shall no longer stay themselves; and they shall Ex. 17:6 (6–7).smite the rocks, and the ice shall flow down at their presence.
And an Ex. 14:29; Isa. 11:16 (15–16); 35:8 (8–10); 51:11 (9–11); 62:10 (10–12); 2 Ne. 21:16. TG Israel, Gathering of.highway shall be cast up in the midst of the great deep.
Their enemies shall become a prey unto them,
And in the Isa. 35:7 (6–7).barren deserts there shall come forth pools of TG Living Water.living water; and the parched ground shall no longer be a thirsty land.
And they shall bring forth their rich Isa. 60:9 (8–12). TG Treasure.treasures unto the children of Ephraim, my servants.
And the boundaries of the everlasting Gen. 49:26; Hab. 3:6; D&C 49:25.hills shall tremble at their presence.

And there shall they fall down and be Deut. 33:16 (13–17). TG Exaltation.crowned with glory, even in Zion, by the hands of the servants of the Lord, even the children of Gen. 48:20 (16–20); 49:26 (22–26); Zech. 10:7 (7–12). TG Israel, Joseph, People of; Mission of Latter-day Saints.Ephraim.
And they shall be filled with Isa. 35:10 (8–10); 51:11; Jer. 31:12 (10–14); D&C 66:11. TG Singing.songs of everlasting joy.

Behold, this is the TG Israel, Blessings of.blessing of the Gen. 21:33.everlasting God upon the TG Israel, Twelve Tribes of.tribes of TG Israel, Mission of.Israel, and the richer blessing upon the head of Gen. 41:52 (50–52); 48:14 (14–20); 1 Chr. 5:1 (1–2); Jer. 31:9 (6–9); Ether 13:7 (7–10); D&C 113:4.Ephraim and his fellows.

And they also of the tribe of Hosea 1:7; D&C 109:64. TG Israel, Judah, People of.Judah, after their pain, shall be Gen. 49:11.sanctified in TG Holiness.holiness before the Lord, to dwell in his TG God, Presence of.presence day and night, forever and ever.

And now, verily saith the Lord, that these things might be known among you, O inhabitants of the earth, I have sent forth mine Rev. 14:6 (6–7); D&C 20:6 (6–12); 133:17.angel flying through the midst of heaven, having the everlasting TG Restoration of the Gospel.gospel, who hath appeared unto some and hath committed it unto man, who shall appear unto D&C 77:8; 88:103 (103–4).many that dwell on the earth.

And this Ps. 67:2 (1–2). TG Last Days.gospel shall be TG Mission of Latter-day Saints; Preaching.preached unto Gen. 12:3; D&C 42:58 (58–60); 43:25 (23–27); 58:64 (63–64); 88:84 (84, 87–92); 90:10; Abr. 2:11 (9–11).every nation, and kindred, and tongue, and people.
And the Rev. 14:7. TG Missionary Work.servants of God shall go forth, saying with a loud voice: Fear God and give glory to him, for the hour of his judgment is come;
And Zech. 14:16. TG Worship.worship him that made heaven, and earth, and the sea, and the D&C 133:20 (20, 23).fountains of waters—

Calling upon the name of the Lord day and night, saying: O that thou wouldst Isa. 64:1 (1–8); D&C 88:95.rend the heavens, that thou wouldst come down, that the mountains might flow down at thy presence.

And it shall be answered upon their heads; for the presence of the Lord shall be Ex. 24:17; Job 41:31; Jer. 9:7; Hel. 5:23; D&C 130:7; 137:2. TG Earth, Cleansing of.as the melting fire that burneth, and as the fire which causeth the waters to boil.
O Lord, thou shalt come down to make thy name known to thine adversaries, and all nations shall tremble at thy presence—
When thou doest Deut. 10:21.terrible things, things they look not for;
Yea, when thou comest down, and the mountains flow down at thy presence, thou shalt 1 Thes. 4:17 (15–18).meet him who rejoiceth and worketh righteousness, who remembereth thee in thy ways.

For since the beginning of the world have not men heard nor perceived by the ear, neither hath any eye seen, O God, besides thee, how great things thou hast Isa. 64:4; 1 Cor. 2:9.prepared for him that Lam. 3:25 (25–26); 1 Ne. 21:23; 2 Ne. 6:7, 13; D&C 98:2.waiteth for thee.

And it shall be said: Isa. 63:1 (1–2).Who is this that cometh down from God in heaven with dyed Luke 22:44; Mosiah 3:7; D&C 19:18. TG Jesus Christ, Second Coming.garments; yea, from the regions which are not known, clothed in his glorious apparel, traveling in the greatness of his strength?
And he shall say: 2 Ne. 8:12; D&C 136:22.I am he who spake in Dan. 9:16.righteousness, mighty to save.
And the Lord shall be Gen. 49:11 (11–12); Rev. 19:13 (11–15).red in his apparel, and his garments like him that treadeth in the wine-vat.

And so great shall be the glory of his presence that the Isa. 13:10 (9–13); 24:23; Joel 2:10; Amos 5:18; D&C 45:42; 88:87.sun shall hide his face in shame, and the moon shall withhold its light, and the stars shall be hurled from their places.

And his Joel 2:11; D&C 35:21; 43:18 (17–25).voice shall be heard: I have Isa. 63:3 (3–9); Rev. 19:15 (11–15); D&C 76:107; 88:106.trodden the wine-press alone, and have brought judgment upon all people; and none were with me;

And I have Ps. 110:6 (1–7); 1 Cor. 15:27 (24–28); Heb. 10:13 (12–14).trampled them in my fury, and I did tread upon them in mine anger, and their blood have I Lev. 8:30; Num. 18:17; Isa. 63:3 (2–4).sprinkled upon my garments, and stained all my raiment; for this was the TG Day of the Lord; Vengeance.day of vengeance which was in my heart.

And now the year of my Isa. 63:4.redeemed is come; and they shall mention the loving kindness of their Lord, and all that he has bestowed upon them according to his Isa. 63:7.goodness, and according to his loving kindness, forever and ever.

In all their Isa. 63:9; Lam. 3:33 (1–39).afflictions he was afflicted. And the angel of his presence saved them; and in his TG Charity; Compassion; God, Love of.love, and in his pity, he TG Jesus Christ, Redeemer.redeemed them, and bore them, and carried them all the days of old;

Yea, and Gen. 5:23; D&C 38:4; 45:11 (11–12).Enoch also, and they who were with him; the prophets who were before him; and Gen. 7:23 (1–24); 8:1 (1–22).Noah also, and they who were before him; and Ex. 3:8 (1–22).Moses also, and they who were before him;

And from TG Dispensations.Moses to Elijah, and from Elijah to John, who were with Christ in his Alma 40:20 (18–21). TG Jesus Christ, Resurrection.resurrection, and the holy apostles, with Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob, shall be in the presence of the Lamb.

And the D&C 29:13.graves of the TG Saints.saints shall be D&C 45:45 (45–46); 88:97 (96–97). TG Resurrection.opened; and they shall come forth and stand on the Matt. 25:34 (31–46). TG Exaltation.right hand of the Lamb, when he shall stand upon Isa. 24:23; Heb. 12:22 (22, 24); Rev. 14:1; D&C 76:66; 84:2 (2, 18, 32).Mount Zion, and upon the holy city, the New Jerusalem; and they shall Isa. 12:5 (1–6).sing the Rev. 15:3 (3–4); D&C 84:98 (98–102).song of the TG Jesus Christ, Lamb of God.Lamb, day and night forever and ever.

And for this cause, that men might be made D&C 93:22. TG Man, Potential to Become like Heavenly Father.partakers of the TG Celestial Glory; Glory; Telestial Glory; Terrestrial Glory.glories which were to be revealed, the Lord sent forth the fulness of his TG Gospel; New and Everlasting Covenant; Restoration of the Gospel.gospel, his everlasting covenant, reasoning in plainness and simplicity—

To TG Mission of Latter-day Saints.prepare the D&C 35:13; 124:1.weak for those things which are coming on the earth, and for the Lord’s errand in the day when the weak shall Matt. 11:25; 1 Cor. 1:27; Alma 32:23; 37:7 (6–7).confound the Isa. 44:25; 1 Cor. 1:20.wise, and the little one become a Josh. 1:6 (6–9); Isa. 60:22; D&C 52:17; 66:8.strong TG Kingdom of God, on Earth.nation, and two shall put their tens of thousands to Deut. 32:30 (29–30).flight.
And by the weak things of the earth the Lord shall Micah 4:13 (11–13).thresh the nations by the power of his Spirit.
And for this cause these commandments were given; they were commanded to be kept from the world in the day that they were given, but now are to D&C 104:58.go forth unto D&C 1:2.all flesh—
And this according to the mind and will of the Lord, who ruleth over all flesh.
And unto him that repenteth and 2 Chr. 35:6; D&C 88:74. TG Sanctification.sanctifieth himself before the Lord shall be given eternal life.

And upon them that Deut. 28:15. TG Disobedience.hearken not to the voice of the Lord shall be fulfilled that which was written by the prophet Moses, that they should be Acts 3:23; 1 Ne. 22:20 (20–21); 3 Ne. 20:23; 21:11; D&C 1:14; JS—H 1:40.cut off from among the people.

And also that which was written by the prophet Mal. 4:1.Malachi: For, behold, the D&C 5:19; JS—H 1:37. TG Last Days.day cometh that shall Isa. 24:6; 66:15 (15–16); Joel 2:5; 1 Ne. 22:15; 3 Ne. 25:1; D&C 29:9; 64:24. TG Earth, Cleansing of.burn as an oven, and all the proud, yea, and all that do JS—M 1:4.wickedly, shall be stubble; and the day that cometh shall burn them up, saith the Lord of hosts, that it shall leave them neither root nor branch.
Wherefore, this shall be the answer of the Lord unto them:
In that day when I came unto mine own, no man among you Matt. 23:38 (37–39); John 1:11.received me, and you were driven out.
When I called again there was none of you to answer; yet my 2 Ne. 28:32.arm was not shortened at all that I could not redeem, neither my Isa. 50:2; Ezek. 37:12 (11–14); Hosea 13:14.power to deliver.
Behold, at my rebuke I Ex. 14:21 (1–31); Josh. 3:17 (14–17); 2 Ne. 7:2. TG Drought.dry up the sea. I make the rivers a wilderness; their fish stink, and die for thirst.
I clothe the heavens with blackness, and make sackcloth their covering.
And Isa. 50:11.this shall ye have of my hand—ye shall lie down in sorrow.

Behold, and lo, there are none to deliver you; for ye TG Apostasy of Israel.obeyed not my voice when I called to you out of the heavens; ye Luke 16:31. TG Prophets, Rejection of.believed not my servants, and when they were 2 Chr. 36:15; Jer. 44:4 (4–5).sent unto you ye received them not.
Wherefore, they sealed up the testimony and bound up the law, and ye were delivered over unto Isa. 8:16 (13–16); Matt. 8:12 (11–12); D&C 77:8. TG Darkness, Spiritual.darkness.
These shall go away into outer darkness, where there is Luke 13:28; D&C 19:5; 101:91 (90–91).weeping, and wailing, and gnashing of teeth.
Behold the Lord your God hath spoken it. Amen.

div.p: A declaration of belief regarding governments and laws in general, adopted by unanimous vote at a general assembly of the Church held at Kirtland, Ohio, August 17, 1835. Many Saints gathered together to consider the proposed contents of the first edition of the Doctrine and Covenants. At that time, this declaration was given the following preamble: “That our belief with regard to earthly governments and laws in general may not be misinterpreted nor misunderstood, we have thought proper to present, at the close of this volume, our opinion concerning the same.”

1–4, Governments should preserve freedom of conscience and worship; 5–8, All men should uphold their governments and owe respect and deference to the law; 9–10, Religious societies should not exercise civil powers; 11–12, Men are justified in defending themselves and their property.

chapter.verse: We believe that Jer. 27:6 (4–11); John 19:11; Rom. 13:1 (1–4); D&C 98:5 (4–7); 101:77; A of F 1:12. TG Citizenship; Governments.governments were instituted of God for the benefit of man; and that he holds men TG Accountability; Judgment.accountable for their acts in relation to them, both in making laws and administering them, for the good and safety of society.

We believe that no government can exist in TG Peace.peace, except such laws are framed and held inviolate as will secure to each individual the TG Agency; Liberty.free exercise of TG Conscience.conscience, the right and control of property, and the Josh. 20:3; D&C 42:18 (18–19).protection of life.

We believe that all governments necessarily require Ezra 7:25; 2 Pet. 2:13 (13–17).civil D&C 98:10 (8–10). TG Delegation of Responsibility.officers and magistrates to enforce the laws of the same; and that such as will administer the law in equity and justice should be sought for and upheld by the voice of the people if a republic, or the will of the sovereign.

We believe that religion is instituted of God; and that men are amenable to him, and to him only, for the exercise of it, unless their religious opinions prompt them to infringe upon the rights and liberties of others; but we do not believe that human law has a right to interfere in prescribing rules of Matt. 22:21; Alma 21:22 (21–22); D&C 63:26; A of F 1:11. TG Worship.worship to bind the consciences of men, nor dictate forms for public or private devotion; that the civil magistrate should restrain crime, but never control conscience; should punish TG Guilt; Punish.guilt, but never suppress the freedom of the soul.

We believe that all men are bound to TG Loyalty; Obedience; Order.sustain and uphold the respective TG Citizenship; Governments.governments in which they reside, while protected in their inherent and inalienable rights by the laws of such governments; and that sedition and TG Rebellion.rebellion are unbecoming every citizen thus protected, and should be punished accordingly; and that all governments have a right to enact such laws as in their own judgments are best calculated to secure the public interest; at the same time, however, holding sacred the freedom of conscience.

We believe that every man should be Rom. 13:7.honored in his station, rulers and magistrates as such, being placed for the protection of the innocent and the punishment of the guilty; and that to the D&C 58:21; 88:34.laws all men owe TG Respect.respect and deference, as without them peace and harmony would be supplanted by anarchy and terror; human laws being instituted for the express purpose of regulating our interests as individuals and nations, between man and man; and divine laws given of heaven, prescribing rules on spiritual concerns, for faith and worship, both to be answered by man to his Maker.

We believe that rulers, states, and governments have a right, and are bound to enact laws for the protection of all TG Citizenship.citizens in the free exercise of their religious Micah 4:5; A of F 1:11.belief; but we do not believe that they have a right in justice to deprive citizens of this privilege, or proscribe them in their opinions, so long as a regard and reverence are shown to the laws and such religious opinions do not justify sedition nor conspiracy.

We believe that the commission of crime should be Alma 1:14 (11–18); D&C 42:84 (84–87).punished according to the nature of the offense; that murder, treason, robbery, theft, and the breach of the general peace, in all respects, should be punished according to their criminality and their tendency to evil among men, by the laws of that government in which the offense is committed; and for the public TG Peace.peace and tranquility all men should step forward and use their ability in bringing Ex. 18:22 (17–26). TG Offense.offenders against good laws to punishment.

We do not believe it just to Alma 30:7 (7–11). TG Governments.mingle religious influence with civil government, whereby one religious society is fostered and another proscribed in its spiritual privileges, and the individual rights of its members, as citizens, denied.

We believe that all religious societies have a right to deal with their members for disorderly conduct, D&C 42:81.according to the rules and regulations of such societies; provided that such dealings be for fellowship and good standing; but we do not believe that any religious society has TG Authority; Excommunication.authority to try men on the right of property or life, to take from them this world’s goods, or to put them in jeopardy of either life or limb, or to inflict any physical punishment upon them. They can only excommunicate them from their society, and withdraw from them their fellowship.

We believe that men should appeal to the civil law for redress of all TG Injustice; Malice; Oppression; Persecution.wrongs and grievances, where personal abuse is inflicted or the right of property or character infringed, where such laws exist as will protect the same; but we believe that all men are justified in TG Deliver.defending themselves, their friends, and property, and the government, from the unlawful assaults and encroachments of all persons in times of exigency, where immediate appeal cannot be made to the laws, and relief afforded.

We believe it just to TG Mission of Latter-day Saints; Warn.preach the gospel to the nations of the earth, and warn the righteous to save themselves from the corruption of the world; but we do not believe it right to interfere with Philem. 1:10 (10–19). TG Bondage, Physical.bond-servants, neither preach the gospel to, nor baptize them contrary to the will and wish of their masters, nor to meddle with or influence them in the least to cause them to be dissatisfied with their situations in this life, thereby jeopardizing the lives of men; such interference we believe to be unlawful and unjust, and dangerous to the peace of every government allowing human beings to be held in TG Slavery.servitude.

div.p: Announcement of the martyrdom of Joseph Smith the Prophet and his brother, Hyrum Smith the Patriarch, at Carthage, Illinois, June 27, 1844. This document was included at the end of the 1844 edition of the Doctrine and Covenants, which was nearly ready for publication when Joseph and Hyrum Smith were murdered.
1–2, Joseph and Hyrum martyred in Carthage Jail; 3, The preeminent position of the Prophet is acclaimed; 4–7, Their innocent blood testifies of the truth and divinity of the work.

chapter.verse: To seal the testimony of this book and the Book of Mormon, we announce the D&C 5:22; 6:30. TG Martyrdom.martyrdom of Joseph Smith the Prophet, and Hyrum Smith the Patriarch. They were shot in Carthage jail, on the 27th of June, 1844, about five o’clock p.m., by an armed mob—painted black—of from 150 to 200 persons. JS—H 1:4.Hyrum was shot first and fell calmly, exclaiming: I am a TG Death; Murder. dead man! Joseph leaped from the window, and was shot dead in the attempt, exclaiming: Ps. 38:21 (21–22); 71:12. O Lord my God! They were both shot after they were dead, in a brutal manner, and both received four balls.

John Taylor and Willard Richards, two of the Twelve, were the only persons in the room at the time; the former was wounded in a savage manner with four balls, but has since recovered; the latter, through the providence of God, escaped, without even a hole in his robe.

Joseph Smith, the TG Prophets, Mission of.Prophet and TG Seer.Seer of the Lord, has done more, TG Jesus Christ, Savior; Salvation.save Jesus only, for the salvation of men in this world, than any other man that ever lived in it. In the short space of twenty years, he has brought forth the Book of Mormon, which he translated by the gift and power of God, and has been the means of publishing it on two continents; has sent the D&C 35:17; 42:12. TG Restoration of the Gospel.fulness of the everlasting gospel, which it contained, to the four quarters of the earth; has brought forth the revelations and commandments which compose this book of Doctrine and Covenants, and many other wise documents and instructions for the benefit of the children of men; gathered many thousands of the Latter-day Saints, founded a great city, and left a fame and name that cannot be slain. He lived great, and he died great in the eyes of God and his people; and like most of the Lord’s anointed in ancient times, has sealed his mission and his works with his own Mosiah 17:20; D&C 136:39. TG Blood, Symbolism of.blood; and so has his brother Hyrum. In life they were not divided, and in death they were not 2 Sam. 1:23.separated!

When Joseph went to Carthage to deliver himself up to the pretended requirements of the law, two or three days previous to his assassination, he said: “I am going like a Isa. 53:7; Jer. 11:19; Mosiah 14:7.lamb to the slaughter; but I am calm as a summer’s morning; I have a TG Conscience.conscience TG Purity.void of offense towards God, and towards all men. I shall die innocent, and it shall yet be said of me—he was murdered in cold blood.”—The same morning, after Hyrum had made ready to go—shall it be said to the slaughter? yes, for so it was—he read the following paragraph, near the close of the twelfth chapter of Ether, in the Book of Mormon, and turned down the leaf upon it:

And it came to pass that I prayed unto the Lord that he would give unto the Gentiles grace, that they might have charity. And it came to pass that the Lord said unto me: If they have not charity it mattereth not unto thee, thou hast been TG Loyalty; Steadfastness. faithful ; wherefore thy garments shall be made D&C 88:74 (74–75). clean . And because thou hast seen thy weakness, thou shalt be made strong, even unto the sitting down in the place which I have prepared in the mansions of my Father. And now I … bid farewell unto the Gentiles; yea, and also unto my brethren whom I love, until we shall meet before the Ether 12:36–38. judgment-seat of Christ, where all men shall know that my garments are not spotted with your blood. The 1 Tim. 2:6; Heb. 9:16 (16–17).testators are now dead, and their Heb. 9:17. TG Testimony.testament is in force.

Hyrum Smith was forty-four years old in February, 1844, and Joseph Smith was thirty-eight in December, 1843; and henceforward their names will be classed among the TG Martyrdom.martyrs of religion; and the reader in every nation will be reminded that the Book of Mormon, and this book of Doctrine and Covenants of the church, cost the best blood of the nineteenth century to bring them forth for the salvation of a ruined world; and that if the fire can scathe a Luke 23:31.green tree for the glory of God, how easy it will burn up the dry trees to purify the vineyard of corruption. They lived for glory; they died for glory; and glory is their eternal TG Reward.reward. From age to age shall their names go down to posterity as gems for the sanctified.

They were innocent of any crime, as they had often been proved before, and were only confined in jail by the conspiracy of traitors and wicked men; and their innocent blood on the floor of Carthage jail is a broad seal affixed to “Mormonism” that cannot be rejected by any court on earth, and their innocent blood on the IE coat of arms.escutcheon of the State of Illinois, with the broken faith of the State as pledged by the governor, is a witness to the truth of the everlasting gospel that all the world cannot impeach; and their innocent blood on the banner of liberty, and on the magna charta of the United States, is an ambassador for the religion of Jesus Christ, that will touch the hearts of honest men among all nations; and their innocent blood, with the innocent blood of all the martyrs under the Rev. 6:9.altar that John saw, will cry unto the Lord of Hosts till he avenges that blood on the earth. Amen.

div.p: The word and will of the Lord, given through President Brigham Young at Winter Quarters, the camp of Israel, Omaha Nation, on the west bank of the Missouri River, near Council Bluffs, Iowa, January 14, 1847.

1–16, How the camp of Israel is to be organized for the westward journey is explained; 17–27, The Saints are commanded to live by numerous gospel standards; 28–33, The Saints should sing, dance, pray, and learn wisdom; 34–42, Prophets are slain so that they might be honored and the wicked condemned.

chapter.verse: The Word and TG God, Will of.Will of the Lord concerning the Camp of D&C 133:32 (32–35); A of F 1:10.Israel in their journeyings to the West:

Let all the people of D&C 20:1 (1–4); 115:4.The Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints, and those who journey with them, be organized into companies, with a covenant and promise to D&C 41:5 (5–6); 56:2 (2–3).keep all the commandments and statutes of the Lord our God.

Let the companies be organized with captains of Ex. 18:21 (21–27).hundreds, captains of fifties, and captains of tens, with a president and his two counselors at their head, under the direction of the Twelve TG Apostles.Apostles.
And this shall be our TG Covenants.covenant—that we will TG Walking with God.walk in all the TG Ordinance.ordinances of the Lord.
Let each company provide themselves with all the teams, wagons, provisions, clothing, and other necessaries for the journey, that they can.
When the companies are organized let them go to with their Judg. 6:14.might, to prepare for those who are to tarry.

Let each company, with their captains and presidents, decide how many can go next spring; then choose out a sufficient number of able-bodied and expert men, to take teams, seeds, and farming utensils, to go as pioneers to prepare for putting in spring crops.

Let each company D&C 38:24 (24–27).bear an equal proportion, according to the dividend of their property, in taking the poor, the TG Poor; Widows.widows, the Ps. 68:5; 146:9 (1–10); James 1:27 (25–27).fatherless, and the families of those who have gone into the army, that the cries of the widow and the 3 Ne. 24:5.fatherless come not up into the ears of the Lord against this people.
Let each company prepare houses, and fields for raising TG Bread; Self-Sacrifice.grain, for those who are to remain behind this season; and this is the will of the Lord concerning his people.
Let every man use all his influence and property to remove this people to the place where the Lord shall locate a TG Stake; Zion.stake of Zion.

And if ye do this with a pure heart, in all faithfulness, ye shall be blessed; you shall be Gen. 26:12; Deut. 28:4 (1–14); Alma 34:20 (17–27). TG Blessing.blessed in your flocks, and in your herds, and in your fields, and in your houses, and in your families.
Let my servants Ezra T. Benson and Erastus Snow organize a company.
And let my servants Orson Pratt and Wilford Woodruff organize a company.
Also, let my servants Amasa Lyman and George A. Smith organize a company.
And appoint presidents, and Deut. 1:15.captains of hundreds, and of fifties, and of tens.
And let my servants that have been appointed go and TG Teaching.teach this, my will, to the saints, that they may be ready to go to a land of peace.
Go thy way and do as I have told you, and TG Courage; Enemies; Fearful.fear not thine enemies; for they shall not have power to stop my work.
Zion shall be D&C 100:13.redeemed in mine own due time.
And if any man shall seek to build up himself, and seeketh not my TG Counsel; Problem-Solving.counsel, he shall have no power, and his TG Foolishness.folly shall be made manifest.
IE Seek the Lord’s counsel; see v. 19.Seek ye; and keep all your TG Honesty; Promise; Vow.pledges one with another; and TG Covet.covet not that which is thy brother’s.

TG Profanity; Self-Mastery.Keep yourselves from evil to take the name of the Lord in vain, for I am the Lord your God, even the Ex. 3:6; Matt. 22:32; 1 Ne. 19:10.God of your fathers, the God of Abraham and of Isaac and of Jacob.

I am he who Gen. 15:14 (13–14); Ex. 13:18 (18, 20); Amos 3:1 (1–2); 1 Ne. 5:15; D&C 103:18.led the children of Israel out of the land of Egypt; and my arm is stretched out in the last days, to Isa. 9:12; Jer. 30:10; 32:17; Ezek. 20:34 (33–34); Hosea 13:9; D&C 38:33.save my people Israel.
Cease to Prov. 17:14; 2 Tim. 2:24; 3 Ne. 11:29 (29–30). TG Contention; Disputations; Strife.contend one with another; cease to speak D&C 20:54. TG Backbiting.evil one of another.
Cease TG Drunkenness; Word of Wisdom.drunkenness; and let your words tend to 1 Pet. 1:15. TG Edification.edifying one another.

If thou TG Borrow; Debt; Honesty.borrowest of thy TG Neighbor.neighbor, thou shalt Ps. 37:21; Mosiah 4:28.restore that which thou hast borrowed; and if thou canst not repay then go straightway and tell thy neighbor, lest he condemn thee.
If thou shalt find that which thy neighbor has Lev. 6:4; Deut. 22:3.lost, thou shalt make diligent search till thou shalt TG Honesty.deliver it to him again.
Thou shalt be TG Diligence.diligent in Prov. 21:20.preserving what thou hast, that thou mayest be a wise TG Stewardship.steward; for it is the free gift of the Lord thy God, and thou art his steward.

If thou art TG Happiness.merry, Ps. 33:1; 147:7; Isa. 12:4; Eph. 5:19 (19–20); 1 Ne. 18:16; Mosiah 2:20 (20–21); Alma 26:8; D&C 25:11 (11–12); 109:79. TG Communication; Singing.praise the Lord with singing, with music, with 2 Sam. 6:14.dancing, and with a TG Prayer.prayer of praise and 2 Chr. 5:13; D&C 97:13. TG Thanksgiving.thanksgiving.
If thou art 2 Sam. 22:7. TG Sorrow.sorrowful, call on the Lord thy God with supplication, that your souls may be TG Joy.joyful.

div.p: A vision given to Joseph Smith the Prophet, in the temple at Kirtland, Ohio, January 21, 1836. The occasion was the administration of ordinances in preparation for the dedication of the temple.
1–6, The Prophet sees his brother Alvin in the celestial kingdom; 7–9, The doctrine of salvation for the dead is revealed; 10, All children are saved in the celestial kingdom.

div.p: A vision given to President Joseph F. Smith in Salt Lake City, Utah, on October 3, 1918. In his opening address at the 89th Semiannual General Conference of the Church, on October 4, 1918, President Smith declared that he had received several divine communications during the previous months. One of these, concerning the Savior’s visit to the spirits of the dead while His body was in the tomb, President Smith had received the previous day. It was written immediately following the close of the conference. On October 31, 1918, it was submitted to the counselors in the First Presidency, the Council of the Twelve, and the Patriarch, and it was unanimously accepted by them.

1–10, President Joseph F. Smith ponders upon the writings of Peter and our Lord’s visit to the spirit world; 11–24, President Smith sees the righteous dead assembled in paradise and Christ’s ministry among them; 25–37, He sees how the preaching of the gospel was organized among the spirits; 38–52, He sees Adam, Eve, and many of the holy prophets in the spirit world who considered their spirit state before their resurrection as a bondage; 53–60, The righteous dead of this day continue their labors in the world of spirits.

div.p: The Bible and the Book of Mormon teach that monogamy is God’s standard for marriage unless He declares otherwise (see 2 Samuel 12:7–8 and Jacob 2:27, 30). Following a revelation to Joseph Smith, the practice of plural marriage was instituted among Church members in the early 1840s (see section 132). From the 1860s to the 1880s, the United States government passed laws to make this religious practice illegal. These laws were eventually upheld by the U.S. Supreme Court. After receiving revelation, President Wilford Woodruff issued the following Manifesto, which was accepted by the Church as authoritative and binding on October 6, 1890. This led to the end of the practice of plural marriage in the Church.

The Book of Mormon teaches that “all are alike unto God,” including “black and white, bond and free, male and female” (2 Nephi 26:33). Throughout the history of the Church, people of every race and ethnicity in many countries have been baptized and have lived as faithful members of the Church. During Joseph Smith’s lifetime, a few black male members of the Church were ordained to the priesthood. Early in its history, Church leaders stopped conferring the priesthood on black males of African descent. Church records offer no clear insights into the origins of this practice. Church leaders believed that a revelation from God was needed to alter this practice and prayerfully sought guidance. The revelation came to Church President Spencer W. Kimball and was affirmed to other Church leaders in the Salt Lake Temple on June 1, 1978. The revelation removed all restrictions with regard to race that once applied to the priesthood.
